AccessibilityBackend.description:
Accessibility capture backend. Each variant corresponds to a platform
accessibility API implementation.

oneOf.description: Automatically detect the best available backend.
Chrome DevTools Protocol (browsers, Electron, Tauri on Windows).

AccessibilityBackend.title: AccessibilityBackend

AccessibilityBounds.description:
Bounding rectangle for an accessibility node. Coordinates are screen
pixels, typically absolute screen coordinates.
AccessibilityBounds.title: AccessibilityBounds

AccessibilityBounds.description:
Bounding rectangle for an accessibility node. Coordinates are screen
pixels, typically absolute screen coordinates.

AccessibilityRole.description:
ARIA/UIA accessibility roles. Based on WAI-ARIA 1.2 with extensions for
Windows UI Automation and other platform-specific accessibility APIs.

AccessibilityState.description:
Accessibility state flags for a node.

These flags represent the current interactive state of an element.
Tri-state booleans use `Option<bool>` — `None` means "not applicable."

isHidden.description: Element is hidden from the accessibility tree.

AccessibilityNode.description:
A node in the accessibility tree.

Each node represents an element in the accessibility hierarchy with its
role, name, value, state, and bounds. The `ref` field provides a stable
identifier for AI-driven automation (e.g., `@e1`, `@e2`).

className.description: CSS class name or control class.

isInteractive.description: Whether the element accepts user interaction.

ref.description: Reference ID like `@e1`, `@e2` for AI interaction.

AccessibilityNode.title: AccessibilityNode

AccessibilityRole.description:
ARIA/UIA accessibility roles. Based on WAI-ARIA 1.2 with extensions for
Windows UI Automation and other platform-specific accessibility APIs.
AccessibilityRole.title: AccessibilityRole

AccessibilityRoleCriterion.description:
Role selector criterion — either a single role name or a list of roles.
Matches the Python `str | list[str] | None` type on
`AccessibilityTarget.role`.
AccessibilityRoleCriterion.title: AccessibilityRoleCriterion

AccessibilityRole.description:
ARIA/UIA accessibility roles. Based on WAI-ARIA 1.2 with extensions for
Windows UI Automation and other platform-specific accessibility APIs.

AccessibilityState.description:
Accessibility state flags for a node.

These flags represent the current interactive state of an element.
Tri-state booleans use `Option<bool>` — `None` means "not applicable."

isHidden.description: Element is hidden from the accessibility tree.

RoleCriterion.description:
Role criterion for [`AccessibilitySelector`] — either a single role or a
list of roles (any match).

AccessibilitySelector.description:
Selector for finding nodes in an accessibility tree.

Provides flexible matching criteria for locating elements by role, name,
automation ID, or other attributes. Multiple criteria are combined with
AND logic.

className.description: Match by CSS / control class name.

AccessibilitySelector.title: AccessibilitySelector

ActionExecutionCreate.description:
Request payload for reporting a single action execution.

Usually submitted as part of a batch; see [`ActionExecutionResponse`].

activeStates.description: IDs of states active when the action ran.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the action completed.

confidenceScore.description: Confidence score of the match in the range `[0.0, 1.0]`.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the action failed.

fromState.description: Source state ID for state-machine actions.

inputData.description: Opaque input data captured for the action.

llmMetrics.description: LLM token and cost metrics, if the action used an LLM.

outputData.description: Opaque output data produced by the action.

parentActionId.description: ID of a parent action, if this is a sub-action.

parentId.description: Parent action ID for child actions within a sequence.

patternId.description: ID of the pattern the action targeted.

screenshotId.description: ID of a screenshot associated with the action.

sequenceNumber.description: Zero-based sequence number of the action within the run.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the action started.

toState.description: Destination state ID for state-machine actions.

ActionStatus.description: Outcome of a single action within a run.

oneOf.description: The action failed (e.g., assertion failure, element not found).
The action exceeded its configured time limit.
The action was skipped (e.g., by a conditional).
The action errored at a system level (e.g., crash, network error).
The action has not been executed yet.

ActionType.description:
Type of action executed.

Covers vision, input, state-machine, control-flow, utility, AI, and custom
action kinds. Variants are serialized as snake_case strings.

oneOf.description: Locate a single pattern in the current scene.
Locate all instances of a pattern in the current scene.
Wait for a pattern to appear.
Wait for a pattern to disappear.
Drag from one point to another.
Verify the current state matches expectations.
Run a sequence of AI prompts.

ActionType.title: ExecutionActionType

ErrorType.description: Category of error that caused an action to fail.

oneOf.description: The target element could not be located.
The action exceeded its configured time limit.
An assertion on the result failed.

LLMMetrics.description: Token usage and cost metrics for an LLM-powered action.

MatchLocation.description:
Pixel coordinates and optional size of a matched pattern.

Inline object on [`ActionExecutionCreate::match_location`]. Lifted to a
named struct so it round-trips through JSON Schema.

height.description: Match height in pixels.

width.description: Match width in pixels.

x.description: X coordinate in pixels.

y.description: Y coordinate in pixels.

MatchLocation.title: ExecutionMatchLocation

actions.description: Actions to record (Python enforces `1..=100`; enforced by the backend).

actionIds.description: Assigned action IDs, in the same order as the submitted batch.

ActionReliabilityStats.description: Reliability statistics aggregated for a single action type.

commonErrors.description:
Common error categories, as opaque records. Python types this as
`list[dict[str, Any]]`; typing it further requires product input.

ActionReliabilityStats.title: ActionReliabilityStats

ElementRect.description:
Bounding rectangle of a DOM element, mirroring the output of
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`.

Contains both the origin+size pair (`x`, `y`, `width`, `height`) and the
edge offsets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) for consumer convenience.

bottom.description: Distance from the top of the viewport plus `height`.

height.description: Height of the element in CSS pixels.

left.description: Distance from the left of the viewport.

right.description: Distance from the left of the viewport plus `width`.

top.description: Distance from the top of the viewport.

width.description: Width of the element in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the element's origin (same as `left`).

y.description: Y coordinate of the element's origin (same as `top`).

ElementState.description:
Observable state of a UI Bridge element as returned from the React
registry.

Every element returned by the bridge includes a snapshot of its current
visibility, interactivity, and form-control value.

checked.description: Current checked state for checkbox/radio elements.

enabled.description: Whether the element is enabled (not disabled).

focused.description: Whether the element currently has keyboard focus.

visible.description: Whether the element is currently visible in the viewport.

ActionResponse.description: Response from executing an action on an element or component.

durationMs.description: Time taken to execute the action in milliseconds.

elementState.description: Updated element state after the action (if applicable).

error.description: Error message if the action failed.

stack.description: Stack trace if the action threw an exception.

timestamp.description: Unix-epoch millisecond timestamp when the action completed.

ActionResponse.title: ActionResponse

ActionStatus.description: Outcome of a single action within a run.

oneOf.description: The action failed (e.g., assertion failure, element not found).
The action exceeded its configured time limit.
The action was skipped (e.g., by a conditional).
The action errored at a system level (e.g., crash, network error).
The action has not been executed yet.

ActionStatus.title: ActionStatus

ActionType.description:
Types of actions in the automation system.

Corresponds to the action types defined in the qontinui-schemas config
models. Variants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE on the wire to match the Python
enum values.
ActionType.title: ActionType

ActiveStatesResult.description: Result of querying currently active states in the state machine.

current_state.description: The "primary" current state, when one can be singled out.

error.description: Error message when the query failed.

state_history.description: Ordered history of states the machine has passed through.

ActiveStatesResult.title: ActiveStatesResult

AgenticStatus.description: Status of the agentic phase execution.

FileChange.description: A file change made during the agentic phase.

path.description: Path of the changed file.

FindingOutput.description: A finding reported by the AI.

needsInput.description: Whether this finding requires human input.

resolved.description: Whether this finding has been resolved.

ReflectionFixOutput.description: A reflection fix reported by the AI.

errorId.description: Identifier for the error being fixed.

AgenticPhaseOutput.description: Canonical structured output from the agentic phase.

confidence.description: AI's confidence that the fixes will pass verification (0.0-1.0).

filesModified.description: Files modified during this agentic phase.

status.description: Overall status of the agentic phase.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was done.

unfixable.description: Whether the AI determined errors are unfixable.

unfixableReason.description: Reason why errors are unfixable (if unfixable is true).

AgenticPhaseOutput.title: AgenticPhaseOutput

AgenticStatus.description: Status of the agentic phase execution.
AgenticStatus.title: AgenticStatus

ExecutionStep.description:
A single step in an AI Builder execution sequence.

This is the **legacy step shape** persisted in `ai_workflows.json`. It
carries fields for every step flavour (`workflow`, `state`, `playwright`,
`prompt`, `action`, `screenshot`) as optional branches. Steps that don't
apply to the given `step_type` have their branch fields set to `None`.

actionType.description:
For action steps: the action type (`"click"`, `"double_click"`,
`"right_click"`).

playwrightScriptContent.description: For playwright steps: the script content.

playwrightScriptId.description: For playwright steps: the script ID.

playwrightTargetUrl.description: For playwright steps: the target URL.

promptContent.description: For prompt steps: the actual prompt content.

promptId.description: For prompt steps: the prompt ID from the library.

screenshotDelay.description: Delay in seconds before taking screenshot (default 0).

screenshotMonitor.description:
Monitor for screenshot capture (number for specific monitor, `"all"`
for all monitors).

takeScreenshot.description: Whether to capture a screenshot after this step.

targetImageId.description: For action steps: the target image ID.

targetImageName.description: For action steps: the target image name (for display).

AiWorkflow.description:
A saved AI Builder workflow template.

Users create these in the Automation Builder tab. Each workflow carries an
ordered list of `ExecutionStep`s plus metadata (goal, category, tags,
context references). The runner persists them in `ai_workflows.json` and
surfaces them through Tauri commands and the HTTP API.

autoIncludeContexts.description: Whether to auto-include contexts based on task mentions (default: true).

captureInputValidation.description: Whether to capture input for coordinate validation.

description.description: Optional description of what this workflow does.

maxIterations.description:
Maximum iterations for the AI loop.
`None` (omitted) means no cap — loop until success or explicit stop.

steps.description: The ordered list of execution steps.

AiWorkflow.title: AiWorkflow

ApiAssertionOperator.description: Comparison operators supported by API assertions.

ApiAssertionType.description: Assertion kinds supported on API responses.

ApiAssertion.description: A single assertion evaluated against an API response.

expected.description:
Expected value. The TS source allows either a string or number, so
this field stays as `serde_json::Value` on the wire.

operator.description: Comparison operator; defaults to `equals` on the consumer side.

ApiAssertion.title: ApiAssertion

ApiAssertionOperator.description: Comparison operators supported by API assertions.
ApiAssertionOperator.title: ApiAssertionOperator

ApiAssertionType.description: Assertion kinds supported on API responses.
ApiAssertionType.title: ApiAssertionType

ApiContentType.description: Content-Type values for an API request body.
ApiContentType.title: ApiContentType

ApiVariableExtraction.description: Extract a named variable from an API response via JSONPath.

defaultValue.description: Default value if the path does not resolve.

jsonPath.description: JSONPath expression used to extract the value.

variableName.description: Variable name to bind the extracted value to.

ApiVariableExtraction.title: ApiVariableExtraction

AppError.description:
Failure modes for app-registry operations. Mirrors the
`responses::SpecError` tagged-enum pattern in
`qontinui-runner/src-tauri/src/spec_api/responses.rs`.
AppError.title: AppError

CanvasPanel.description:
A canvas panel rendered in the dashboard widget.

Wire-format mirror of the runner's `StoredPanel` struct
(`qontinui-runner/src-tauri/src/mcp/canvas.rs`). The runner emits this
inside [`AppEvent::CanvasUpdate`] via the `canvas-update` Tauri channel.
Field names are snake_case to match the existing TS interface in
`qontinui-schemas/ts/src/canvas/index.ts`.

`data` stays `serde_json::Value` because each `component` type has a
different inner shape (Markdown, CodeDiff, Table, …); the per-component
data schemas live in the TS module above and are intentionally not
modeled as a Rust discriminated union (would balloon the schema for
little gain on the Rust side).

FlowEvent.description:
Events emitted during flow execution for UI updates.

Internally tagged with `"type"` and snake_case variant names.

oneOf.description: A step is about to execute.

Flow execution was paused via `pause_flow_execution`.

Emitted on the `flow-event` Tauri channel by
`qontinui-runner/src-tauri/src/commands/flow.rs::pause_flow_execution`
and consumed by `useFlowExecutionData.ts`. `step_id` is intentionally
emitted as `null` when no current step (rather than skipped) to
preserve the legacy `serde_json::json!` wire shape.

step_id.description: Step the flow was paused at (`null` if no current step).

oneOf.description:
Flow execution was resumed via `resume_flow_execution` after a pause.

Emitted on the `flow-event` Tauri channel by
`qontinui-runner/src-tauri/src/commands/flow.rs::resume_flow_execution`.
`step_id` is intentionally emitted as `null` when no current step.

step_id.description: Step execution will resume from (`null` if no current step).

AppEvent.description:
Unified application events for frontend communication.

Adjacently tagged: each variant serializes as
`{"event_type": "<VariantName>", "data": {..}}`.
The React frontend dispatches on `event_type` and reads `data`.

oneOf.description: Standard executor event with data payload.
Web extraction event with data payload.
Orchestrator state change event for real-time UI updates.

phase.description: Current phase (setup, verification, agentic, completion).

oneOf.description: Step progress event for tracking individual steps.
Task run update event for status changes.
Approval required -- workflow is paused waiting for human review.
Approval resolved -- human responded to approval request.
A deferred question was created during autonomous execution.

confidence.description: Confidence score (0.0-1.0).

iteration.description: Iteration when the question was raised.

oneOf.description: A deferred question was reviewed by a human.

Canvas panel created, updated, or removed.

Wraps a [`CanvasPanel`] when `action` is `"create"` / `"update"`;
`panel` is `None` for `"delete"` and `"clear"`. `panel.data` remains
a free-form `serde_json::Value` because the inner shape varies per
component type (Markdown, CodeDiff, Table, …) — the canonical map
is in `qontinui-schemas/ts/src/canvas/index.ts`.
Real-time AI output chunk for live streaming to the frontend.

accumulated_length.description: Total accumulated output length so far.

chunk.description: The text chunk received from the AI.

task_run_id.description: Task run ID this output belongs to.

failed_step_count.description: Number of verification steps that failed.

is_stalled.description: True if failed_step_count has not decreased in 3 consecutive iterations.

new_failures.description: Failures not present in the previous iteration.

passed_step_count.description: Number of verification steps that passed.

repeated_failures.description: Failures that were also present in the previous iteration.

skipped_step_count.description: Number of verification steps that were skipped.

oneOf.description: Blame attribution results from the blame engine.

attributed_failures.description: Number of failures with blame attributions.

revert_patterns.description: Number of files exhibiting revert patterns.

oneOf.description: Constraint evaluation results after an agentic phase.
Workflow has been added to the execution queue.

task_run_id.description: Task run ID for the queued workflow.

oneOf.description: Workflow has been dequeued and is starting execution.

task_run_id.description: Task run ID for the dequeued workflow.

wait_time_ms.description: Time spent waiting in the queue, in milliseconds.

oneOf.description: Real-time cost update after each AI call.
Cost anomaly detected via statistical analysis.

AppEvent.title: AppEvent

CandidateMiss.description:
A near-match candidate — an element that scored above the cut-off but
didn't fully match the assertion criteria.

fieldDiffs.description: Per-field diffs explaining why the candidate didn't fully match.

path.description: CSS selector or DOM path identifying the candidate.

FieldDiff.description:
Per-field diff between an assertion's expected criteria and a
candidate element's actual value.

actual.description: Actual value from the bridge element.

expected.description: Expected value from the IR assertion.

MissReason.description:
Coarse-grained classification of an assertion miss.

Per §5.3 — locked as a unit enum. Structured detail (per-field diffs,
near-match candidates) lives on `AssertionMiss` / `CandidateMiss`.

oneOf.description: No bridge element scored above the match threshold.
Best candidate's ARIA role differed from the expected role.
Best candidate's text differed from the expected text.
Best candidate was found but not visible.
Best candidate had a differing attribute (data-*, aria-*, etc.).

Multiple elements matched equally well; assertion expected a
unique target.

AssertionMiss.description: Top-level miss diagnostic for a failed assertion.

candidates.description:
Top-N near-match candidates, capped externally. Ordered by score
descending.

reason.description: Coarse-grained classification of why the match failed.

AssertionMiss.title: AssertionMiss

AssertionMiss.description: Top-level miss diagnostic for a failed assertion.

candidates.description:
Top-N near-match candidates, capped externally. Ordered by score
descending.

reason.description: Coarse-grained classification of why the match failed.

AssertionOutcome.description:
Internally-tagged on `status` — `{ "status": "pass", "matched": {...} }`
or `{ "status": "fail", "miss": {...} }`. Distinct from the
worker-output `{ "type": ..., "data": ... }` envelope used elsewhere
in the codebase.

oneOf.description: Assertion passed against the named matched element.

matched.description: The element that satisfied the assertion.

oneOf.description: Assertion failed; the miss carries reason + near-match candidates.

severity.description:
Free-form severity string — `"critical"` / `"error"` / `"warning"`
/ `"info"`. Free-form so policy adapters can introduce new tiers
without bumping the schema.

AssertionScope.description:
Filter narrowing which assertions / states a policy conjunct applies
to. Empty fields (default) mean "everything"; `assertion_ids` uses an
explicit `Option` to distinguish "everything" (`None`) from "none"
(`Some([])`).

assertionIds.description:
Restrict to a specific set of assertion IDs. `None` = all
assertions; `Some([])` = no assertions (a deliberately empty
scope — useful for testing policies).

categories.description: Restrict to these category values. Empty = all categories.

groups.description: Restrict to these group identifiers. Empty = all groups.

severities.description: Restrict to these severity values. Empty = all severities.

states.description: Restrict to these state IDs. Empty = all states.

AssertionScope.title: AssertionScope

AssertionSeverityCounts.description: Counts of assertion failures bucketed by severity.

error.description: Number of `error` failures.

AssertionSeverityCounts.title: AssertionSeverityCounts

TransitionInfo.description: Information about a single available transition from the current state.

to_state.description:
Destination state ID (`null` if the transition stays within the same
state set — e.g. a self-transition or a guard-only action).

workflows.description: Names of workflows attached to this transition.

CanonicalStep.description:
Discriminated union over the four canonical step variants.

Wire format is a flat object with a `"type"` discriminator — serde's
internal tagging merges the inner struct's fields (including the flattened
[`BaseStepFields`]) up into the top-level object. Example:

```text
{"type":"command","id":"s1","name":"build","phase":"setup","mode":"shell","command":"cargo build"}
```

Consumers that want a strict 4-variant typed view should use
[`CanonicalStep`]. Consumers that need to tolerate runner-specific step
types (e.g. `gate`, `screenshot`, `playwright`, `state`, `action`,
`log_watch`, and others dispatched by the runner but absent from the
wire-contract surface) should use [`UnifiedStep`], which preserves
unknown payloads verbatim as `serde_json::Value`.

Variant sizes are similar (~200–672 bytes each); the asymmetry reflects
real differences in step-field cardinality and doesn't warrant boxing.
CanonicalStep.title: CanonicalStep

CanvasPanel.description:
A canvas panel rendered in the dashboard widget.

Wire-format mirror of the runner's `StoredPanel` struct
(`qontinui-runner/src-tauri/src/mcp/canvas.rs`). The runner emits this
inside [`AppEvent::CanvasUpdate`] via the `canvas-update` Tauri channel.
Field names are snake_case to match the existing TS interface in
`qontinui-schemas/ts/src/canvas/index.ts`.

`data` stays `serde_json::Value` because each `component` type has a
different inner shape (Markdown, CodeDiff, Table, …); the per-component
data schemas live in the TS module above and are intentionally not
modeled as a Rust discriminated union (would balloon the schema for
little gain on the Rust side).
CanvasPanel.title: CanvasPanel

Unknown variants are rejected at the wire.

oneOf.description: All assertions in scope must pass.
At most `count` assertions in scope may fail.

count.description: Maximum allowed failures.

oneOf.description:
Fraction of failures (failed / total in scope) must be strictly
less than `rate`.
For every state in scope, its `match_rate` must be at least `rate`.
At least one state in scope must have `match_rate` at least `rate`.

Top-level `match_outcome` must be at least the given outcome,
ordered `NoMatch < PartialMatch < FullMatch`.

ConstraintCheck.description:
What to check and how.

Internally tagged by the `type` field. Variants correspond to the four
check kinds implemented by the runner's constraint engine.

oneOf.description:
Grep modified files for a regex pattern.
Violation if the pattern IS found (use for secrets, debug statements, etc.)

file_glob.description:
Optional glob to limit which modified files are checked.
Default: all modified files.

oneOf.description:
Grep modified files for a regex pattern.
Violation if the pattern is NOT found (use for required headers, licenses, etc.)

file_glob.description: Optional glob to limit which modified files are checked.

pattern.description: Regex pattern that must be present.

oneOf.description:
Check that all modified files are within allowed paths.
Violation if any modified file is outside the allowed directories.

allowed_paths.description:
Allowed directory prefixes (relative to project root).
e.g., `["src/", "tests/", "config/"]`.

oneOf.description:
Run a shell command. Violation if exit code is non-zero.
Useful for quick compilation checks, linting, etc.

cwd.description: Working directory (relative to project root). Default: project root.

ConstraintSeverity.description:
Severity of a constraint violation.

- `Block`: Reject the fix, inject violation context, re-run agentic phase
  without consuming an iteration. After `max_retries`, consume the iteration.
- `Warn`: Apply the fix, but inject violation context for the next iteration.
- `Log`: Record only, don't affect execution.

oneOf.description:
Reject the fix and re-run the agentic phase with injected violation
context. After `max_retries`, the iteration is consumed.
Apply the fix, but inject violation context for the next iteration.
Record the violation only; do not affect execution.

description.description: Why this constraint exists (shown to the AI on violation).

enabled.description: Whether this constraint is enabled. Default: true.

ContextAutoInclude.description:
Rules for automatically including a context in AI tasks.

When an AI task is created, the runner evaluates these rules to decide
which contexts should be auto-included. Multiple rules are OR'd together
(any match triggers inclusion).

actionTypes.description:
Action types in the loaded config that trigger inclusion
(e.g., `CLICK`, `FIND`).

errorPatterns.description: Regex patterns in recent logs that trigger inclusion.

filePatterns.description: Glob patterns for files being worked on (e.g., `*.rs`, `src/api/**`).

taskMentions.description:
Keywords in the task prompt that trigger inclusion
(case-insensitive).

Context.description:
AI context — a markdown document injected into AI task prompts to
provide background knowledge, coding standards, architectural guidance,
or debugging tips.

autoInclude.description: Rules for automatic inclusion in AI tasks.

content.description: Markdown content injected into AI prompts.

id.description:
Unique identifier (UUID v4 or a prefixed string like
`"ctx-schema-flow"`).

tags.description: Tags for flexible grouping and search.

Context.title: Context

CoordinateSystem.description:
Coordinate system identifier. Always specify which system you are working
with to avoid confusion in multi-monitor setups.

oneOf.description:
Absolute screen coordinates (OS-level). May be negative for monitors
left of or above the primary. Used by pyautogui, mouse ops, and
window positioning.

Relative to the virtual desktop origin `(min_x, min_y)`. Always
non-negative within the virtual desktop bounds. Used by screenshot
pixel lookups and FIND match results.

Relative to a specific monitor's top-left corner. Requires
`monitor_index`. Used by `searchRegion` in state configurations.

CoordinateSystem.title: CoordinateSystem

with to avoid confusion in multi-monitor setups.

Absolute screen coordinates (OS-level). May be negative for monitors
left of or above the primary. Used by pyautogui, mouse ops, and
window positioning.

Relative to the virtual desktop origin `(min_x, min_y)`. Always
non-negative within the virtual desktop bounds. Used by screenshot
pixel lookups and FIND match results.

Relative to a specific monitor's top-left corner. Requires
`monitor_index`. Used by `searchRegion` in state configurations.

Coordinates.description:
X,Y coordinates with optional coordinate system specification.

Coordinates without an explicit `system` default to
[`CoordinateSystem::Screen`] for backward compatibility.

x.description: X coordinate (horizontal position).

y.description: Y coordinate (vertical position).

Coordinates.title: Coordinates

CostTrendDataPoint.description: Single data point in a cost-trend time series.

costUsd.description: Total cost in USD for the period.

llmActionCount.description: Number of LLM actions in the period.

runsCount.description: Number of runs in the period.

tokensInput.description: Total input tokens for the period.

tokensOutput.description: Total output tokens for the period.

CostTrendDataPoint.title: CostTrendDataPoint

CostTrendDataPoint.description: Single data point in a cost-trend time series.

costUsd.description: Total cost in USD for the period.

llmActionCount.description: Number of LLM actions in the period.

runsCount.description: Number of runs in the period.

tokensInput.description: Total input tokens for the period.

tokensOutput.description: Total output tokens for the period.

CostTrendResponse.description: Response envelope for the cost-trend analytics endpoint.

endDate.description: End date of the reporting window.

overallStats.description:
Overall cost statistics over the full window. Shape is intentionally
opaque (`dict[str, Any]` in Python).

projectId.description: Project the trend was computed for.

startDate.description: Start date of the reporting window.

CostTrendResponse.title: CostTrendResponse

CoverageData.description: Coverage data computed for a workflow run.

coveragePercentage.description: Overall coverage as a percentage in the range `[0.0, 100.0]`.

stateVisitCounts.description: Per-state visit counts, keyed by state ID.

statesCovered.description: Number of states visited at least once.

totalStates.description: Total number of states in the workflow.

totalTransitions.description: Total number of transitions in the workflow.

transitionExecutionCounts.description: Per-transition execution counts, keyed by transition ID.

transitionsCovered.description: Number of transitions executed at least once.

uncoveredStates.description: IDs of states that were not visited.

uncoveredTransitions.description: IDs of transitions that were not executed.

CoverageData.title: CoverageData

CreateMcpCallInput.description:
Input shape for recording an MCP call to the `mcp_calls` table.

`arguments` / `resolved_arguments` / `response` / `extractions` /
`assertions` are serialized-JSON strings rather than `serde_json::Value`
because the DB layer stores them as `TEXT` / `JSONB` strings and does not
round-trip through a `Value`.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the call failed.

extractions.description: JSON-serialized extractions (variables pulled from the response).

CreateMcpCallInput.title: CreateMcpCallInput

HttpConfig.description:
HTTP-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `headers` typically carries an `Authorization` token.
See module-level docs.

headers.description:
HTTP headers to include on every request. **Secret surface** —
typically includes `Authorization: Bearer …`.

url.description:
Server URL (e.g. `"http://localhost:8080/mcp"`). The runner appends
`/tools/list` and `/tools/call` to this base.

McpTransport.description:
Transport type for an MCP server connection.

Serialized as lowercase to match the existing DB column values
(`"stdio"` / `"http"`). Defaults to `Stdio` to preserve the pre-extraction
default from the runner.

oneOf.description:
Stdio subprocess transport — launches the configured command and
speaks newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout.
HTTP transport — POSTs JSON-RPC 2.0 to the configured URL.

StdioConfig.description:
Stdio-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `command`, `args`, and `env` must be treated as
execution-critical. See module-level docs.

cwd.description: Working directory (absolute path). `None` inherits the runner's cwd.

env.description:
Extra environment variables for the subprocess. **Secret surface** —
frequently holds API tokens.

CreateMcpServerInput.description:
Request body for creating a new MCP server configuration.

**Secret surface**: as with [`McpServerConfig`], the nested transport
configs carry secrets.
CreateMcpServerInput.title: CreateMcpServerInput

CreateOlConfigRequest.description: Request payload for creating a new saved orchestration-loop config.

configJson.description:
The full loop config as a JSON blob (should match the
[`OrchestrationLoopConfig`] shape).

name.description: Human-readable name for the new preset.

CreateOlConfigRequest.title: CreateOlConfigRequest

CatchUpPolicy.description:
Catch-up policy applied when the runner restarts after missing one or
more scheduled slots.

oneOf.description: Run the missed slot once for each occurrence that was skipped.

Skip all missed slots; emit `MissedRunnerDown` history rows for
audit but do not execute.
Collapse multiple missed slots into a single catch-up run.

rearmDelayMinutes.description:
Minutes to wait after an execution completes before re-evaluating
conditions for another run.

IdleCondition.description:
Condition that requires the runner to be idle (not executing workflows or
AI tasks) before the task may run.

McpConnectionRef.description:
Lightweight reference to an MCP connection for [`ScheduledTaskType::RemoteAgent`].

`name` is the MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.
`url` is an optional override; when omitted the runner resolves the URL
from its existing MCP config at dispatch time.

name.description: MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.

url.description:
Optional URL override; falls back to the runner's MCP config when
`None`.

RepositoryInactiveCondition.description:
Condition that requires repositories to have no file modifications for a
period. ALL configured repositories must be inactive for the overall
condition to be met.

repositories.description: List of repositories to watch. ALL must be inactive simultaneously.

RepositoryWatch.description: A single repository to monitor for inactivity.

inactiveMinutes.description: Minutes of inactivity required before the watch is considered met.

path.description: Path to the repository directory.

ScheduleConditions.description: Conditions that must ALL be met before task execution.

requireIdle.description: Require the runner to be idle.

requireRepoInactive.description: Require repository file inactivity across one or more paths.

timeoutMinutes.description:
Maximum time to wait for conditions (minutes). `None` = wait
indefinitely.

ScheduleExpression.description:
How a task should be scheduled.

Serialized with the external tag `type` and payload under `value` so that
`Once("...")`, `Cron("...")`, and `Interval(60)` round-trip as
`{ "type": "Once", "value": "..." }` etc. The `Condition` variant wraps a
[`ConditionScheduleConfig`] rather than a scalar, but uses the same
`{ type, value }` envelope.

oneOf.description: Run once at a specific datetime (ISO 8601).

Cron expression (e.g., `"0 9 * * *"` for 9 AM daily). The runner
accepts both 5-field and 6/7-field cron forms and normalizes internally.
Interval in seconds between runs (for testing/debugging).

Condition-only schedule: no time trigger, runs whenever the attached
[`ScheduleConditions`] on the task are met.

ScheduledTaskType.description:
Type of task to schedule.

Internally tagged by `task_type`: the variant fields are inlined alongside
the discriminator rather than nested under a `value` key.

oneOf.description:
Run a workflow — either a legacy workflow-by-name from a loaded config
or a unified workflow by ID.

config_path.description: Optional path to a workflow config file.

workflow_id.description:
If set, run a unified workflow by ID instead of a legacy workflow
by name.

workflow_name.description: Display name (also used to look up legacy workflows).

oneOf.description: Run a prompt from the Prompt Library.

max_sessions.description:
Optional override for `max_sessions`. `None` uses the prompt's own
setting.

prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to run.

oneOf.description: Trigger auto-fix: check findings and fix any auto-fixable items.

check_findings.description: Whether to check the findings queue before running.

force_run.description: Force a run even if no findings are present.

oneOf.description:
Execute a watcher (screenpipe-inspired reactive AI agent). Queries the
activity timeline, reasons with AI, and triggers an action.

watcher_id.description: ID of the watcher definition in PostgreSQL.

oneOf.description:
Continuous background capture (screenpipe-style). Periodically
captures screen state and stores it in the activity timeline.

capture_on_focus_change.description: Whether to also trigger a capture on window focus change.

oneOf.description:
Run an ad-hoc Claude agent prompt without pre-registering it in the
Prompt Library. Reuses the runner's existing `POST /prompts/run`
ad-hoc surface — no separate Claude CLI plumbing required.

allowed_tools.description:
Allowed tool names (e.g. `["Bash", "Read", "Write", "Edit"]`).
Empty = use the runner's default tool allow-list.

max_turns.description:
Hard cap on Claude turns per run (safety bound). `None` =
runner's default cap (typically 50).

mcp_connections.description:
Optional MCP connection references resolved at dispatch time
against the runner's existing MCP config. Empty = inherit
whatever the runner currently has configured.

prompt.description: The prompt content to send to the Claude session.

timeout_seconds.description:
Wall-clock timeout in seconds. `None` = runner default
(typically 600s = 10 min).

working_directory.description:
Working directory for the spawned session. `None` means the
runner's project root. Stored as a string (not `PathBuf`) to keep
this crate JSON-Schema-friendly.

CreateScheduledTaskRequest.description: Request body for creating a new scheduled task.

catchUpGraceSeconds.description:
Optional catch-up grace window override (seconds). `None` =
runner default (300s).

catchUpPolicy.description:
Optional catch-up policy override. `None` = runner default
([`CatchUpPolicy::RunOnce`]).

conditions.description: Optional conditions that must be met before execution.

launchFailureBackoffSeconds.description:
Optional override for the launch-failure backoff base (seconds).
`None` = runner default (60s).

skipIfCompleted.description: Skip future runs once the task has succeeded.

task.description: Task type and its per-type configuration.

CreateScheduledTaskRequest.title: CreateScheduledTaskRequest

oneOf.description: A one-off AI task launched from a prompt.
A task executed by the scheduler.

CreateTaskRunRequest.description:
Request body for creating a task run (simplified shape used by the runner's
create-task endpoint).

executionStepsJson.description: Serialized execution steps, if provided ad-hoc.

CreateTaskRunRequest.title: CreateTaskRunRequest

CriterionOverride.description:
An override for a verification criterion.

Workers can emit these to indicate that a failing criterion should be
accepted as-is with justification, rather than requiring a fix.

item.description: What specifically is being overridden (e.g., class name, file path).

iteration.description: Iteration when this override was recorded.

justification.description: Justification for why this override is acceptable.

recordedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the override was recorded.

workerId.description: Worker ID that provided the override (if multi-worker).

CriterionOverride.title: CriterionOverride

CriterionType.description: Type of verification for a criterion.

oneOf.description: Can be verified programmatically without AI.

CriterionType.title: CriterionType

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

VerificationCategoryKind.description:
Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].

DagApprovalStep.description:
DAG approval gate — pauses and waits for human approval before continuing.

Wire tag: `"dag_approval"`.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds before auto-approving (to prevent permanent block).

DagApprovalStep.title: DagApprovalStep

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

VerificationCategoryKind.description:
Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].

DagCancelStep.description:
DAG cancel node — terminates the workflow run with a cancellation reason.

Wire tag: `"dag_cancel"`.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

DagCancelStep.title: DagCancelStep

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

VerificationCategoryKind.description:
Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].

DagLoopStep.description:
DAG loop node — repeats a set of steps up to a maximum iteration count.

Wire tag: `"dag_loop"`.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

loopCondition.description: Condition to evaluate each iteration (JSON expression or step ID).

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

DagLoopStep.title: DagLoopStep

DecomposerConfig.description:
Configuration for the task-decomposition subsystem.

When enabled, a single high-level task may be split into a plan of
sub-tasks executed in sequence within the loop.

modelOverride.description: Override the default AI model used for planning.

DecomposerConfig.title: DecomposerConfig

DevSeedFindingPayload.description:
Payload shape emitted on the `dev:seed-finding` Tauri event.

Field names use camelCase so the TS listener (`TauriFindingsListener.ts`)
can spread them directly into a `Finding` object without translation.
The actual emit site is in `commands::dev_findings::dev_seed_finding`.

Renamed in the schema registry to `DevSeedFindingPayload` to disambiguate
from the various `Finding*` types in `qontinui_types::findings`.
DevSeedFindingPayload.title: DevSeedFindingPayload

DeviceBranchSummary.description:
One entry in the `GET /coord/git-ops/branches` response — the latest
branch a given device is on for a given repo, derived from the most
recent `checkout`/`branch_create` op per `(device_id, repo)`.

recorded_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (RFC 3339) of the op this summary was derived from.

DeviceBranchSummary.title: DeviceBranchSummary

DiagnosePhaseConfig.description:
Configuration for the diagnostic-evaluation phase.

Captures UI state after workflow execution and classifies failure root
causes.

assertions.description:
Assertions to run against the UI after workflow execution.
Each assertion is a JSON object passed to
`POST /ui-bridge/control/assert`.

captureSnapshot.description: Whether to capture a full DOM snapshot for AI triage context.

modelOverride.description: Model override for the triage AI call. If `None`, uses default routing.

snapshotMaxChars.description:
Maximum characters to include from the snapshot in the AI triage
prompt.

DiagnosePhaseConfig.title: DiagnosePhaseConfig

RootCauseCategory.description: Root-cause classification for a diagnostic failure.

oneOf.description: The UI itself rendered incorrectly (wrong components, broken layout).
UI Bridge misread the page (element not found, wrong selector).
The assertions/verification are wrong (testing the wrong thing).
The workflow-generation prompt was unclear or incomplete.

The generated state machine has logical errors (wrong transitions,
missing states).
Network error, timeout, crashed app, or other infrastructure failure.

DiagnosticResult.description: Result of a single diagnostic evaluation.

assertionResults.description: Assertion results (each with pass/fail and details).

pageHealth.description: Page-health status blob from UI Bridge.

passed.description: Whether all assertions passed and the page is healthy.

promptRewriteSuggestion.description: AI-generated recommendation for the next iteration's prompt.

rootCause.description: Classified root cause (only meaningful when `passed == false`).

DiagnosticResult.title: DiagnosticResult

ElementRect.description:
Bounding rectangle of a DOM element, mirroring the output of
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`.

Contains both the origin+size pair (`x`, `y`, `width`, `height`) and the
edge offsets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) for consumer convenience.

bottom.description: Distance from the top of the viewport plus `height`.

height.description: Height of the element in CSS pixels.

left.description: Distance from the left of the viewport.

right.description: Distance from the left of the viewport plus `width`.

top.description: Distance from the top of the viewport.

width.description: Width of the element in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the element's origin (same as `left`).

y.description: Y coordinate of the element's origin (same as `top`).

ElementState.description:
Observable state of a UI Bridge element as returned from the React
registry.

Every element returned by the bridge includes a snapshot of its current
visibility, interactivity, and form-control value.

checked.description: Current checked state for checkbox/radio elements.

enabled.description: Whether the element is enabled (not disabled).

focused.description: Whether the element currently has keyboard focus.

visible.description: Whether the element is currently visible in the viewport.

DiscoveredElement.description: An element found during a discovery scan.

registered.description: Whether the element is already registered in the bridge registry.

DiscoveredElement.title: DiscoveredElement

DiscoveredState.description:
A discovered UI state (collection of co-occurring elements).

States represent distinct UI screens or views identified by the set of
images that consistently appear together.

confidence.description: Confidence score for state detection (0.0–1.0). Defaults to `1.0`.

description.description: Description of what this state represents.

elementIds.description: IDs of DOM elements (for web extraction).

imageIds.description: IDs of images in this state.

renderIds.description: IDs of renders where this state appears.

DiscoveredState.title: DiscoveredState

DiscoveryBoundingBox.description:
Bounding box for a discovered image element.

Pixel-space rectangle on a source screenshot. `width` / `height` are `> 0`
on the Python side (validator not duplicated here — this is a wire-format
layer).

height.description: Height of the bounding box (pixels, positive).

width.description: Width of the bounding box (pixels, positive).

x.description: X coordinate of the top-left corner (pixels).

y.description: Y coordinate of the top-left corner (pixels).

DiscoveredStateImage.description:
Visual element within a discovered state.

Represents an image crop from a screenshot with its bounding box and
optional pixel-level identification.

confidence.description: Confidence score for this image (0.0–1.0). Defaults to `1.0`.

pixelHash.description: Hash of pixel data for deduplication.

stateId.description: ID of the state this image belongs to.

DiscoveredStateImage.title: DiscoveredStateImage

DiscoveryTransitionTrigger.description:
Trigger for a discovered state transition.

Describes the action (click, type, …) that caused a transition. All
identifying fields are optional — different discovery sources populate
different subsets.

elementId.description: ID of the DOM element (for web extraction).

imageId.description: ID of the image that was clicked/interacted with.

selector.description: CSS selector for the trigger element.

type.description: Type of trigger action. Defaults to `click` when omitted on the wire.

TransitionTriggerType.description:
Type of action that triggers a state transition.

Mirrors Python `TransitionTriggerType(str, Enum)`.

DiscoveredTransition.description:
A transition between discovered states.

Transitions represent actions that change the active set of states on the
screen.

confidence.description: Confidence score for transition detection (0.0–1.0). Defaults to `1.0`.

DiscoveredTransition.title: DiscoveredTransition

DiscoveryBoundingBox.description:
Bounding box for a discovered image element.

Pixel-space rectangle on a source screenshot. `width` / `height` are `> 0`
on the Python side (validator not duplicated here — this is a wire-format
layer).

height.description: Height of the bounding box (pixels, positive).

width.description: Width of the bounding box (pixels, positive).

x.description: X coordinate of the top-left corner (pixels).

y.description: Y coordinate of the top-left corner (pixels).

DiscoveryBoundingBox.title: DiscoveryBoundingBox

DiscoveryRequest.description:
Options for a UI Bridge element-discovery scan.

Discovery crawls the live DOM and returns elements that match the
provided filters, regardless of whether they are registered in the
bridge registry.

interactiveOnly.description: If `true`, only return interactive elements (buttons, inputs, etc.).

limit.description: Maximum number of elements to return.

root.description: CSS selector for the root element to start scanning from.

selector.description: CSS selector filter (only elements matching this selector).

DiscoveryRequest.title: DiscoveryRequest

DiscoveredElement.description: An element found during a discovery scan.

registered.description: Whether the element is already registered in the bridge registry.

ElementRect.description:
Bounding rectangle of a DOM element, mirroring the output of
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`.

Contains both the origin+size pair (`x`, `y`, `width`, `height`) and the
edge offsets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) for consumer convenience.

bottom.description: Distance from the top of the viewport plus `height`.

height.description: Height of the element in CSS pixels.

left.description: Distance from the left of the viewport.

right.description: Distance from the left of the viewport plus `width`.

top.description: Distance from the top of the viewport.

width.description: Width of the element in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the element's origin (same as `left`).

y.description: Y coordinate of the element's origin (same as `top`).

ElementState.description:
Observable state of a UI Bridge element as returned from the React
registry.

Every element returned by the bridge includes a snapshot of its current
visibility, interactivity, and form-control value.

checked.description: Current checked state for checkbox/radio elements.

enabled.description: Whether the element is enabled (not disabled).

focused.description: Whether the element currently has keyboard focus.

visible.description: Whether the element is currently visible in the viewport.

DiscoveryResponse.description: Response from a UI Bridge discovery scan.

durationMs.description: Time taken for the discovery scan in milliseconds.

elements.description: Discovered elements matching the request filters.

timestamp.description: Unix-epoch millisecond timestamp of the scan.

total.description: Total number of elements found (before any limit).

DiscoveryResponse.title: DiscoveryResponse

DiscoverySourceType.description:
Source of a state-discovery result.

Identifies which discovery pathway produced the state machine. Mirrors
Python `DiscoverySourceType(str, Enum)`.
DiscoverySourceType.title: DiscoverySourceType

DiscoveryStrategy.description:
Strategy used by the state-discovery pass.

Serialized as a bare lowercase string.
DiscoveryStrategy.title: DiscoveryStrategy

TransitionTriggerType.description:
Type of action that triggers a state transition.

Mirrors Python `TransitionTriggerType(str, Enum)`.

DiscoveryTransitionTrigger.description:
Trigger for a discovered state transition.

Describes the action (click, type, …) that caused a transition. All
identifying fields are optional — different discovery sources populate
different subsets.

elementId.description: ID of the DOM element (for web extraction).

imageId.description: ID of the image that was clicked/interacted with.

selector.description: CSS selector for the trigger element.

type.description: Type of trigger action. Defaults to `click` when omitted on the wire.

DiscoveryTransitionTrigger.title: DiscoveryTransitionTrigger

MatchLocation.description: Location of a pattern match on screen.

h.description: Height of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

w.description: Width of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

NodeMetadata.description:
Metadata for a tree node — action configuration, runtime data, state
context, and timing. All fields are optional because different node
types populate different fields.

config.description: Action configuration (JSON object — shape varies by `ActionType`).

isExpandable.description: Whether this action can have child nodes.

isInline.description: Whether this action is displayed inline.

NodeStatus.description: Execution status of a tree node.

NodeType.description: Types of nodes in the execution tree.

RuntimeData.description:
Runtime execution data captured during action execution.

Different fields are populated depending on the action type. All fields
are optional; the wire schema allows additional runtime fields via a
`flatten`-d map.

StateContext.description: State-machine context captured before/after an action.

TimingInfo.description:
Precise timing information for an event. Times are ISO 8601 strings (see
crate-level docs for the rationale — the types crate is wire-only and
doesn't depend on a chrono version).

endTime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. `None` while the event is still in flight.

TopMatch.description: A single match result with confidence and location.

DisplayNode.description:
Display node structure used by the frontend — extended version of
`TreeNode` with tree-rendering properties. NOT persisted; constructed
from `TreeNode` data for UI display.

isExpanded.description: Whether this node should be expanded in the UI (default: true).

level.description: Nesting level in the tree (0 for root, 1 for first-level children).

DisplayNode.title: DisplayNode

DomainAssignment.description:
Full configuration for a domain that workers can be assigned to.

Domains represent logical areas of responsibility within a project
(e.g., "frontend", "backend", "database", "api"). Workers are assigned to
zero or more domains, and criteria can be scoped to a single domain for
multi-worker verification.

assignedWorkers.description: Workers currently assigned to this domain.

description.description: Description of what this domain covers.

domainCriteria.description: Success-criterion IDs that are specific to this domain.

filePatterns.description:
File patterns that belong to this domain
(e.g., `"src/frontend/**/*.ts"`).

keywords.description: Keywords that help identify this domain.

systemPromptContext.description: Additional system-prompt context for workers in this domain.

DomainAssignment.title: DomainAssignment

DomainKnowledge.description:
A piece of domain knowledge attached to a state — free-form notes that
help the AI reason about what a state represents.

content.description: Full knowledge content (markdown or plain text).

id.description: Unique identifier for this knowledge entry.

DomainKnowledge.title: DomainKnowledge

Confidence.description: Confidence level for findings and verification results.

oneOf.description: High confidence — deterministic evidence or clear observation.
Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.
Low confidence — speculative or weakly supported.

CriterionType.description: Type of verification for a criterion.

oneOf.description: Can be verified programmatically without AI.

VerificationResult.description: Result of a single verification check.

confidence.description: Confidence level (for AI verification).

rawOutput.description: Raw output / details, e.g., captured command output.

workerIds.description: Workers that contributed to this domain.

WaitOptions.description:
Wait-condition options attached to an element action request.

Before executing the action the bridge can optionally wait until the
target element reaches a specified visibility/enabled/focused state.

enabled.description: Wait until the element is enabled (or disabled if `false`).

focused.description: Wait until the element has focus (or loses focus if `false`).

interval.description: Polling interval in milliseconds for condition checks.

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait in milliseconds before timing out.

visible.description: Wait until the element is visible (or hidden if `false`).

ElementActionRequest.description: Request to execute an action on a UI Bridge element.

waitOptions.description: Optional wait conditions to satisfy before executing.

ElementActionRequest.title: ElementActionRequest

ElementBbox.description:
Viewport-relative bounding box in CSS pixels.

This is the live on-screen geometry captured at snapshot time via
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`. Present only when the SDK has a
live DOM ref for the element; absent when the element is registered
without a ref or when the snapshot is served from the DOM-fallback
scanner.

Click target for a hit is `(x + width/2, y + height/2)`.

height.description: Height of the bbox in CSS pixels.

width.description: Width of the bbox in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the bbox origin in viewport CSS pixels.

y.description: Y coordinate of the bbox origin in viewport CSS pixels.

ElementIdentifier.description:
Identifier bundle for locating a UI Bridge element.

Elements can be addressed by any combination of UI-Bridge ID, test ID,
AWAS ID, HTML ID, XPath, or CSS selector. The `xpath` and `selector`
fields are always present; the named IDs are optional.

ElementRect.description:
Bounding rectangle of a DOM element, mirroring the output of
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`.

Contains both the origin+size pair (`x`, `y`, `width`, `height`) and the
edge offsets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) for consumer convenience.

bottom.description: Distance from the top of the viewport plus `height`.

height.description: Height of the element in CSS pixels.

left.description: Distance from the left of the viewport.

right.description: Distance from the left of the viewport plus `width`.

top.description: Distance from the top of the viewport.

width.description: Width of the element in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the element's origin (same as `left`).

y.description: Y coordinate of the element's origin (same as `top`).

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

Run a sequence of AI prompts.

ExecutionStep.description:
A single step in an AI Builder execution sequence.

This is the **legacy step shape** persisted in `ai_workflows.json`. It
carries fields for every step flavour (`workflow`, `state`, `playwright`,
`prompt`, `action`, `screenshot`) as optional branches. Steps that don't
apply to the given `step_type` have their branch fields set to `None`.

actionType.description:
For action steps: the action type (`"click"`, `"double_click"`,
`"right_click"`).

playwrightScriptContent.description: For playwright steps: the script content.

playwrightScriptId.description: For playwright steps: the script ID.

playwrightTargetUrl.description: For playwright steps: the target URL.

promptContent.description: For prompt steps: the actual prompt content.

promptId.description: For prompt steps: the prompt ID from the library.

screenshotDelay.description: Delay in seconds before taking screenshot (default 0).

screenshotMonitor.description:
Monitor for screenshot capture (number for specific monitor, `"all"`
for all monitors).

takeScreenshot.description: Whether to capture a screenshot after this step.

targetImageId.description: For action steps: the target image ID.

targetImageName.description: For action steps: the target image name (for display).

ExecutionStep.title: ExecutionStep

DisplayNode.description:
Display node structure used by the frontend — extended version of
`TreeNode` with tree-rendering properties. NOT persisted; constructed
from `TreeNode` data for UI display.

isExpanded.description: Whether this node should be expanded in the UI (default: true).

level.description: Nesting level in the tree (0 for root, 1 for first-level children).

MatchLocation.description: Location of a pattern match on screen.

h.description: Height of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

w.description: Width of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

NodeMetadata.description:
Metadata for a tree node — action configuration, runtime data, state
context, and timing. All fields are optional because different node
types populate different fields.

config.description: Action configuration (JSON object — shape varies by `ActionType`).

isExpandable.description: Whether this action can have child nodes.

isInline.description: Whether this action is displayed inline.

NodeStatus.description: Execution status of a tree node.

NodeType.description: Types of nodes in the execution tree.

RuntimeData.description:
Runtime execution data captured during action execution.

Different fields are populated depending on the action type. All fields
are optional; the wire schema allows additional runtime fields via a
`flatten`-d map.

StateContext.description: State-machine context captured before/after an action.

TimingInfo.description:
Precise timing information for an event. Times are ISO 8601 strings (see
crate-level docs for the rationale — the types crate is wire-only and
doesn't depend on a chrono version).

endTime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. `None` while the event is still in flight.

TopMatch.description: A single match result with confidence and location.

ExecutionTreeResponse.description: Full execution tree reconstructed from events.

initialStateIds.description: Initial active states when the workflow started.

stateNameMap.description: Mapping of state IDs to display names.

ExecutionTreeResponse.title: ExecutionTreeResponse

runsCount.description: Number of runs in the period.

totalActions.description: Total actions executed in the period.

oneOf.description: High confidence — deterministic evidence or clear observation.
Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.
Low confidence — speculative or weakly supported.

Finding.description: A finding recorded by a worker agent.

confidence.description: Confidence level.

evidence.description: Supporting evidence (file paths, log excerpts, etc.).

findingType.description:
Type of finding.

Valid types: `"bug"`, `"root_cause"`, `"observation"`, `"hypothesis"`,
`"solution"`, `"environment"`. Note: `"environment"` findings (PATH
issues, disk space, tools not installed) require user intervention and
should NOT trigger automatic retries.

Finding.title: Finding

FindingActionType.description:
Type of action recommended for a finding.

Mirrors Python `FindingActionType(str, Enum)`.

oneOf.description: Can be automatically fixed without user intervention.
Requires user decision or input to resolve.
No action needed — for awareness only.

FindingActionType.title: FindingActionType

FindingActionType.description:
Type of action recommended for a finding.

Mirrors Python `FindingActionType(str, Enum)`.

oneOf.description: Can be automatically fixed without user intervention.
Requires user decision or input to resolve.
No action needed — for awareness only.

FindingCategory.description:
Category of a detected finding.

Determines the kind of issue or observation surfaced during analysis.
Mirrors Python `FindingCategory(str, Enum)`.

oneOf.description: A security concern (vulnerability, exposed secret, etc.).
A runtime issue observed during execution.
An issue that was already fixed.
Behavior that looked suspicious but is expected.

FindingCodeContext.description:
Code context for a finding.

Provides location (file/line/column) and an optional snippet for findings
that relate to specific code.

column.description: Column number where the finding was detected.

file.description: File path where the finding was detected.

line.description: Line number where the finding was detected.

snippet.description: Code snippet related to the finding (max 1000 chars on the Python side).

FindingCreate.description:
Schema for creating a finding (Runner → Backend).

Sent by the runner when an AI analysis session detects an issue or
observation.

actionType.description: Type of action for this finding.

codeContext.description: Code context, if the finding relates to specific code.

sessionNum.description: Session number where the finding was detected.

signatureHash.description: Hash used to deduplicate findings across sessions.

title.description: Brief title describing the finding (max 500 chars on the Python side).

userInput.description: User-input request, if `action_type` requires a user decision.

FindingSeverity.description:
Severity level of a finding.

Lifecycle (ordered, most-severe first): `CRITICAL` → `HIGH` → `MEDIUM` →
`LOW` → `INFO`. Mirrors Python `FindingSeverity(str, Enum)`.

oneOf.description: Major issue — should be fixed soon.

FindingUserInput.description:
User-input request attached to a finding.

Defines the question to pose and the expected input format when a finding
requires a user decision.

inputType.description: Type of input expected — typically `"text"` or `"choice"`.

question.description: Question to present to the user.

FindingBatchCreate.description:
Request schema for batch finding creation.

Allows creating multiple findings in a single request. The Python side
enforces `1 <= len(findings) <= 50`; validators on the Rust side are
intentionally omitted to keep this a pure wire-format layer.

findings.description: Findings to create (1–50 items on the Python side).

TaskRunFinding.description:
A finding surfaced during a task run (bug, enhancement, TODO, etc.).

All nullable fields here are required-nullable on the wire (always present,
possibly `null`), so they use `serde(default)` without `skip_serializing_if`.

actionType.description: How the finding should be acted upon.

codeSnippet.description: Snippet of code illustrating the issue.

columnNumber.description: Column number where the issue was found.

detectedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was detected.

detectedInSession.description: Session number in which the finding was detected.

filePath.description: File path where the issue was found.

inputOptions.description: Suggested response options for the user, if input is needed.

lineNumber.description: Line number where the issue was found.

needsInput.description: Whether this finding requires user input.

question.description: Question posed to the user, if input is needed.

resolution.description: How the finding was resolved, if applicable.

resolvedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was resolved.

resolvedInSession.description: Session number in which the finding was resolved.

signatureHash.description: Hash used to deduplicate findings across runs.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the record was last updated.

TaskRunFindingActionType.description: How a finding should be acted upon.

TaskRunFindingCategory.description: Category of a finding surfaced during a task run.

oneOf.description: A security issue (e.g., vulnerability, exposed secret).
A runtime issue observed during execution.
An issue that was already fixed.
Behavior that looked suspicious but is expected.
Critical severity — blocks release or execution.
Needs user input before it can proceed.

FlowEvent.description:
Events emitted during flow execution for UI updates.

Internally tagged with `"type"` and snake_case variant names.

oneOf.description: A step is about to execute.

Flow execution was paused via `pause_flow_execution`.

Emitted on the `flow-event` Tauri channel by
`qontinui-runner/src-tauri/src/commands/flow.rs::pause_flow_execution`
and consumed by `useFlowExecutionData.ts`. `step_id` is intentionally
emitted as `null` when no current step (rather than skipped) to
preserve the legacy `serde_json::json!` wire shape.

step_id.description: Step the flow was paused at (`null` if no current step).

oneOf.description:
Flow execution was resumed via `resume_flow_execution` after a pause.

Emitted on the `flow-event` Tauri channel by
`qontinui-runner/src-tauri/src/commands/flow.rs::resume_flow_execution`.
`step_id` is intentionally emitted as `null` when no current step.

step_id.description: Step execution will resume from (`null` if no current step).

VgaAction.description:
A single action inside a [`VgaAutomateStep`] action sequence.

Wire shape is internally tagged with `"kind"`:
- `{ "kind": "click", "elementId": "<uuid>", "timeoutMs": 10000 }`
- `{ "kind": "type", "text": "hello", "elementId": "<uuid>", "timeoutMs": 10000 }`
  (`elementId` omitted/null = type into the currently focused element)
- `{ "kind": "wait_for", "elementId": "<uuid>", "timeoutMs": 30000 }`

oneOf.description:
Click the element identified by `element_id` (UUID referencing an
element inside the VGA state machine).

elementId.description: UUID of the target element in the VGA state machine's element set.

timeoutMs.description:
Per-action timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `10000` on the
consumer side.

oneOf.description:
Type `text` into `element_id`, or into the currently focused element
when `element_id` is absent/`null`.

elementId.description:
UUID of the target element. Absent / `null` means "type into
whichever element currently has focus".

timeoutMs.description:
Per-action timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `10000` on the
consumer side.

oneOf.description: Wait for `element_id` to become visible / actionable.

timeoutMs.description:
Per-action timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `30000` on the
consumer side.

VgaAutomateStep.description:
Run a Visual GUI Automation (VGA) action sequence against a target process.

Wire tag: `"vga_automate"`.

Delegates to the Python `qontinui.vga.worker` via the `python-bridge` IPC.
The worker loads the referenced state machine (`state_machine_id` →
`runner.vga_state_machines`), focuses `target_process`, and executes each
entry in `action_sequence` by grounding the element prompt against a fresh
screenshot and dispatching the HAL click/type/wait primitive.

Canonical field name for the VGA state machine reference is
`vgaStateMachineId` (camelCase) in emitted/accepted JSON. Two
legacy aliases exist for back-compat: `vga_state_machine_id`
(snake_case) and the bare `stateMachineId`. New code must emit
`vgaStateMachineId`. Aliases may be removed after 2026-Q3.

actionSequence.description: Ordered sequence of VGA actions to execute.

async.description:
Reserved for future async mode. Currently must be `false` (or omitted)
— the handler rejects `true` until async mode is implemented.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

stateMachineId.description:
UUID referencing `runner.vga_state_machines.id` — the persisted state
machine that defines the elements this step may click / type into /
wait for.

targetProcess.description:
Target process / window — e.g. `"notepad++.exe"`. Used by the HAL to
focus the correct top-level window before each action.

timeoutMs.description:
Overall step timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `300000` (5 minutes)
on the consumer side; bounds `[1000, 3600000]`.

Run deterministic Rust fixups on a workflow JSON file.

Wire tag: `"workflow_fixup"`.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

fixupCriteriaPath.description: Path to criteria JSON file (for `validate_criteria` mode).

fixupInputPath.description: Path to the workflow JSON file to fix (supports `{{artifact_dir}}`).

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

refInheritModelOverrides.description: Whether to inherit model overrides from the parent context (default: `true`).

refWorkflowInputs.description: Input variables substituted into the child workflow's prompt.

refWorkflowName.description: Display name of the referenced workflow (denormalized for UI).

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).
