FullRunnerStep.description:
Fully typed discriminated union over **all** step variants registered in
the runner's `HandlerRegistry`.

## Wire format

Internally tagged with `"type"`, matching existing JSON on the wire:
```json
{"type": "command", "mode": "shell", "command": "cargo build", ...}
{"type": "prompt", "phase": "agentic", "content": "..."}
{"type": "ui_bridge", "action": "navigate", "url": "..."}
{"type": "code_execution", "code": "print('hello')"}
```

## Step arrays remain `Vec<serde_json::Value>`

`UnifiedWorkflow.setup_steps` / `.verification_steps` / `.agentic_steps` /
`.completion_steps` stay as `Vec<serde_json::Value>` until the Session 2
migration lands. `FullRunnerStep` is available for typed access but is not
yet threaded into the workflow frame fields.

## Variant coverage

| Variant | Wire tag | Handler |
|---------|----------|---------|
| `Command` | `"command"` | `CommandHandler` (sub-modes: shell/check/check_group/test) |
| `Prompt` | `"prompt"` | `PromptStepHandler` |
| `UiBridge` | `"ui_bridge"` | `UiBridgeHandler` (actions: navigate/execute/assert/snapshot/compare/snapshot_assert/action_plan) |
| `Workflow` | `"workflow"` | `WorkflowStepHandler` |
| `CodeExecution` | `"code_execution"` | `CodeExecutionHandler` |
| `ExecutePlaybook` | `"execute_playbook"` | `ExecutePlaybookHandler` |
| `NativeAccessibility` | `"native_accessibility"` | `NativeAccessibilityHandler` |
| `RestartProcess` | `"restart_process"` | `RestartProcessHandler` |
| `SaveWorkflowArtifact` | `"save_workflow_artifact"` | `SaveWorkflowArtifactHandler` |
| `WorkflowFixup` | `"workflow_fixup"` | `WorkflowFixupHandler` |
| `UiBridgeDesignAudit` | `"ui_bridge_design_audit"` | `UiBridgeDesignAuditHandler` |
| `UiBridgeVisualAssertion` | `"ui_bridge_visual_assertion"` | `UiBridgeVisualAssertionHandler` |
| `VgaAutomate` | `"vga_automate"` | `VgaAutomateHandler` |
| `WorkflowRef` | `"workflow_ref"` | `WorkflowRefHandler` |
| `DagCancel` | `"dag_cancel"` | `dag_nodes::DagCancelHandler` |
| `DagApproval` | `"dag_approval"` | `dag_nodes::DagApprovalHandler` |
| `DagLoop` | `"dag_loop"` | `dag_nodes::DagLoopHandler` |

Variant sizes range ~200–672 bytes depending on each step struct's field
cardinality. `#[allow(large_enum_variant)]` because the sizes reflect real
step shapes; boxing would add heap indirection on every deserialize and
dispatch without meaningful savings — no hot path holds dense
`Vec<FullRunnerStep>` in memory.
FullRunnerStep.title: FullRunnerStep

ActionType.description:
Type of action executed.

Covers vision, input, state-machine, control-flow, utility, AI, and custom
action kinds. Variants are serialized as snake_case strings.

oneOf.description: Locate a single pattern in the current scene.
Locate all instances of a pattern in the current scene.
Wait for a pattern to appear.
Wait for a pattern to disappear.
Drag from one point to another.
Verify the current state matches expectations.
Run a sequence of AI prompts.

HistoricalActionQuery.description: Query filters for looking up historical action results.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (Python constrains to `1..=100`).

successOnly.description: If true, only successful actions are returned.

workflowId.description: Filter by workflow ID.

HistoricalActionQuery.title: HistoricalActionQuery

ActionStatus.description: Outcome of a single action within a run.

oneOf.description: The action failed (e.g., assertion failure, element not found).
The action exceeded its configured time limit.
The action was skipped (e.g., by a conditional).
The action errored at a system level (e.g., crash, network error).
The action has not been executed yet.

HTTP-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `headers` typically carries an `Authorization` token.
See module-level docs.

headers.description:
HTTP headers to include on every request. **Secret surface** —
typically includes `Authorization: Bearer …`.

url.description:
Server URL (e.g. `"http://localhost:8080/mcp"`). The runner appends
`/tools/list` and `/tools/call` to this base.

HttpMethod.description:
HTTP methods accepted by API-request command steps.

Serialized uppercase (`GET`, `POST`, …) to match HTTP convention.
HttpMethod.title: HttpMethod

IdleCondition.description:
Condition that requires the runner to be idle (not executing workflows or
AI tasks) before the task may run.
IdleCondition.title: IdleCondition

ImplementFixesConfig.description: Configuration for the implement-fixes phase (Claude CLI).

additionalContext.description: Additional context to include in the fix prompt.

model.description:
Model to use (e.g., `"claude-opus-4-6"`). Defaults to `claude-opus-4-6`
when unset.

timeoutSecs.description: Timeout in seconds for the fix agent. Defaults to 600 when unset.

ImplementFixesConfig.title: ImplementFixesConfig

CheckIssueDetail.description: A specific issue detail from an individual verification check.

code.description: Error code or lint rule, if applicable.

file.description: File path where the issue was detected.

fixable.description: Whether the check can auto-fix this issue.

severity.description: Severity label (free-form string from the upstream check).

IndividualCheckResult.description: Result of a single named verification check (e.g., `"eslint"`, `"mypy"`).

durationMs.description: How long the check took, in milliseconds.

errorMessage.description: Error message, if the check itself failed to run.

filesChecked.description: Number of files the check inspected.

issuesFixed.description: Number of issues auto-fixed by this check.

issuesFound.description: Number of issues surfaced by this check.

InitialStateRef.description:
A lightweight reference to a state by id and display name.

Used in [`ResolvedInitialStates`] and [`ResolvedInitialStatesResult`] to
let the UI render human-readable lists without a separate lookup.

InitialStatesSource.description:
Source of initial states configuration.

- `Defaults`: states with `is_initial=true` in the state machine definition

- `Override`: session-only override from the runner UI

Serialized as a bare lowercase string (`"defaults"` / `"workflow"` /
`"override"`) to match the TS literal union.

emitted assertions; the wider legacy schema also supports point/region

partially-populated criteria without tripping the schema and so the
projection can pass the bytes through verbatim.

appId.description:
Owning app id (slug). Multi-tenant Spec API contract: every IR
document is self-describing about which registered app it belongs
to. `#[serde(default)]` allows pre-multi-app on-disk IRs (no
`app_id` field) to deserialize as empty string; handlers and the
`POST /apps/<id>/spec/author` adapter validate non-empty and fill
from the URL path on read (Stream C safety-net). Stream F's bulk
backfill rewrites every on-disk IR so empty values don't persist.
TS / Python codegen promote serde defaults to required per the
`qontinui-schemas` project convention.

`skip_serializing_if = "String::is_empty"` preserves byte-identical
round-trip for pre-multi-app on-disk IRs that lack the field.
Post-Stream-F backfill, every IR has a non-empty `app_id` so the
skip never fires.

status.description:
Lifecycle status for the flywheel coverage-growth loop. `None` =
implicitly `Promoted` (legacy + on-disk specs that predate the field).
Set to `Proposed` by `spec_authoring`, `Pending` when staged in
`_pending/`, `Promoted` after the 2-green sweep moves the file to
`pages/<id>/`.

ProposalStatus.description:
Lifecycle status for spec proposals produced by the Stream E coverage-growth
flywheel. `None` on an existing `IrProvenance` means the spec predates the
flywheel and is implicitly `Promoted`.

oneOf.description: Candidate emitted by `spec_authoring` but not yet validated.
Candidate landed in `_pending/`; awaiting consecutive greens.
Candidate has been promoted to `pages/<id>/`. Default for legacy specs.

IrPageSpec.description:
Top-level IR page specification (formerly `IrDocument`).

One `IrPageSpec` corresponds to one `state-machine.derived.json` file on
disk under `qontinui-runner/specs/pages/<id>/`. This is the authoring
surface — the projection layer derives the runtime `LegacySpec` from this.

synthesizedGroups.description:
Groups produced by workflow-criteria synthesis (NOT derived from
`IRState` annotations). Mirror of TS `IRDocument.synthesizedGroups`.
The `rename = "synthesizedGroups"` is explicit (rather than relying on
`rename_all = "camelCase"`) so the serialized name is unambiguous in
snapshot tests + cross-language fixtures.

IrPageSpec.title: IrPageSpec

IssueSeverity.description: Severity of an issue reported against a run.

oneOf.description: Critical severity — blocks release or execution.

IssueSeverity.title: IssueSeverity

IssueStatus.description: Lifecycle status of an issue reported against a run.

oneOf.description: The issue has just been reported.
The issue has been triaged and is open.
The issue is being worked on.
The issue will not be fixed.

IssueStatus.title: IssueStatus

IssueType.description: Category label describing the kind of issue detected.

oneOf.description: An operation exceeded its time limit.
The observed state did not match expectations.
A target element could not be located.

IssueType.title: IssueType

DiagnosticResult.description: Result of a single diagnostic evaluation.

assertionResults.description: Assertion results (each with pass/fail and details).

pageHealth.description: Page-health status blob from UI Bridge.

passed.description: Whether all assertions passed and the page is healthy.

promptRewriteSuggestion.description: AI-generated recommendation for the next iteration's prompt.

rootCause.description: Classified root cause (only meaningful when `passed == false`).

ExitCheckResult.description: Result of evaluating whether the loop should exit.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the decision.

shouldExit.description: Whether the loop should stop after this iteration.

RootCauseCategory.description: Root-cause classification for a diagnostic failure.

oneOf.description: The UI itself rendered incorrectly (wrong components, broken layout).
UI Bridge misread the page (element not found, wrong selector).
The assertions/verification are wrong (testing the wrong thing).
The workflow-generation prompt was unclear or incomplete.

The generated state machine has logical errors (wrong transitions,
missing states).
Network error, timeout, crashed app, or other infrastructure failure.

contextSummarized.description: Whether the loop context was summarized during this iteration.

diagnosticResult.description: Diagnostic-phase result (if the diagnose phase is configured).

fixCount.description: Number of fixes proposed during reflection.

fixesImplemented.description: Pipeline mode: whether fixes were implemented during this iteration.

generatedWorkflowId.description: Pipeline mode: ID of the workflow generated during the build phase.

rebuildTriggered.description: Pipeline mode: whether a rebuild was triggered for the next iteration.

reflectionTaskRunId.description: Task-run ID produced by the reflection step (if any).

stallDetected.description: Stall-detection reason, if a stall was detected.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 start timestamp.

taskRunId.description: Task-run ID produced by the workflow execution.

Confidence.description: Confidence level for findings and verification results.

oneOf.description: High confidence — deterministic evidence or clear observation.
Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.
Low confidence — speculative or weakly supported.

CriterionOverride.description:
An override for a verification criterion.

Workers can emit these to indicate that a failing criterion should be
accepted as-is with justification, rather than requiring a fix.

item.description: What specifically is being overridden (e.g., class name, file path).

iteration.description: Iteration when this override was recorded.

justification.description: Justification for why this override is acceptable.

recordedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the override was recorded.

workerId.description: Worker ID that provided the override (if multi-worker).

CriterionType.description: Type of verification for a criterion.

oneOf.description: Can be verified programmatically without AI.

VerificationResult.description: Result of a single verification check.

confidence.description: Confidence level (for AI verification).

rawOutput.description: Raw output / details, e.g., captured command output.

IterationVerificationResults.description: Aggregated verification results for a single iteration.

aiPassed.description: Whether all required AI checks passed (true if no AI criteria).

appliedOverrides.description: Criterion overrides applied in this iteration.

deterministicPassed.description: Whether all required deterministic checks passed.

overriddenCriteria.description: Criteria that failed but were accepted due to overrides.

IterationVerificationResults.title: IterationVerificationResults

ModelCostBreakdown.description: Cost breakdown for a single LLM model.

LogSourceMode.description:
Simple log-source mode.

Serialized as a bare lowercase string: `"default"`, `"ai"`, or `"all"`.

oneOf.description: Use the global default profile (from Settings → Log Sources).
Let AI automatically select relevant sources based on context.

profile_id.description: ID of the log-source profile to use.

LogSourceSelection.description:
Log source selection for a workflow.

- `"default"` / `"ai"` / `"all"`: Use the corresponding [`LogSourceMode`].
- `{ "profile_id": "..." }`: Use a specific profile.

Serialized as an untagged union: a bare string for the mode variants, or an
object with a `profile_id` key for the profile variant.
LogSourceSelection.title: LogSourceSelection

DiagnosticResult.description: Result of a single diagnostic evaluation.

assertionResults.description: Assertion results (each with pass/fail and details).

pageHealth.description: Page-health status blob from UI Bridge.

passed.description: Whether all assertions passed and the page is healthy.

promptRewriteSuggestion.description: AI-generated recommendation for the next iteration's prompt.

rootCause.description: Classified root cause (only meaningful when `passed == false`).

ExitCheckResult.description: Result of evaluating whether the loop should exit.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the decision.

shouldExit.description: Whether the loop should stop after this iteration.

contextSummarized.description: Whether the loop context was summarized during this iteration.

diagnosticResult.description: Diagnostic-phase result (if the diagnose phase is configured).

fixCount.description: Number of fixes proposed during reflection.

fixesImplemented.description: Pipeline mode: whether fixes were implemented during this iteration.

generatedWorkflowId.description: Pipeline mode: ID of the workflow generated during the build phase.

rebuildTriggered.description: Pipeline mode: whether a rebuild was triggered for the next iteration.

reflectionTaskRunId.description: Task-run ID produced by the reflection step (if any).

stallDetected.description: Stall-detection reason, if a stall was detected.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 start timestamp.

taskRunId.description: Task-run ID produced by the workflow execution.

LoopPhase.description: Current phase of the orchestration loop.

oneOf.description: Executing the workflow on the target runner.
Checking whether the exit condition is met.
Waiting for the fixer workflow to complete.
Waiting for the target runner to become healthy.

OrchestrationLoopStatus.description: Runtime state of an orchestration loop.

currentIteration.description: Iteration index (1-based) currently executing or just completed.

error.description: Terminal error message (only set in the `Error` phase).

isPipeline.description: Whether this loop is in pipeline mode.

maxIterations.description: Iteration cap for this run. `None` renders as `"∞"`/unlimited in the UI.

phase.description: Current phase.

running.description: Whether the loop is currently running.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 start timestamp.

workflowId.description: The workflow ID being executed.

RootCauseCategory.description: Root-cause classification for a diagnostic failure.

oneOf.description: The UI itself rendered incorrectly (wrong components, broken layout).
UI Bridge misread the page (element not found, wrong selector).
The assertions/verification are wrong (testing the wrong thing).
The workflow-generation prompt was unclear or incomplete.

The generated state machine has logical errors (wrong transitions,
missing states).
Network error, timeout, crashed app, or other infrastructure failure.

CoordinateSystem.description:
Coordinate system identifier. Always specify which system you are working
with to avoid confusion in multi-monitor setups.

oneOf.description:
Absolute screen coordinates (OS-level). May be negative for monitors
left of or above the primary. Used by pyautogui, mouse ops, and
window positioning.

Relative to the virtual desktop origin `(min_x, min_y)`. Always
non-negative within the virtual desktop bounds. Used by screenshot
pixel lookups and FIND match results.

Relative to a specific monitor's top-left corner. Requires
`monitor_index`. Used by `searchRegion` in state configurations.

Coordinates.description:
X,Y coordinates with optional coordinate system specification.

Coordinates without an explicit `system` default to
[`CoordinateSystem::Screen`] for backward compatibility.

x.description: X coordinate (horizontal position).

y.description: Y coordinate (vertical position).

MatchAdjustment.description: Match adjustment — modify the matched region before acting.
MatchAdjustment.title: MatchAdjustment

MatchLocation.description: Location of a pattern match on screen.

h.description: Height of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

w.description: Width of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

MatchLocation.title: MatchLocation

MatchMethod.description: Template matching method used by OpenCV-style correlation search.
MatchMethod.title: MatchMethod

oneOf.description: All critical assertions passed across all states.
Some critical assertions failed; some passed.

MatchedElement.description: Identity of the bridge element that satisfied an assertion.

path.description: CSS selector or DOM path identifying the element.

role.description: ARIA role of the matched element.

text.description: Visible text of the matched element.

MatchedElement.title: MatchedElement

McpCallRecord.description:
Persisted MCP call record as read back from the `mcp_calls` table.

Same shape as [`CreateMcpCallInput`] plus the row id and creation
timestamp.
McpCallRecord.title: McpCallRecord

McpCallRecord.description:
Persisted MCP call record as read back from the `mcp_calls` table.

Same shape as [`CreateMcpCallInput`] plus the row id and creation
timestamp.

McpCallsResult.description: Result envelope for an `mcp_calls` query scoped to a single task run.

calls.description: Matched call records (paginated; see `total_count` for the full size).

count.description: Number of calls returned in `calls`.

failedCount.description: Number of failed calls in the current page.

hasMore.description: Whether there are more rows after the current page.

successCount.description: Number of successful calls in the current page.

taskRunId.description: Task run the query was scoped to.

totalCount.description:
Total matching rows across all pages. Defaults to `0` on older rows
that predate the field; consumers should prefer `count` when this is
absent.

McpCallsResult.title: McpCallsResult

McpConnectionRef.description:
Lightweight reference to an MCP connection for [`ScheduledTaskType::RemoteAgent`].

`name` is the MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.
`url` is an optional override; when omitted the runner resolves the URL
from its existing MCP config at dispatch time.

name.description: MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.

url.description:
Optional URL override; falls back to the runner's MCP config when
`None`.

McpConnectionRef.title: McpConnectionRef

HttpConfig.description:
HTTP-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `headers` typically carries an `Authorization` token.
See module-level docs.

headers.description:
HTTP headers to include on every request. **Secret surface** —
typically includes `Authorization: Bearer …`.

url.description:
Server URL (e.g. `"http://localhost:8080/mcp"`). The runner appends
`/tools/list` and `/tools/call` to this base.

McpTransport.description:
Transport type for an MCP server connection.

Serialized as lowercase to match the existing DB column values
(`"stdio"` / `"http"`). Defaults to `Stdio` to preserve the pre-extraction
default from the runner.

oneOf.description:
Stdio subprocess transport — launches the configured command and
speaks newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout.
HTTP transport — POSTs JSON-RPC 2.0 to the configured URL.

StdioConfig.description:
Stdio-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `command`, `args`, and `env` must be treated as
execution-critical. See module-level docs.

cwd.description: Working directory (absolute path). `None` inherits the runner's cwd.

env.description:
Extra environment variables for the subprocess. **Secret surface** —
frequently holds API tokens.

McpServerConfig.description:
Full configuration for a registered MCP server.

Persisted in the `mcp_servers` Postgres table and surfaced to the frontend
through the `mcp_*` Tauri commands and the MCP `mcp-servers` HTTP
endpoint. The `transport` field selects which of `stdio_config` /
`http_config` is meaningful; the other is expected to be `None`.

**Secret surface**: the nested `stdio_config` / `http_config` carry secrets
— see their own docs and the module-level security note.

autoStart.description: Auto-connect when the runner launches. Default: `false`.

cachedTools.description:
Serialized JSON list of tools cached from the last successful
connection. Stored as a string for DB portability.

enabled.description:
Whether this server is enabled. Disabled servers won't be connected
even if `auto_start` is true. Default: `true`.

httpConfig.description:
HTTP-transport settings. Expected to be `Some` iff
`transport == McpTransport::Http`.

stdioConfig.description:
Stdio-transport settings. Expected to be `Some` iff
`transport == McpTransport::Stdio`.

timeoutSeconds.description: Per-request connection / tool-call timeout in seconds. Default: `30`.

toolsCachedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when `cached_tools` was populated.

McpServerConfig.title: McpServerConfig

McpToolInfo.description: Single tool exposed by an MCP server, as returned by `tools/list`.

inputSchema.description: Input-argument schema. Wire field is `"inputSchema"` per MCP spec.

name.description: Tool name (the argument passed back on `tools/call`).

McpToolInputSchema.description:
Input-parameter schema for an MCP tool.

Subset of JSON Schema — enough to render a form and validate arguments
before dispatching a `tools/call`. The `properties` and `required` fields
are passed through verbatim from the server.

properties.description: JSON-Schema-shaped property descriptors, kept as opaque JSON.

required.description: Names of required properties.

McpServerStatus.description:
Status of a single MCP server, as reported by the client manager.

Derived from runtime connection state; never persisted. `tools` is
populated only when `connected == true`.

connected.description: Whether the client currently holds a live connection.

error.description: Last connection / tool-call error, if any.

lastConnectAttempt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent connection attempt.

lastConnected.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent successful connection.

serverId.description: ID of the server this status refers to.

McpServerStatus.title: McpServerStatus

McpToolCallResult.description:
Result of a single `tools/call` invocation.

Shape is always the same regardless of success / failure: check `success`
first, then read `content` (on success) or `error` (on failure).

content.description:
Response content (present on success). Usually a JSON object/array,
but can be a primitive if the tool returned text-only content.

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration of the call in milliseconds.

error.description: Error message (present on failure).

McpToolCallResult.title: McpToolCallResult

McpToolInputSchema.description:
Input-parameter schema for an MCP tool.

Subset of JSON Schema — enough to render a form and validate arguments
before dispatching a `tools/call`. The `properties` and `required` fields
are passed through verbatim from the server.

properties.description: JSON-Schema-shaped property descriptors, kept as opaque JSON.

required.description: Names of required properties.

McpToolInfo.description: Single tool exposed by an MCP server, as returned by `tools/list`.

inputSchema.description: Input-argument schema. Wire field is `"inputSchema"` per MCP spec.

name.description: Tool name (the argument passed back on `tools/call`).

McpToolInfo.title: McpToolInfo

properties.description: JSON-Schema-shaped property descriptors, kept as opaque JSON.

required.description: Names of required properties.

Serialized as lowercase to match the existing DB column values

MemorySummary.description:
Lighter-weight projection for `/coord/memory/list` — strips the
(potentially-large) `content` blob so a list-all-memories scan stays
cheap. Fetch full payload via `GET /coord/memory/:name`.

MemoryListResponse.description:
`GET /coord/memory/list` response envelope. Wrapped in a struct
(rather than a bare `Vec<MemorySummary>`) so the generated TS / Python
schemas get a named exported interface rather than an anonymous array
alias.
MemoryListResponse.title: MemoryListResponse

MemoryRow.description:
One row in `coord.memories`. Returned by `GET /coord/memory/:name`
(as the `latest` field of `MemoryWithHistory` + each `history` entry)
and by `GET /coord/memory/:name/version/:version`.
MemoryRow.title: MemoryRow

MemorySummary.description:
Lighter-weight projection for `/coord/memory/list` — strips the
(potentially-large) `content` blob so a list-all-memories scan stays
cheap. Fetch full payload via `GET /coord/memory/:name`.
MemorySummary.title: MemorySummary

MemoryUpsertRequest.description:
`POST /coord/memory/upsert` body. `name` + `content` are the only
required fields; the rest is optional metadata.

UUID fields (`written_by_agent`, `written_by_device`) are `String` on
the wire; coord parses them with `Uuid::parse_str` and returns 400
on parse failure.
MemoryUpsertRequest.title: MemoryUpsertRequest

MemoryRow.description:
One row in `coord.memories`. Returned by `GET /coord/memory/:name`
(as the `latest` field of `MemoryWithHistory` + each `history` entry)
and by `GET /coord/memory/:name/version/:version`.

MemoryWithHistory.description:
`GET /coord/memory/:name` response — latest row + the 10
most-recent versions (DESC). The first entry of `history` is the
same row as `latest`; subsequent entries are prior versions
(including tombstones, so the dashboard can render "deleted at v3
then restored at v4").

`latest` is flattened on the wire — the response object's top-level
fields are the `MemoryRow` columns plus a `history` array.
MemoryWithHistory.title: MemoryWithHistory

MissReason.description:
Coarse-grained classification of an assertion miss.

Per §5.3 — locked as a unit enum. Structured detail (per-field diffs,
near-match candidates) lives on `AssertionMiss` / `CandidateMiss`.

oneOf.description: No bridge element scored above the match threshold.
Best candidate's ARIA role differed from the expected role.
Best candidate's text differed from the expected text.
Best candidate was found but not visible.
Best candidate had a differing attribute (data-*, aria-*, etc.).

Multiple elements matched equally well; assertion expected a
unique target.

MissReason.title: MissReason

ModelCostBreakdown.description: Cost breakdown for a single LLM model.

actionCount.description: Number of actions that used this model.

costUsd.description: Total cost in USD for this model.

tokensInput.description: Total input tokens for this model.

tokensOutput.description: Total output tokens for this model.

ModelCostBreakdown.title: ModelCostBreakdown

RoutingRule.description:
A conditional routing rule that selects model/provider based on runtime
context.

Rules are evaluated in order; the first matching rule wins. Condition
syntax: `"<variable> <op> <value>"` where:
- Variables: `verification_failures`, `iteration`, `stage_index`
- Operators: `>=`, `>`, `<=`, `<`, `==`, `!=`

maxTokens.description: Max tokens override when this rule matches.

model.description: Model to use when this rule matches.

provider.description: Provider to use when this rule matches.

temperature.description: Temperature override when this rule matches.

ModelOverrideConfig.description:
Per-phase model override configuration.

Each phase can independently specify a provider and/or model, along with
optional temperature, max_tokens, fallback config, and conditional routing
rules.

fallbackModel.description: Fallback model if the primary fails with a retryable error.

fallbackProvider.description: Fallback provider if the primary fails with a retryable error.

maxTokens.description: Max output tokens override for this phase.

routingRules.description:
Conditional routing rules evaluated at runtime. First matching rule
wins; unmatched falls back to this config's static fields.

ModelOverrideConfig.title: ModelOverrideConfig

MonitorPosition.description:
Spatial position of a monitor in a left-to-right layout, derived from the
X coordinate. Used for human-friendly display; use `index` for
programmatic operations.

Monitor.description:
Standardized monitor information — a physical display with its position
in the virtual desktop and metadata for UI display.

height.description: Monitor height in pixels.

isPrimary.description: Whether this is the primary/main monitor.

position.description: Spatial position based on X coordinate (for UI display).

width.description: Monitor width in pixels.

x.description: X position in absolute screen coordinates (can be negative).

y.description: Y position in absolute screen coordinates (can be negative).

Monitor.title: Monitor

MonitorPosition.description:
Spatial position of a monitor in a left-to-right layout, derived from the
X coordinate. Used for human-friendly display; use `index` for
programmatic operations.
MonitorPosition.title: MonitorPosition

MouseButton.description:
Mouse button for click actions.

Serialized as a bare lowercase string.
MouseButton.title: MouseButton

oneOf.description: Restart the target runner between iterations.

rebuild.description: Whether to also rebuild the target before restarting.

oneOf.description: Only restart if the workflow signaled a restart is needed.

rebuild.description: Whether to also rebuild when a signal triggers the restart.

oneOf.description: Wait for the target runner to be healthy (no restart).

context.description: Additional free-form context to pass to the generator.

contextIds.description: IDs of stored `Context` records to include.

description.description: Human description of the desired workflow.

DecomposerConfig.description:
Configuration for the task-decomposition subsystem.

When enabled, a single high-level task may be split into a plan of
sub-tasks executed in sequence within the loop.

modelOverride.description: Override the default AI model used for planning.

DiagnosePhaseConfig.description:
Configuration for the diagnostic-evaluation phase.

Captures UI state after workflow execution and classifies failure root
causes.

assertions.description:
Assertions to run against the UI after workflow execution.
Each assertion is a JSON object passed to
`POST /ui-bridge/control/assert`.

captureSnapshot.description: Whether to capture a full DOM snapshot for AI triage context.

modelOverride.description: Model override for the triage AI call. If `None`, uses default routing.

snapshotMaxChars.description:
Maximum characters to include from the snapshot in the AI triage
prompt.

ExitStrategy.description: How to evaluate whether the loop should exit.

oneOf.description: Exit when reflection finds 0 new fixes.
Exit when workflow verification passes on the first iteration.

Exit when diagnostic evaluation passes (all assertions pass + healthy
page).

ImplementFixesConfig.description: Configuration for the implement-fixes phase (Claude CLI).

additionalContext.description: Additional context to include in the fix prompt.

model.description:
Model to use (e.g., `"claude-opus-4-6"`). Defaults to `claude-opus-4-6`
when unset.

timeoutSecs.description: Timeout in seconds for the fix agent. Defaults to 600 when unset.

MultiLoopEntry.description: A single entry in a multi-loop configuration.

label.description: Human label (e.g., `"Pages 1–10"` or an app section name).

loopId.description: Unique identifier for this loop instance.

OrchestrationLoopConfig.description:
Full configuration for an orchestration loop run.

Covers the simple mode (fixed `workflow_id`, `max_iterations`, exit
strategy) as well as pipeline mode (build / diagnose / reflect / fix
phases) when `pipeline` is populated.

exitStrategy.description: How to decide when to stop.

maxIterations.description:
Maximum number of iterations.
`None` (omitted) means no cap — loop until success or explicit stop.

pipeline.description:
Pipeline-mode configuration. When present, enables build / reflect /
fix phases.

retryOnFailure.description:
When `true`, a failed workflow iteration doesn't count as a terminal
failure. Instead, the loop waits for the fixer workflow to complete,
then re-runs. The loop only exits on success or `max_iterations`.

targetRunnerId.description:
Target runner ID (for supervisor restart calls). If `None`, uses
`"primary"`.

targetRunnerPort.description:
Target runner port to execute workflows on.
If `None`, targets self (this runner's own port).

waitForFixer.description:
Whether to wait for the fixer workflow before starting the next
iteration. Only applies when `retry_on_failure` is `true`.

workflowId.description:
Workflow ID to execute each iteration.
Required for simple mode; optional in pipeline mode if the build phase
is configured.

PipelineConfig.description:
Pipeline-mode configuration for the build → execute → diagnose → reflect →
fix cycle.

build.description:
Generate the workflow from a description (optional — if absent, the
top-level `workflow_id` is used).

diagnose.description:
Diagnostic-evaluation phase — runs after Execute, before Reflect.
Captures UI state via UI Bridge and classifies failure root causes.

implementFixes.description: Implement fixes via Claude CLI after reflection finds issues.

StallDetectorConfig.description:
Configuration for the stall-detection subsystem.

Controls how the loop engine decides a run is stuck in a repeated-action /
oscillation / runaway-step pattern and forces an exit.

maxRepeatedActions.description: Maximum times the same action may repeat before stall is declared.

maxTotalSteps.description: Absolute ceiling on total steps across all iterations.

oscillationWindow.description: Window (in actions) used to detect oscillation between two states.

stallTimeoutSecs.description: Wall-clock seconds without progress before stall is declared.

SummarizationConfig.description:
Configuration for the context-summarization subsystem.

When the loop's rolling context approaches the token budget, older
iterations are compressed into a summary to keep the prompt small.

maxTokensBudget.description: Target maximum tokens for the full loop context.

preserveLastNIterations.description: Number of most-recent iterations to keep verbatim (never summarized).

summaryMaxTokens.description: Cap on tokens emitted by a single summarization pass.

tokenThresholdPct.description: Fraction of the token budget (0.0–1.0) at which summarization triggers.

MultiLoopConfig.description: Configuration for launching multiple loops at once.

loops.description: Individual loop configurations, each targeting a different runner.

stopAllOnError.description: Stop all loops if any single loop errors out.

MultiLoopConfig.title: MultiLoopConfig

oneOf.description: Restart the target runner between iterations.

rebuild.description: Whether to also rebuild the target before restarting.

oneOf.description: Only restart if the workflow signaled a restart is needed.

rebuild.description: Whether to also rebuild when a signal triggers the restart.

oneOf.description: Wait for the target runner to be healthy (no restart).

context.description: Additional free-form context to pass to the generator.

contextIds.description: IDs of stored `Context` records to include.

description.description: Human description of the desired workflow.

oscillationWindow.description: Window (in actions) used to detect oscillation between two states.

stallTimeoutSecs.description: Wall-clock seconds without progress before stall is declared.

tokenThresholdPct.description: Fraction of the token budget (0.0–1.0) at which summarization triggers.

DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

NavigationResult.description:
Result of navigating to one or more target states.

Navigation uses multistate pathfinding to determine the optimal path.
Nested `results` allow a single top-level navigation to fan out to several
targets and report per-target outcomes.

active_states.description: States active after the navigation completed.

error.description: Error message when navigation failed.

path.description: Ordered list of state IDs visited.

target_state.description: Target state for this navigation branch (if applicable).

description.description: Why this constraint exists (shown to the AI on violation).

enabled.description: Whether this constraint is enabled. Default: true.

ConstraintCheck.description:
What to check and how.

Internally tagged by the `type` field. Variants correspond to the four
check kinds implemented by the runner's constraint engine.

oneOf.description:
Grep modified files for a regex pattern.
Violation if the pattern IS found (use for secrets, debug statements, etc.)

file_glob.description:
Optional glob to limit which modified files are checked.
Default: all modified files.

oneOf.description:
Grep modified files for a regex pattern.
Violation if the pattern is NOT found (use for required headers, licenses, etc.)

file_glob.description: Optional glob to limit which modified files are checked.

pattern.description: Regex pattern that must be present.

oneOf.description:
Check that all modified files are within allowed paths.
Violation if any modified file is outside the allowed directories.

allowed_paths.description:
Allowed directory prefixes (relative to project root).
e.g., `["src/", "tests/", "config/"]`.

oneOf.description:
Run a shell command. Violation if exit code is non-zero.
Useful for quick compilation checks, linting, etc.

cwd.description: Working directory (relative to project root). Default: project root.

ConstraintSeverity.description:
Severity of a constraint violation.

- `Block`: Reject the fix, inject violation context, re-run agentic phase
  without consuming an iteration. After `max_retries`, consume the iteration.
- `Warn`: Apply the fix, but inject violation context for the next iteration.
- `Log`: Record only, don't affect execution.

oneOf.description:
Reject the fix and re-run the agentic phase with injected violation
context. After `max_retries`, the iteration is consumed.
Apply the fix, but inject violation context for the next iteration.
Record the violation only; do not affect execution.

NewConstraintProposal.description:
A new constraint proposed by the AI during an agentic phase.

Serialized with `"type": "new_constraint"` via the `ConstraintProposal`
enum's internal tag.
NewConstraintProposal.title: NewConstraintProposal

NextTaskInfo.description: Minimal description of the next task due to run.

nextRun.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the next scheduled run.

MatchLocation.description: Location of a pattern match on screen.

h.description: Height of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

w.description: Width of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

RuntimeData.description:
Runtime execution data captured during action execution.

Different fields are populated depending on the action type. All fields
are optional; the wire schema allows additional runtime fields via a
`flatten`-d map.

StateContext.description: State-machine context captured before/after an action.

TimingInfo.description:
Precise timing information for an event. Times are ISO 8601 strings (see
crate-level docs for the rationale — the types crate is wire-only and
doesn't depend on a chrono version).

endTime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. `None` while the event is still in flight.

TopMatch.description: A single match result with confidence and location.

NodeMetadata.description:
Metadata for a tree node — action configuration, runtime data, state
context, and timing. All fields are optional because different node
types populate different fields.

config.description: Action configuration (JSON object — shape varies by `ActionType`).

isExpandable.description: Whether this action can have child nodes.

isInline.description: Whether this action is displayed inline.

NodeMetadata.title: NodeMetadata

NodeStatus.description: Execution status of a tree node.

NodeType.description: Types of nodes in the execution tree.

OlConfig.description:
A saved orchestration-loop configuration preset.

Persisted in the runner's `orchestration_loop_configs` PostgreSQL table and
surfaced to the frontend through the `ol_list_configs` / `ol_get_config`
Tauri commands. The inner `config_json` is an `OrchestrationLoopConfig`
serialized as a JSON blob — stored as `Value` here to preserve forward
compatibility with older presets if the config schema grows new fields.

configJson.description: The full [`OrchestrationLoopConfig`] as a JSON blob.

isFavorite.description: Whether this preset is pinned as a favorite in the UI.

OlConfig.title: OlConfig

oneOf.description: Restart the target runner between iterations.

rebuild.description: Whether to also rebuild the target before restarting.

oneOf.description: Only restart if the workflow signaled a restart is needed.

rebuild.description: Whether to also rebuild when a signal triggers the restart.

oneOf.description: Wait for the target runner to be healthy (no restart).

context.description: Additional free-form context to pass to the generator.

contextIds.description: IDs of stored `Context` records to include.

description.description: Human description of the desired workflow.

DecomposerConfig.description:
Configuration for the task-decomposition subsystem.

When enabled, a single high-level task may be split into a plan of
sub-tasks executed in sequence within the loop.

modelOverride.description: Override the default AI model used for planning.

DiagnosePhaseConfig.description:
Configuration for the diagnostic-evaluation phase.

Captures UI state after workflow execution and classifies failure root
causes.

assertions.description:
Assertions to run against the UI after workflow execution.
Each assertion is a JSON object passed to
`POST /ui-bridge/control/assert`.

modelOverride.description: Model override for the triage AI call. If `None`, uses default routing.

snapshotMaxChars.description:
Maximum characters to include from the snapshot in the AI triage
prompt.

ExitStrategy.description: How to evaluate whether the loop should exit.

oneOf.description: Exit when reflection finds 0 new fixes.
Exit when workflow verification passes on the first iteration.

Exit when diagnostic evaluation passes (all assertions pass + healthy
page).

additionalContext.description: Additional context to include in the fix prompt.

model.description:
Model to use (e.g., `"claude-opus-4-6"`). Defaults to `claude-opus-4-6`
when unset.

timeoutSecs.description: Timeout in seconds for the fix agent. Defaults to 600 when unset.

PipelineConfig.description:
Pipeline-mode configuration for the build → execute → diagnose → reflect →
fix cycle.

build.description:
Generate the workflow from a description (optional — if absent, the
top-level `workflow_id` is used).

diagnose.description:
Diagnostic-evaluation phase — runs after Execute, before Reflect.
Captures UI state via UI Bridge and classifies failure root causes.

implementFixes.description: Implement fixes via Claude CLI after reflection finds issues.

StallDetectorConfig.description:
Configuration for the stall-detection subsystem.

Controls how the loop engine decides a run is stuck in a repeated-action /
oscillation / runaway-step pattern and forces an exit.

maxRepeatedActions.description: Maximum times the same action may repeat before stall is declared.

maxTotalSteps.description: Absolute ceiling on total steps across all iterations.

oscillationWindow.description: Window (in actions) used to detect oscillation between two states.

stallTimeoutSecs.description: Wall-clock seconds without progress before stall is declared.

SummarizationConfig.description:
Configuration for the context-summarization subsystem.

When the loop's rolling context approaches the token budget, older
iterations are compressed into a summary to keep the prompt small.

maxTokensBudget.description: Target maximum tokens for the full loop context.

preserveLastNIterations.description: Number of most-recent iterations to keep verbatim (never summarized).

summaryMaxTokens.description: Cap on tokens emitted by a single summarization pass.

tokenThresholdPct.description: Fraction of the token budget (0.0–1.0) at which summarization triggers.

OrchestrationLoopConfig.description:
Full configuration for an orchestration loop run.

Covers the simple mode (fixed `workflow_id`, `max_iterations`, exit
strategy) as well as pipeline mode (build / diagnose / reflect / fix
phases) when `pipeline` is populated.

exitStrategy.description: How to decide when to stop.

maxIterations.description:
Maximum number of iterations.
`None` (omitted) means no cap — loop until success or explicit stop.

pipeline.description:
Pipeline-mode configuration. When present, enables build / reflect /
fix phases.

retryOnFailure.description:
When `true`, a failed workflow iteration doesn't count as a terminal
failure. Instead, the loop waits for the fixer workflow to complete,
then re-runs. The loop only exits on success or `max_iterations`.

targetRunnerId.description:
Target runner ID (for supervisor restart calls). If `None`, uses
`"primary"`.

targetRunnerPort.description:
Target runner port to execute workflows on.
If `None`, targets self (this runner's own port).

waitForFixer.description:
Whether to wait for the fixer workflow before starting the next
iteration. Only applies when `retry_on_failure` is `true`.

workflowId.description:
Workflow ID to execute each iteration.
Required for simple mode; optional in pipeline mode if the build phase
is configured.

OrchestrationLoopConfig.title: OrchestrationLoopConfig

AssertionScope.description:
Filter narrowing which assertions / states a policy conjunct applies
to. Empty fields (default) mean "everything"; `assertion_ids` uses an
explicit `Option` to distinguish "everything" (`None`) from "none"
(`Some([])`).

assertionIds.description:
Restrict to a specific set of assertion IDs. `None` = all
assertions; `Some([])` = no assertions (a deliberately empty
scope — useful for testing policies).

categories.description: Restrict to these category values. Empty = all categories.

groups.description: Restrict to these group identifiers. Empty = all groups.

severities.description: Restrict to these severity values. Empty = all severities.

states.description: Restrict to these state IDs. Empty = all states.

ConjunctRule.description:
Internally-tagged on `kind`. AND-conjuncts only per §5.7 — no OR.
Unknown variants are rejected at the wire.

oneOf.description: All assertions in scope must pass.
At most `count` assertions in scope may fail.

count.description: Maximum allowed failures.

oneOf.description:
Fraction of failures (failed / total in scope) must be strictly
less than `rate`.
For every state in scope, its `match_rate` must be at least `rate`.
At least one state in scope must have `match_rate` at least `rate`.

Top-level `match_outcome` must be at least the given outcome,
ordered `NoMatch < PartialMatch < FullMatch`.
All critical assertions passed across all states.
Some critical assertions failed; some passed.

PolicyConjunct.description: One AND-conjunct: a named rule applied within a scope.

scope.description:
Filter narrowing which assertions / states this conjunct applies
to.

PolicyConjunct.title: PolicyConjunct

evidence.description:
Free-form human-readable explanation (e.g.
`"3 of 17 assertions failed (max 5)"`).

PolicyStatus.description: Pass / fail / indeterminate verdict for a policy or conjunct.

oneOf.description: Could not be evaluated (e.g. empty scope, missing inputs).

PolicyEvaluation.description:
Top-level wrapper for evaluating a `SpecCheckPolicy` against a
`SpecCheckResult`.

conjunctResults.description:
One result per conjunct in the policy, in policy declaration
order.

overallStatus.description:
Aggregate of all conjunct statuses — fails if any conjunct fails;
indeterminate if any conjunct is indeterminate and none failed.

PolicyEvaluation.title: PolicyEvaluation

PolicyStatus.description: Pass / fail / indeterminate verdict for a policy or conjunct.

oneOf.description: Could not be evaluated (e.g. empty scope, missing inputs).

PolicyStatus.title: PolicyStatus

`"javascript"` but the serde `alias = "java_script"` is preserved for
backwards compatibility with any settings that were written before the

ProcessConfig.description:
Configuration for a managed process.

Persisted in `settings.json` under `managed_processes`. Surfaced to the
frontend through the `get_process_configs` command and to MCP consumers
through the `processes` endpoint.

buildCommand.description: Build command to run before restarting (e.g., "cargo", "npm").

devOnly.description:
Whether this is a dev-mode-only service (not started in production
builds).

ignorePatterns.description:
Regex patterns for errors to ignore (matched against error message and
raw entry).

rebuildEnabled.description: Whether rebuild and AI fix features are enabled for this process.

startGroup.description:
Startup group for ordered startup (lower groups start first, default 0).
Processes in the same group start together. The runner waits for health
ports in each group to be ready before starting the next group.

ProcessConfig.title: ProcessConfig

ProcessState.description:
State of a managed process.

Ordering mirrors the lifecycle progression:
`Stopped → Starting → (Building) → Running → Healthy → Stopping → Stopped`,
or to `Failed` on any abnormal exit.
`ExternallyOwned` is a special out-of-band state meaning "a process is
running on our port, but this runner did not spawn it" — used by the
process-reconcile feature to surface port squatters without touching the
normal lifecycle.

oneOf.description:
A process is running on our configured port but was not spawned by this
runner. The runner will not attempt to stop or restart it.

ProcessState.title: ProcessState

ProcessState.description:
State of a managed process.

Ordering mirrors the lifecycle progression:
`Stopped → Starting → (Building) → Running → Healthy → Stopping → Stopped`,
or to `Failed` on any abnormal exit.
`ExternallyOwned` is a special out-of-band state meaning "a process is
running on our port, but this runner did not spawn it" — used by the
process-reconcile feature to surface port squatters without touching the
normal lifecycle.

oneOf.description:
A process is running on our configured port but was not spawned by this
runner. The runner will not attempt to stop or restart it.

ProcessStatus.description:
Status summary of a managed process.

Derived from runtime state each time it is requested; never persisted.

errorCount.description: Number of errors detected from this process

hasBuildCommand.description: Whether this process has a build command configured

portHealthy.description: Whether the health port is responding

restartCount.description: Number of times this process has been restarted

uptimeSecs.description: Uptime in seconds (None if not running)

ProcessStatus.title: ProcessStatus

PromptStepPhase.description:
Phases in which a [`PromptStep`] may appear.

Prompt steps are the only variant that may appear in the agentic phase.

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

VerificationCategoryKind.description:
Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

isSummaryStep.description: Marks this prompt as the summary step at the end of completion.

phase.description: Phase in which the step appears.

promptId.description: Saved prompt ID (when the body is a reference).

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

PromptStepPhase.description:
Phases in which a [`PromptStep`] may appear.

Prompt steps are the only variant that may appear in the agentic phase.
PromptStepPhase.title: PromptStepPhase

ProposalStatus.description:
Lifecycle status for spec proposals produced by the Stream E coverage-growth
flywheel. `None` on an existing `IrProvenance` means the spec predates the
flywheel and is implicitly `Promoted`.

RecommendationReviewDecisionPayload.description:
Payload shape for the `review-approved` and `review-rejected` Tauri events
emitted by `commands::productivity::approve_recommendation` /
`reject_recommendation` after a user resolves a medium-confidence
recommendation card. Field names are explicit camelCase to match the
`serde_json::json!()` literal previously used at the emit site.

Single struct shared by both channels: only `user_decision` differs
(`"approved"` vs `"rejected"`), so a tagged enum would inflate the wire
format without payoff.
RecommendationReviewDecisionPayload.title: RecommendationReviewDecisionPayload

Confidence.description:
Confidence level — high / medium / low.

Collides with `qontinui_types::verification::Confidence` in the
cross-repo schema registry; registered as `SpecCheckConfidence` in
`schema_export.rs`. See module docs.

oneOf.description: Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.

Confidence.title: SpecCheckConfidence

RecommendedState.description: The single state the matcher recommends the caller treat as "current".

confidence.description: Matcher confidence in the recommendation.

reason.description: Free-form explanation (e.g. "highest match rate among all states").

RecommendedState.title: RecommendedState

RecordGitOpRequest.description:
`POST /coord/git-ops/record` body. `repo` + `op_kind` are the only
required fields; the rest is optional metadata.

`device_id` / `session_id` are NOT in this body — coord resolves the
tenant from the `X-Qontinui-Tenant-Id` header and reads device/session
from the request wrapper (mirroring the federation-report surface).

op_kind.description:
One of: `commit | push | checkout | branch_create | merge |
rebase | reset | stash | tag` (free-form on the wire).

repo.description:
Repo basename (origin remote URL basename, falling back to the
working-dir basename for remote-less clones).

RecordGitOpRequest.title: RecordGitOpRequest

ReflectionFixOutput.description: A reflection fix reported by the AI.

errorId.description: Identifier for the error being fixed.

CoordinateSystem.description:
Coordinate system identifier. Always specify which system you are working
with to avoid confusion in multi-monitor setups.

oneOf.description:
Absolute screen coordinates (OS-level). May be negative for monitors
left of or above the primary. Used by pyautogui, mouse ops, and
window positioning.

Relative to the virtual desktop origin `(min_x, min_y)`. Always
non-negative within the virtual desktop bounds. Used by screenshot
pixel lookups and FIND match results.

Relative to a specific monitor's top-left corner. Requires
`monitor_index`. Used by `searchRegion` in state configurations.

Region.description:
Rectangular region on screen. Like [`Coordinates`], can optionally specify
which coordinate system the `x`/`y` are in.

height.description: Height of the region (must be positive).

width.description: Width of the region (must be positive).

x.description: X coordinate of the top-left corner.

y.description: Y coordinate of the top-left corner.

Region.title: Region

Used in [`ResolvedInitialStates`] and [`ResolvedInitialStatesResult`] to
let the UI render human-readable lists without a separate lookup.

InitialStatesSource.description:
Source of initial states configuration.

- `Defaults`: states with `is_initial=true` in the state machine definition
- `Workflow`: initial states defined on the workflow (`initialStateIds`)
- `Override`: session-only override from the runner UI

Serialized as a bare lowercase string (`"defaults"` / `"workflow"` /
`"override"`) to match the TS literal union.

ResourceLimits.description:
Resource limits for workflow execution.

When a limit is approached (within the warning threshold), the tracker
emits context injection actions. When exceeded, it emits stronger actions.

maxFilesModified.description: Maximum number of unique files modified across all iterations.

maxWallTimeSecs.description: Maximum wall-clock time for the entire workflow (seconds).

warningThreshold.description:
Warning threshold as a fraction (0.0-1.0). When resource usage exceeds
this fraction of the limit, a warning is injected.
Default: 0.75 (warn at 75% of limit).

ResourceLimits.title: ResourceLimits

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

restartWaitForHealth.description: Whether to wait for health port after restart (default: `true`).

oneOf.description: The UI itself rendered incorrectly (wrong components, broken layout).
UI Bridge misread the page (element not found, wrong selector).

The workflow-generation prompt was unclear or incomplete.

RoutingRule.description:
A conditional routing rule that selects model/provider based on runtime
context.

Rules are evaluated in order; the first matching rule wins. Condition
syntax: `"<variable> <op> <value>"` where:
- Variables: `verification_failures`, `iteration`, `stage_index`
- Operators: `>=`, `>`, `<=`, `<`, `==`, `!=`

maxTokens.description: Max tokens override when this rule matches.

model.description: Model to use when this rule matches.

provider.description: Provider to use when this rule matches.

temperature.description: Temperature override when this rule matches.

RoutingRule.title: RoutingRule

context.description: Optional free-form context string appended to the prompt.

displayPrompt.description: Display-only version of the prompt (shown in the UI).

maxTraceScreenshots.description: Cap on trace screenshots to include.

maxVideoFrames.description: Cap on video frames to extract for the prompt.

tracePath.description: Optional path to a trace file.

data.description: Structured data payload from a synchronous prompt run.

error.description: Error message if the call failed.

logFile.description: Path to the log file for the session.

output.description: Immediate output if the call ran synchronously.

pid.description: OS process ID of the spawned AI session, if any.

sessionId.description: ID of the created AI session, if any.

stateFile.description: Path to the state file tracking the session.

success.description: Whether the prompt was accepted and started successfully.

taskRunId.description: ID of the created task run, if any.

RunStatus.description: Lifecycle status of an execution run.

oneOf.description: The run has been created but has not started yet.
The run finished with an error.
The run exceeded its configured time limit.
The run was cancelled before natural completion.
The run is paused and can be resumed.

RunnerCrash.description: Most recent crash dump metadata, if any.

filePath.description: Path to the crash dump file on disk.

panicLocation.description: Source location (file/line) where the panic originated.

panicMessage.description: Panic message captured from the runner process.

reportedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the crash was reported.

thread.description: Name of the thread that panicked.

RunnerStatus.description:
Health/availability state of a runner, computed server-side from the
WebSocket-presence and heartbeat-freshness signals.

oneOf.description: WS connected OR heartbeat fresh — fully reachable.
Heartbeat is stale but not yet cold; runner may still be reachable.
No WS connection and heartbeat is cold.
Just registered; waiting for first heartbeat.

RunnerUiError.description: Structured error reported by the runner that the UI surfaces verbatim.

detail.description: Optional long-form detail (stack trace, stderr, etc.).

reportedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the error was reported.

Runner.description:
Canonical runner entity. One row per registered runner; the same shape is
served to every consumer (mobile, web, runner UI).

Replaces the prior split between `runners` (fleet/heartbeat),
`runner_connections` (transient WS sessions), and per-consumer projection
types like `RunnerConnection` / `ServerRunner` / `RegisteredRunner` /
`WebIntegrationStatus`.

capabilities.description:
Free-form capability tags advertised by the runner
(e.g., `["python", "playwright", "vision"]`).

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the runner was first registered.

derivedStatus.description:
Computed health state derived from `ws_connected` and
`last_heartbeat`. Authoritative for UI display.

hostname.description: Reported hostname of the machine the runner is running on.

ipAddress.description: Reported network address the runner is reachable on.

lastHeartbeat.description:
ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent heartbeat received from the
runner, if any.

port.description: Reported port the runner's HTTP API is listening on.

recentCrash.description: Most recently captured crash dump metadata, if any.

uiError.description: Most recently reported structured UI error, if any.

userId.description: Owning user identifier (UUID as a string).

wsConnected.description: Whether the server currently holds an open WebSocket from the runner.

Runner.title: Runner

RunnerConnectedConnection.description:
Wire-format runner shape carried inside `runner_connected.connection`.

This is intentionally a partial Runner payload (snake_case, only the
fields the Python emitter actually populates in
`RunnerEventPublisher.publish_runner_connected`). It is NOT
`qontinui_types::runner::Runner` — that shape requires `derived_status`,
`capabilities`, `created_at` etc., which the connection payload doesn't
include. Consumers reading this should refetch the canonical Runner row
via REST after seeing `runner_connected`.

disconnected_at.description:
Always present as `null` on the wire when the runner is still
connected. Python ships this field unconditionally.

RunnerConnectedConnection.title: RunnerConnectedConnection

RunnerCrash.description: Most recent crash dump metadata, if any.

filePath.description: Path to the crash dump file on disk.

panicLocation.description: Source location (file/line) where the panic originated.

panicMessage.description: Panic message captured from the runner process.

reportedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the crash was reported.

thread.description: Name of the thread that panicked.

RunnerCrash.title: RunnerCrash

RunnerFinding.description:
A finding detected by AI analysis (runner-local wire shape).

Wire format: serialized via `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]` so all
snake_case Rust field names ship as camelCase on the Tauri event channels
`finding_detected` and `finding_resolved`. The frontend listener in
`services/TauriFindingsListener.ts` MUST read these fields by their
camelCase names — reading snake_case silently evaluates to `undefined`.

Renamed in the schema registry to `RunnerFinding` to disambiguate from
`qontinui_types::verification::Finding`, which has a different shape
(`confidence`, `findingType`, `evidence` vs this struct's flat fields).
RunnerFinding.title: RunnerFinding

RunnerFindingCodeContext.description:
Code context for a finding (runner-local wire shape).

Renamed in the schema registry to `RunnerFindingCodeContext` to
disambiguate from `qontinui_types::findings::FindingCodeContext`.

User input request for a finding (runner-local wire shape).

RunnerFindingUserInput.title: RunnerFindingUserInput

RunnerMetadata.description: Information about the runner environment that produced the run.

runnerVersion.description: Semantic version of the runner binary.

screenResolution.description: Screen resolution as a free-form string (e.g., `"1920x1080"`).

RunnerMetadata.title: RunnerMetadata

RunnerRelayMessage.description:
Discriminated union of every WS envelope `mcp/backend_relay.rs::handle_outbound`
ships to the backend after rewrapping a Tauri-side `AppEvent`.

Internally tagged on `"type"` with snake_case variant names because that's
what the prior `serde_json::json!` literals emitted on the wire.

Wire keys are snake_case (NOT camelCase) — verified against the literal
strings in `handle_outbound` lines ~605-638. Consumers that expect
camelCase (e.g. some web hooks) are wrong; the relay never emitted
camelCase here.

oneOf.description:
Phase completion result. Emitted from the `phase-result` broadcast
channel; `data` carries the original `AppEvent` payload
(`{ execution_id, result }`).

UI error reported by the runner. Emitted from the `ui-error` broadcast
channel; `data` carries the original `AppEvent` payload (matches
`qontinui_types::runner::RunnerUiError`'s wire shape). Always present
(serializes as `null` when absent) to match the legacy `json!` literal.

Most-recent crash dump metadata. Emitted from the `recent-crash`
broadcast channel; `data` carries the original `AppEvent` payload
(matches `qontinui_types::runner::RunnerCrash`'s wire shape).

AI session output forwarded from the `ai-output` broadcast channel
(mobile chat sessions). `data` carries the original `AiOutput`
`AppEvent` payload.

AI session lifecycle state from the `session-state` broadcast channel.
`data` carries the original `AppEvent` payload (state transitions for
the mobile chat UI).

PTY output from the `terminal-output` broadcast channel. The relay
pulls the inner `terminal_id` and `data` out of the AppEvent payload
and ships them at the envelope's top level (NOT under a `data` key).
`data` here is the base64-encoded PTY bytes — same shape as
`qontinui_types::terminal::TerminalOutputEvent.data`. Always present
(serialize as `null` when absent) to match legacy literal output.

PTY exit signal from the `terminal-exit` broadcast channel. As above,
the relay flattens `terminal_id` + `exit_code` to the envelope's top
level rather than nesting under a `data` field.

RunnerRelayMessage.title: RunnerRelayMessage

RunnerStatus.description:
Health/availability state of a runner, computed server-side from the
WebSocket-presence and heartbeat-freshness signals.

oneOf.description: WS connected OR heartbeat fresh — fully reachable.
Heartbeat is stale but not yet cold; runner may still be reachable.
No WS connection and heartbeat is cold.
Just registered; waiting for first heartbeat.

RunnerStatus.title: RunnerStatus

RunnerConnectedConnection.description:
Wire-format runner shape carried inside `runner_connected.connection`.

This is intentionally a partial Runner payload (snake_case, only the
fields the Python emitter actually populates in
`RunnerEventPublisher.publish_runner_connected`). It is NOT
`qontinui_types::runner::Runner` — that shape requires `derived_status`,
`capabilities`, `created_at` etc., which the connection payload doesn't
include. Consumers reading this should refetch the canonical Runner row
via REST after seeing `runner_connected`.

disconnected_at.description:
Always present as `null` on the wire when the runner is still
connected. Python ships this field unconditionally.

RunnerConnectedConnection.title: RunnerConnectedConnection

RunnerStatusEvent.description:
Discriminated union of every event the Python backend pushes to the web
client over the `/api/v1/runners/status` WebSocket.

Internally tagged on `"type"` matching the Python wire format. Variant
renames (`runner.woke` keeps the dot rather than going to snake_case)
preserve the literal strings the emitter sends.

Source of truth: the `publish_*` methods in
`qontinui-web/backend/app/services/runner/event_publisher.py` and the
`initial_state` send in `runner_status_ws.py`.

oneOf.description:
Snapshot pushed on WS connect. Each entry is a full canonical Runner
(as serialized by `qontinui_schemas.runner.Runner` Pydantic). Carried
here as `Value` because the canonical `Runner` lives in
`qontinui_types::runner::Runner`; the TS binding consumer should
narrow `runners[i] as Runner` after import.

A runner WS handshake completed. `connection` is the partial
connection payload; refetch via REST to hydrate to full Runner.

Wake-intent fulfilled — the WakeRunnerModal listens for this to
transition out of "waking" state.

Server-side error emitted by the WS endpoint (e.g. failed to load
initial state). Treated as a soft warning by clients; the connection
stays open.

RunnerStatusEvent.title: RunnerStatusEvent

reportedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the error was reported.

ScheduleExpression.description:
How a task should be scheduled.

Serialized with the external tag `type` and payload under `value` so that
`Once("...")`, `Cron("...")`, and `Interval(60)` round-trip as
`{ "type": "Once", "value": "..." }` etc. The `Condition` variant wraps a
[`ConditionScheduleConfig`] rather than a scalar, but uses the same
`{ type, value }` envelope.

oneOf.description: Run once at a specific datetime (ISO 8601).

Cron expression (e.g., `"0 9 * * *"` for 9 AM daily). The runner
accepts both 5-field and 6/7-field cron forms and normalizes internally.
Interval in seconds between runs (for testing/debugging).

Condition-only schedule: no time trigger, runs whenever the attached
[`ScheduleConditions`] on the task are met.

ScheduleExpression.title: ScheduleExpression

audit but do not execute.
Collapse multiple missed slots into a single catch-up run.

rearmDelayMinutes.description:
Minutes to wait after an execution completes before re-evaluating
conditions for another run.

idleMet.description:

waitingSince.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when conditions began being evaluated.
