[`ConditionScheduleConfig`] rather than a scalar, but uses the same
`{ type, value }` envelope.

oneOf.description: Run once at a specific datetime (ISO 8601).

Cron expression (e.g., `"0 9 * * *"` for 9 AM daily). The runner
accepts both 5-field and 6/7-field cron forms and normalizes internally.
Interval in seconds between runs (for testing/debugging).

Condition-only schedule: no time trigger, runs whenever the attached
[`ScheduleConditions`] on the task are met.

ScheduledTaskStatus.description: Status of a scheduled task execution.

oneOf.description: Finished with an error after the task had started running.

The task could not even start (workflow file missing, prompt resolve
failure, ai_session spawn error, etc.). Distinct from `Failed` because
it drives the launch-failure backoff in
[`ScheduledTask::launch_failure_backoff_seconds`].

Skipped because the task had already completed successfully and
`skip_if_completed` is `true`.

Slot detected as missed during catch-up reconciliation while the
runner was offline. Recorded as a history row when
[`CatchUpPolicy::Skip`] is in effect; never returned for currently
queued tasks.

ScheduledTaskType.description:
Type of task to schedule.

Internally tagged by `task_type`: the variant fields are inlined alongside
the discriminator rather than nested under a `value` key.

oneOf.description:
Run a workflow — either a legacy workflow-by-name from a loaded config
or a unified workflow by ID.

config_path.description: Optional path to a workflow config file.

workflow_id.description:
If set, run a unified workflow by ID instead of a legacy workflow
by name.

workflow_name.description: Display name (also used to look up legacy workflows).

oneOf.description: Run a prompt from the Prompt Library.

max_sessions.description:
Optional override for `max_sessions`. `None` uses the prompt's own
setting.

prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to run.

oneOf.description: Trigger auto-fix: check findings and fix any auto-fixable items.

check_findings.description: Whether to check the findings queue before running.

force_run.description: Force a run even if no findings are present.

oneOf.description:
Execute a watcher (screenpipe-inspired reactive AI agent). Queries the
activity timeline, reasons with AI, and triggers an action.

watcher_id.description: ID of the watcher definition in PostgreSQL.

oneOf.description:
Continuous background capture (screenpipe-style). Periodically
captures screen state and stores it in the activity timeline.

capture_on_focus_change.description: Whether to also trigger a capture on window focus change.

oneOf.description:
Run an ad-hoc Claude agent prompt without pre-registering it in the
Prompt Library. Reuses the runner's existing `POST /prompts/run`
ad-hoc surface — no separate Claude CLI plumbing required.

allowed_tools.description:
Allowed tool names (e.g. `["Bash", "Read", "Write", "Edit"]`).
Empty = use the runner's default tool allow-list.

max_turns.description:
Hard cap on Claude turns per run (safety bound). `None` =
runner's default cap (typically 50).

mcp_connections.description:
Optional MCP connection references resolved at dispatch time
against the runner's existing MCP config. Empty = inherit
whatever the runner currently has configured.

prompt.description: The prompt content to send to the Claude session.

timeout_seconds.description:
Wall-clock timeout in seconds. `None` = runner default
(typically 600s = 10 min).

working_directory.description:
Working directory for the spawned session. `None` means the
runner's project root. Stored as a string (not `PathBuf`) to keep
this crate JSON-Schema-friendly.

TaskExecutionRecord.description: Record of a single task execution.

autoFixSessionId.description: Session ID of the auto-fix session, if one was triggered.

catchUpRun.description:
Whether this execution record was created by the catch-up reconciler
(rather than the normal scheduling tick).

errorMessage.description: Error message if the execution failed.

executionId.description: Unique ID for this execution (UUID v4 string).

scheduledFor.description:
ISO 8601 timestamp of the *scheduled* slot this execution covers,
distinct from `started_at` (which is the actual launch time). Allows
detecting "ran X minutes late" and is consumed by the missed-run
reconciler. Optional for backward compatibility with execution rows
written before this field existed.

sessionId.description:
Session ID if this execution triggered an AI session, used for
downstream success tracking.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when execution started.

success.description: Whether the task succeeded, read from the session checkpoint.

triggeredAutoFix.description: Whether auto-fix was triggered after this execution.

ScheduledTask.description:
A scheduled task definition — the full persisted frame, including computed
fields (`last_run`, `next_run`) and condition-evaluation state
(`conditions`, `condition_status`).

autoFixOnFailure.description: Automatically trigger auto-fix when this task fails.

catchUpGraceSeconds.description:
Slots within this number of seconds of "now" are not treated as
missed (the normal scheduler will pick them up). Default 300s.

catchUpPolicy.description:
Catch-up policy applied when the runner restarts after missing one
or more scheduled slots.

conditionStatus.description: Present while the task is waiting for its conditions to be met.

conditions.description: Optional conditions that must be met before execution.

consecutiveLaunchFailures.description:
Number of consecutive launch failures since the task last started
successfully. Drives exponential backoff. Reset to 0 on the next
successful launch.

enabled.description: Whether the task is enabled and eligible to run.

lastRun.description: Record of the most recent execution.

launchFailureBackoffSeconds.description:
**Base** backoff in seconds for launch failures (not the current
accumulated backoff). The runner computes the effective delay as
`min(base * 2^(consecutive_launch_failures - 1), 86400)`.
Default 60s.

nextRun.description: Next scheduled run time (ISO 8601), computed by the runner.

skipIfCompleted.description: Skip future runs once the task has succeeded at least once.

successCriteria.description: Free-form description of success criteria, for human reference.

task.description: Task type and its per-type configuration.

ScheduledTask.title: ScheduledTask

ScheduledTaskStatus.description: Status of a scheduled task execution.

oneOf.description: Finished with an error after the task had started running.

The task could not even start (workflow file missing, prompt resolve
failure, ai_session spawn error, etc.). Distinct from `Failed` because
it drives the launch-failure backoff in
[`ScheduledTask::launch_failure_backoff_seconds`].

Skipped because the task had already completed successfully and
`skip_if_completed` is `true`.

Slot detected as missed during catch-up reconciliation while the
runner was offline. Recorded as a history row when
[`CatchUpPolicy::Skip`] is in effect; never returned for currently
queued tasks.

ScheduledTaskStatus.title: ScheduledTaskStatus

McpConnectionRef.description:
Lightweight reference to an MCP connection for [`ScheduledTaskType::RemoteAgent`].

`name` is the MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.
`url` is an optional override; when omitted the runner resolves the URL
from its existing MCP config at dispatch time.

name.description: MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.

url.description:
Optional URL override; falls back to the runner's MCP config when
`None`.

defaultAutoFixOnFailure.description: Default `auto_fix_on_failure` value for newly created tasks.

enabled.description: Whether the scheduler is enabled globally.

maxConcurrent.description: Maximum number of scheduled tasks allowed to run concurrently.

timezone.description: Timezone for schedule interpretation (IANA name). `None` = local time.

NextTaskInfo.description: Minimal description of the next task due to run.

nextRun.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the next scheduled run.

SchedulerStatus.description:
Summary of the scheduler's current runtime state, returned from the status
API.

nextTask.description: The next task scheduled to run, if any.

pendingTasks.description: Number of tasks scheduled but not yet running.

ScreenshotAnnotationShape.description: Kind of shape overlaid on a screenshot annotation.

ScreenshotAnnotation.description: Annotation overlaid on a screenshot (box, circle, arrow, or text).

height.description: Annotation height in pixels.

width.description: Annotation width in pixels.

x.description: X coordinate in pixels.

y.description: Y coordinate in pixels.

ScreenshotAnnotation.title: ScreenshotAnnotation

ScreenshotAnnotationShape.description: Kind of shape overlaid on a screenshot annotation.
ScreenshotAnnotationShape.title: ScreenshotAnnotationShape

oneOf.description: Captured as part of state verification.

ScrollDirection.description:
Scroll direction for the `scroll` action.

Serialized as a bare lowercase string.
ScrollDirection.title: ScrollDirection

CoordinateSystem.description:
Coordinate system identifier. Always specify which system you are working
with to avoid confusion in multi-monitor setups.

oneOf.description:
Absolute screen coordinates (OS-level). May be negative for monitors
left of or above the primary. Used by pyautogui, mouse ops, and
window positioning.

Relative to the virtual desktop origin `(min_x, min_y)`. Always
non-negative within the virtual desktop bounds. Used by screenshot
pixel lookups and FIND match results.

Relative to a specific monitor's top-left corner. Requires
`monitor_index`. Used by `searchRegion` in state configurations.

Coordinates.description:
X,Y coordinates with optional coordinate system specification.

Coordinates without an explicit `system` default to
[`CoordinateSystem::Screen`] for backward compatibility.

x.description: X coordinate (horizontal position).

y.description: Y coordinate (vertical position).

MatchAdjustment.description: Match adjustment — modify the matched region before acting.

MatchMethod.description: Template matching method used by OpenCV-style correlation search.

Region.description:
Rectangular region on screen. Like [`Coordinates`], can optionally specify
which coordinate system the `x`/`y` are in.

height.description: Height of the region (must be positive).

width.description: Width of the region (must be positive).

x.description: X coordinate of the top-left corner.

y.description: Y coordinate of the top-left corner.

SearchStrategy.description:
Strategy for selecting among multiple matches in a FIND action.

Variants serialize uppercase (`"FIRST"`, `"ALL"`, `"BEST"`, `"EACH"`) to
match the existing Python enum and all stored action configs.

has_text_embedding.description: Whether a text embedding vector is available.

image_url.description: Presigned URL for displaying the image.

SearchStrategy.description:
Strategy for selecting among multiple matches in a FIND action.

Variants serialize uppercase (`"FIRST"`, `"ALL"`, `"BEST"`, `"EACH"`) to
match the existing Python enum and all stored action configs.
SearchStrategy.title: SearchStrategy

min_similarity.description:
Minimum similarity threshold (0–1). CLIP text-to-image similarities
typically fall in 0.15–0.35, so 0.2 is a reasonable default.

has_text_embedding.description: Whether a text embedding vector is available.

image_url.description: Presigned URL for displaying the image.

SpecChanged.description:
Existing `POST /spec/author` change-broadcast payload. Pre-dates the
Plan 06 `SpecApiEvent` sum-type but keeps the same shape so external
SSE subscribers (the runner's `use-discovered-specs.ts`) keep working.

appId.description:
Owning app id (multi-tenant Spec API). Subscribers filter on this
to receive only their app's events. `#[serde(default)]` allows
legacy emitters (pre-multi-app) to omit the field; downstream
per-app routing treats empty as `qontinui-runner` for back-compat.

atMs.description: Epoch milliseconds the event was emitted at.

kind.description:
"ir-and-projection" today; future kinds may include
"projection-only" if a regen runs without an IR write.

SpecApiEvent.description:
Tagged sum-type for every event broadcast on the Spec API channel.

Serialization uses `serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "kebab-case")` so
the wire discriminator is exactly the SSE event name (e.g.
`"spec-changed"`, `"spec-check-invoked"`).

oneOf.description:
Existing — emitted by `POST /spec/author` after a successful IR +
projection write.

B — entry into `evaluate` / `evaluate_batch` / policy. Emitted by
`spec_api/spec_check.rs` HTTP handlers after `wrap_snapshot`
returns and before the evaluator runs.

B — completion roll-up. Payload joins via `snapshot_id`; no fat
result body in the broadcast.

B — workflow-step policy conjunct failed. One emit per failed
`ConjunctEvaluation`.

observed.description: Small scalar/struct — NOT the full result.

snapshot_id.description: The snapshot that produced the second green.

oneOf.description: Flywheel — proposal dropped back to the queue after a red B run.

SpecApiEvent.title: SpecApiEvent

SpecCheckConfidence.description:
Confidence level — high / medium / low.

Collides with `qontinui_types::verification::Confidence` in the
cross-repo schema registry; registered as `SpecCheckConfidence` in
`schema_export.rs`. See module docs.

oneOf.description: Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.

SpecCheckConfidence.title: SpecCheckConfidence

AssertionScope.description:
Filter narrowing which assertions / states a policy conjunct applies
to. Empty fields (default) mean "everything"; `assertion_ids` uses an
explicit `Option` to distinguish "everything" (`None`) from "none"
(`Some([])`).

assertionIds.description:
Restrict to a specific set of assertion IDs. `None` = all
assertions; `Some([])` = no assertions (a deliberately empty
scope — useful for testing policies).

categories.description: Restrict to these category values. Empty = all categories.

groups.description: Restrict to these group identifiers. Empty = all groups.

severities.description: Restrict to these severity values. Empty = all severities.

states.description: Restrict to these state IDs. Empty = all states.

ConjunctRule.description:
Internally-tagged on `kind`. AND-conjuncts only per §5.7 — no OR.
Unknown variants are rejected at the wire.

oneOf.description: All assertions in scope must pass.
At most `count` assertions in scope may fail.

count.description: Maximum allowed failures.

oneOf.description:
Fraction of failures (failed / total in scope) must be strictly
less than `rate`.
For every state in scope, its `match_rate` must be at least `rate`.
At least one state in scope must have `match_rate` at least `rate`.

Top-level `match_outcome` must be at least the given outcome,
ordered `NoMatch < PartialMatch < FullMatch`.
All critical assertions passed across all states.
Some critical assertions failed; some passed.

PolicyConjunct.description: One AND-conjunct: a named rule applied within a scope.

scope.description:
Filter narrowing which assertions / states this conjunct applies
to.

SpecCheckPolicy.description:
AND-only policy — all conjuncts must hold for the policy to pass.
Per §5.7 there is no OR; compose multiple steps if you need
disjunction.
SpecCheckPolicy.title: SpecCheckPolicy

AssertionMiss.description: Top-level miss diagnostic for a failed assertion.

candidates.description:
Top-N near-match candidates, capped externally. Ordered by score
descending.

reason.description: Coarse-grained classification of why the match failed.

AssertionOutcome.description:
Internally-tagged on `status` — `{ "status": "pass", "matched": {...} }`
or `{ "status": "fail", "miss": {...} }`. Distinct from the
worker-output `{ "type": ..., "data": ... }` envelope used elsewhere
in the codebase.

oneOf.description: Assertion passed against the named matched element.

matched.description: The element that satisfied the assertion.

oneOf.description: Assertion failed; the miss carries reason + near-match candidates.

severity.description:
Free-form severity string — `"critical"` / `"error"` / `"warning"`
/ `"info"`. Free-form so policy adapters can introduce new tiers
without bumping the schema.

AssertionSeverityCounts.description: Counts of assertion failures bucketed by severity.

error.description: Number of `error` failures.

BridgeFingerprint.description:
Fingerprint of the bridge snapshot's app/route/version context — §5.8 G3.

Apps may surface additional fingerprint fields in future; new fields
require a schema bump (`deny_unknown_fields` is set).

appVersion.description: Optional app version string (semver or git short-SHA).

elementCount.description: Number of elements visible in the snapshot.

route.description: Optional route path / hash at snapshot time.

snapshotTimestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the snapshot was indexed.

CandidateMiss.description:
A near-match candidate — an element that scored above the cut-off but
didn't fully match the assertion criteria.

fieldDiffs.description: Per-field diffs explaining why the candidate didn't fully match.

path.description: CSS selector or DOM path identifying the candidate.

Confidence.description:
Confidence level — high / medium / low.

Collides with `qontinui_types::verification::Confidence` in the
cross-repo schema registry; registered as `SpecCheckConfidence` in
`schema_export.rs`. See module docs.

oneOf.description: Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.

Confidence.title: SpecCheckConfidence

FieldDiff.description:
Per-field diff between an assertion's expected criteria and a
candidate element's actual value.

actual.description: Actual value from the bridge element.

expected.description: Expected value from the IR assertion.

oneOf.description: All critical assertions passed across all states.
Some critical assertions failed; some passed.

MatchedElement.description: Identity of the bridge element that satisfied an assertion.

path.description: CSS selector or DOM path identifying the element.

role.description: ARIA role of the matched element.

text.description: Visible text of the matched element.

MissReason.description:
Coarse-grained classification of an assertion miss.

Per §5.3 — locked as a unit enum. Structured detail (per-field diffs,
near-match candidates) lives on `AssertionMiss` / `CandidateMiss`.

oneOf.description: No bridge element scored above the match threshold.
Best candidate's ARIA role differed from the expected role.
Best candidate's text differed from the expected text.
Best candidate was found but not visible.
Best candidate had a differing attribute (data-*, aria-*, etc.).

Multiple elements matched equally well; assertion expected a
unique target.

RecommendedState.description: The single state the matcher recommends the caller treat as "current".

confidence.description: Matcher confidence in the recommendation.

reason.description: Free-form explanation (e.g. "highest match rate among all states").

SpecCheckSummary.description: Aggregate summary across all evaluated states.

overallMatchRate.description: Mean of per-state match rates, weighted equally.

recommendationReason.description:
Diagnostic explaining why `recommended_state` is `None`. Set when
the matcher *deliberately* withholds a recommendation (e.g. the
spec failed distinctness validation per §5.12); absent otherwise
(e.g. when every state simply scored below the confidence floor).
Free-form for forward-compat; current values:
`"spec_validation_failed"`.

recommendedState.description:
The single state (if any) the matcher recommends the caller treat
as "current". `None` when the matcher has no preference.

severityCounts.description: Counts of assertion failures bucketed by severity.

assertions.description: One entry per IR assertion in the state.

SpecCheckResult.description:
Result of evaluating one or more page specs against a bridge snapshot.

`result_schema_version` is the FIRST field per §5.15 — read-time
forward migration uses it to route legacy JSONB through
`result_migration`. Pre-versioned rows (written before v1 shipped)
deserialize as `0`.

bridgeFingerprint.description: App / route / bridge-version fingerprint at snapshot time. §5.8 G3.

evaluatedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC. When the spec was hashed and the snapshot indexed.

resultSchemaVersion.description: v1 = 1. Missing on the wire (pre-versioned persisted rows) → 0.

snapshotId.description: Caller-minted snapshot ID. Format: `"scs_" + ULID()`. See §5.8.

snapshotSha256.description:
JCS-canonicalized SHA-256 of the raw snapshot payload, as
`"sha256-<hex>"`. Available on adapter calls that went through
`wrap_snapshot` (HTTP / MCP fresh-fetch path); `None` for in-process
evaluator calls (validator, distinctness check, supplied-snapshot
path that doesn't precompute it) which either don't have raw bytes
to hash or chose to skip the JCS round-trip.

Consumers that join spec-check results back to a stored snapshot
blob should prefer this over `bridge_fingerprint.snapshot_timestamp`
(timestamps collide; content hashes don't).

specContentHash.description: Hash of the spec IR document at evaluation time. `"sha256-<hex>"`.

stateResults.description: One result per `IrState` in the spec.

summary.description: Aggregate summary across all evaluated states.

warnings.description: Soft signals — currently used for `Stale` from `SnapshotFetchError`.

SpecCheckResult.title: SpecCheckResult

failOn.description:
Severities that should cause the step to fail when present in

failWhenNoApp.description:
If `true`, the step fails when the target app isn't connected to
the bridge.

failWhenNoSpec.description: If `true`, the step fails when no spec exists for `page_id`.

confidence.description: Matcher confidence in the recommendation.

reason.description: Free-form explanation (e.g. "highest match rate among all states").

overallMatchRate.description: Mean of per-state match rates, weighted equally.

recommendedState.description:

SpecValidation.description: Per-page validation summary surfaced on `GET /spec/list`. §5.12 G2.

degenerateStateIds.description:
State IDs whose distinctness signal is empty (a state with no
assertions that distinguish it from any other state).

indistinguishableStatePairs.description:
Pairs of state IDs that share an identical normalized distinctness
signal. Each inner array is sorted lexicographically so the same
pair always serializes the same way regardless of detection order.

SpecValidation.title: SpecValidation

StageCondition.description:
Condition for conditional stage execution.

When attached to a [`WorkflowStage`], the stage is skipped if the condition
evaluates to "should skip". All condition fields are optional and combine
with AND semantics — all specified conditions must be met for the stage to
run.

ifPrevious.description:
Run this stage only if the previous stage had this outcome.

- `"passed"`: run only if previous stage verification passed
- `"failed"`: run only if previous stage verification failed
- `"any"`: always run regardless of previous outcome (default behavior)

minFailures.description:
Skip this stage if the total number of failed stages so far is below
this threshold. Useful for "recovery" stages that only run when
multiple prior stages have failed.

minIteration.description:
Run this stage only after this many loop iterations have occurred
(across all stages). Useful for "escalation" stages that only kick in
after initial attempts have failed.

StageCondition.title: StageCondition

StageInput.description: An input required by a stage, referencing a prior stage's output.

fromStage.description:
Which stage provides this input (stage id). If omitted, searches all
prior stages.

key.description:
The key to bind this input to (matches a [`StageOutput::key`] from a
prior stage).

required.description:
Whether this input is required (default: `true`). Missing required
inputs are Critical findings.

StageInput.title: StageInput

StageOutput.description: An output declared by a stage, available to subsequent stages.
StageOutput.title: StageOutput

StageTransition.description:
A record of a stage transition during task execution.

Used for building the stage-based timeline on the recap page.

iteration.description: Iteration number at the time of the transition.

StageTransition.title: StageTransition

StallDetectorConfig.description:
Configuration for the stall-detection subsystem.

Controls how the loop engine decides a run is stuck in a repeated-action /
oscillation / runaway-step pattern and forces an exit.

maxRepeatedActions.description: Maximum times the same action may repeat before stall is declared.

maxTotalSteps.description: Absolute ceiling on total steps across all iterations.

oscillationWindow.description: Window (in actions) used to detect oscillation between two states.

StateContext.description: State-machine context captured before/after an action.
StateContext.title: StateContext

DiscoveredState.description:
A discovered UI state (collection of co-occurring elements).

States represent distinct UI screens or views identified by the set of
images that consistently appear together.

confidence.description: Confidence score for state detection (0.0–1.0). Defaults to `1.0`.

description.description: Description of what this state represents.

elementIds.description: IDs of DOM elements (for web extraction).

imageIds.description: IDs of images in this state.

renderIds.description: IDs of renders where this state appears.

DiscoveredStateImage.description:
Visual element within a discovered state.

Represents an image crop from a screenshot with its bounding box and
optional pixel-level identification.

confidence.description: Confidence score for this image (0.0–1.0). Defaults to `1.0`.

pixelHash.description: Hash of pixel data for deduplication.

stateId.description: ID of the state this image belongs to.

DiscoveredTransition.description:
A transition between discovered states.

Transitions represent actions that change the active set of states on the
screen.

confidence.description: Confidence score for transition detection (0.0–1.0). Defaults to `1.0`.

DiscoveryBoundingBox.description:
Bounding box for a discovered image element.

Pixel-space rectangle on a source screenshot. `width` / `height` are `> 0`
on the Python side (validator not duplicated here — this is a wire-format
layer).

height.description: Height of the bounding box (pixels, positive).

width.description: Width of the bounding box (pixels, positive).

x.description: X coordinate of the top-left corner (pixels).

y.description: Y coordinate of the top-left corner (pixels).

DiscoverySourceType.description:
Source of a state-discovery result.

Identifies which discovery pathway produced the state machine. Mirrors
Python `DiscoverySourceType(str, Enum)`.

DiscoveryTransitionTrigger.description:
Trigger for a discovered state transition.

Describes the action (click, type, …) that caused a transition. All
identifying fields are optional — different discovery sources populate
different subsets.

elementId.description: ID of the DOM element (for web extraction).

imageId.description: ID of the image that was clicked/interacted with.

selector.description: CSS selector for the trigger element.

type.description: Type of trigger action. Defaults to `click` when omitted on the wire.

TransitionTriggerType.description:
Type of action that triggers a state transition.

Mirrors Python `TransitionTriggerType(str, Enum)`.

confidence.description: Overall confidence score (0.0–1.0).

elementToRenders.description: Mapping of element IDs to render IDs where they appear.

projectId.description: ID of the project this belongs to.

sourceSessionId.description: ID of the source session (extraction, recording, …).

sourceType.description: How this state machine was discovered.

oneOf.description: Single left click on the target element.
Double click on the target element.

StateMachineState.description:
A UI state, defined by which elements are present on screen.

States are discovered via co-occurrence analysis of DOM snapshots.

acceptance_criteria.description: Acceptance criteria for verifying the state is reached.

confidence.description: Confidence score (0.0–1.0) from the discovery pass.

domain_knowledge.description: Domain knowledge entries attached to this state.

element_ids.description: Element IDs that must be present for this state to be active.

render_ids.description: Render IDs that contributed to discovering this state.

state_id.description:
Stable logical state ID (distinct from `id`; set by the user or
generator).

StateMachineTransition.description:
A transition between states, consisting of one or more actions.

Transitions define the edges of the state machine graph.

actions.description: Ordered list of actions executed as part of this transition.

activate_states.description: States activated when this transition fires.

exit_states.description: States exited when this transition fires.

from_states.description: States this transition may be taken from.

path_cost.description: Cost used by pathfinding to prefer cheaper transitions.

stays_visible.description: Whether source states stay visible after activation.

TransitionAction.description:
A single action within a transition.

Each action targets an element and performs an interaction. Most fields are
only meaningful for specific action types — see the per-field docs.

drag_hold_delay.description: Hold delay before the first move in milliseconds (`drag` action).

drag_html5.description: Dispatch HTML5 drag events alongside mouse events (`drag` action).

drag_steps.description: Number of intermediate mousemove steps (`drag` action).

drag_target.description: Drag target element ID or selector (`drag` action).

drag_target_position.description:
Drag target position (e.g., `{ x, y }` stringified, or a named
position) (`drag` action).

position.description:
Click position relative to the element
(`click` / `doubleClick` / `rightClick`).

select_by_label.description: Select by label instead of value (`select` action).

target.description: Target element ID (used by most element actions).

type.description: The kind of action to perform.

value.description: Value to select or set (`select` / `setValue` actions).

TransitionActionValue.description:
A value for `select` / `setValue` actions: either a single string or a list
of strings (for multi-select).

Serialized as an untagged union matching the TS `string | string[]`.

StateMachineConfigFull.description:
A config with all its states and transitions loaded.

Used when the full state machine needs to be displayed or exported. This
mirrors the TypeScript `StateMachineConfigFull extends StateMachineConfig`
by flattening the base config's fields via `#[serde(flatten)]`.

element_count.description: Number of unique elements discovered in this config.

include_html_ids.description: Whether to include HTML IDs when generating selectors.

render_count.description: Number of DOM renders collected for this config.

states.description: All states belonging to this config.

transitions.description: All transitions belonging to this config.

StateMachineConfigFull.title: StateMachineConfigFull

StateMachineExport.description:
Portable export format for state machines.

Used when exporting a discovery result to a shareable artifact.
`source_type` is kept as a free-form `String` to match Python's
`DiscoverySourceType | str` union (enabling imports that predate the enum).

sourceType.description:
Original discovery source (string for forward compatibility — Python
accepts `DiscoverySourceType | str`).

StateMachineExport.title: StateMachineExport

StateMachineExportFormat.description:
Format for exporting a state machine config to JSON.

Compatible with `UIBridgeRuntime.from_dict()` in the qontinui library. The
nested maps hold opaque per-state / per-transition / per-config dictionaries
because the exporter serializes implementation-specific fields not captured
by the DTO types.

All UI Bridge SDK standard actions plus workflow-level actions

Parameterized actions: `click`, `doubleClick`, `rightClick`, `type`,
`select`, `scroll`, `drag`.
No-param actions: `clear`, `focus`, `blur`, `hover`, `check`, `uncheck`,
`toggle`, `setValue`, `submit`, `reset`.
Workflow-level actions: `wait`, `navigate`.

Serialized as a bare camelCase string matching the TypeScript literal
union (e.g., `"doubleClick"`, `"setValue"`).

oneOf.description: Single left click on the target element.
Double click on the target element.
Right click (context menu) on the target element.
Type text into the target element.
Select a value or label on a `<select>` element.
Move keyboard focus to the target element.
Remove keyboard focus from the target element.
Hover the mouse over the target element.
Scroll the target element or page.
Set the value of a form control directly.
Drag from the target element to another position/element.
Submit the form containing the target element.
Reset the form containing the target element.

TransitionAction.description:
A single action within a transition.

Each action targets an element and performs an interaction. Most fields are
only meaningful for specific action types — see the per-field docs.

drag_hold_delay.description: Hold delay before the first move in milliseconds (`drag` action).

drag_html5.description: Dispatch HTML5 drag events alongside mouse events (`drag` action).

drag_steps.description: Number of intermediate mousemove steps (`drag` action).

drag_target.description: Drag target element ID or selector (`drag` action).

drag_target_position.description:
Drag target position (e.g., `{ x, y }` stringified, or a named
position) (`drag` action).

position.description:
Click position relative to the element
(`click` / `doubleClick` / `rightClick`).

select_by_label.description: Select by label instead of value (`select` action).

target.description: Target element ID (used by most element actions).

type.description: The kind of action to perform.

value.description: Value to select or set (`select` / `setValue` actions).

TransitionActionValue.description:
A value for `select` / `setValue` actions: either a single string or a list
of strings (for multi-select).

Serialized as an untagged union matching the TS `string | string[]`.

StateMachineTransitionUpdate.description: Payload for updating an existing transition.
StateMachineTransitionUpdate.title: StateMachineTransitionUpdate

AssertionMiss.description: Top-level miss diagnostic for a failed assertion.

candidates.description:
Top-N near-match candidates, capped externally. Ordered by score
descending.

reason.description: Coarse-grained classification of why the match failed.

AssertionOutcome.description:
Internally-tagged on `status` — `{ "status": "pass", "matched": {...} }`
or `{ "status": "fail", "miss": {...} }`. Distinct from the
worker-output `{ "type": ..., "data": ... }` envelope used elsewhere
in the codebase.

oneOf.description: Assertion passed against the named matched element.

matched.description: The element that satisfied the assertion.

oneOf.description: Assertion failed; the miss carries reason + near-match candidates.

severity.description:
Free-form severity string — `"critical"` / `"error"` / `"warning"`
/ `"info"`. Free-form so policy adapters can introduce new tiers
without bumping the schema.

CandidateMiss.description:
A near-match candidate — an element that scored above the cut-off but
didn't fully match the assertion criteria.

fieldDiffs.description: Per-field diffs explaining why the candidate didn't fully match.

path.description: CSS selector or DOM path identifying the candidate.

FieldDiff.description:
Per-field diff between an assertion's expected criteria and a
candidate element's actual value.

actual.description: Actual value from the bridge element.

expected.description: Expected value from the IR assertion.

MatchedElement.description: Identity of the bridge element that satisfied an assertion.

path.description: CSS selector or DOM path identifying the element.

role.description: ARIA role of the matched element.

text.description: Visible text of the matched element.

MissReason.description:
Coarse-grained classification of an assertion miss.

Per §5.3 — locked as a unit enum. Structured detail (per-field diffs,
near-match candidates) lives on `AssertionMiss` / `CandidateMiss`.

oneOf.description: No bridge element scored above the match threshold.
Best candidate's ARIA role differed from the expected role.
Best candidate's text differed from the expected text.
Best candidate was found but not visible.
Best candidate had a differing attribute (data-*, aria-*, etc.).

Multiple elements matched equally well; assertion expected a
unique target.

assertions.description: One entry per IR assertion in the state.

StateNodeData.description:
Data passed to a state node in the ReactFlow graph editor.

Mirrors the TypeScript shape with camelCase field names. The
`onStartElementDrag` callback from the TS type is a UI-layer concern and is
intentionally omitted from the DTO.

confidence.description: Discovery confidence.

elementCount.description: Number of elements defining this state.

elementThumbnails.description:
Optional map of element ID → base64 thumbnail image (data URL or raw
base64 PNG).

isDropTarget.description: Whether this node is the current drop target (drag-and-drop).

isInitial.description: Whether this is an initial state.

isSelected.description: Whether the node is currently selected in the editor.

StateNodeData.title: StateNodeData

StateFilterItem.description: State item for the filter dropdown.

count.description: Number of embeddings in this state.

StatesResponse.description: Response with list of states for filtering.

count.description: Total number of states.

StatesResponse.title: StatesResponse

StdioConfig.description:
Stdio-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `command`, `args`, and `env` must be treated as
execution-critical. See module-level docs.

cwd.description: Working directory (absolute path). `None` inherits the runner's cwd.

env.description:
Extra environment variables for the subprocess. **Secret surface** —
frequently holds API tokens.

StdioConfig.title: StdioConfig

StepExecutionConfig.description:
Execution config captured for a verification step.

The TypeScript type includes an index signature `[key: string]: unknown`,
so extra arbitrary fields are captured in `extra` via `serde(flatten)` and
passed through opaquely.

targetImageId.description: Target image ID, if the action references an image.

targetImageName.description: Target image name, if the action references an image.

StepExecutionConfig.title: StepExecutionConfig

needsInput.description: Whether this finding requires human input.

StructuredSignal.description: A signal from the worker to the orchestrator.

message.description: Optional message providing context for the signal.

StructuredSignal.title: StructuredSignal

CriterionType.description: Type of verification for a criterion.

oneOf.description: Can be verified programmatically without AI.
Log pattern must match (or not match).

SuccessCriterion.description: A success criterion that must be met for task completion.

domain.description: Domain this criterion belongs to (for multi-worker verification).

evaluationPrompt.description: For AI-evaluated criteria: the evaluation prompt.

isCritical.description:
Whether failure of this criterion blocks task completion.
- `true` (default): failure blocks completion, worker must fix.
- `false`: failure is informational, doesn't block completion.

required.description: Whether this criterion must pass for task completion.

type.description: Type of verification required. Serialized as `"type"` on the wire.

verificationConfig.description:
Configuration blob for the verification method (command args, log
patterns, Playwright script path, etc.).

verificationMethod.description: For deterministic criteria: the verification method to use.

weight.description: Optional weight for partial success scoring.

SuccessCriterion.title: SuccessCriterion

SummarizationConfig.description:
Configuration for the context-summarization subsystem.

When the loop's rolling context approaches the token budget, older
iterations are compressed into a summary to keep the prompt small.

maxTokensBudget.description: Target maximum tokens for the full loop context.

preserveLastNIterations.description: Number of most-recent iterations to keep verbatim (never summarized).

summaryMaxTokens.description: Cap on tokens emitted by a single summarization pass.

tokenThresholdPct.description: Fraction of the token budget (0.0–1.0) at which summarization triggers.

SummarizationConfig.title: SummarizationConfig

AccessibilityRoleCriterion.description:
Role selector criterion — either a single role name or a list of roles.
Matches the Python `str | list[str] | None` type on
`AccessibilityTarget.role`.

CoordinateSystem.description:
Coordinate system identifier. Always specify which system you are working
with to avoid confusion in multi-monitor setups.

oneOf.description:
Absolute screen coordinates (OS-level). May be negative for monitors
left of or above the primary. Used by pyautogui, mouse ops, and
window positioning.

Relative to the virtual desktop origin `(min_x, min_y)`. Always
non-negative within the virtual desktop bounds. Used by screenshot
pixel lookups and FIND match results.

Relative to a specific monitor's top-left corner. Requires
`monitor_index`. Used by `searchRegion` in state configurations.

Coordinates.description:
X,Y coordinates with optional coordinate system specification.

Coordinates without an explicit `system` default to
[`CoordinateSystem::Screen`] for backward compatibility.

x.description: X coordinate (horizontal position).

y.description: Y coordinate (vertical position).

MatchAdjustment.description: Match adjustment — modify the matched region before acting.

MatchMethod.description: Template matching method used by OpenCV-style correlation search.

Region.description:
Rectangular region on screen. Like [`Coordinates`], can optionally specify
which coordinate system the `x`/`y` are in.

height.description: Height of the region (must be positive).

width.description: Width of the region (must be positive).

x.description: X coordinate of the top-left corner.

y.description: Y coordinate of the top-left corner.

SearchStrategy.description:
Strategy for selecting among multiple matches in a FIND action.

Variants serialize uppercase (`"FIRST"`, `"ALL"`, `"BEST"`, `"EACH"`) to
match the existing Python enum and all stored action configs.

TextSearchOptions.description: Text search options for OCR-based finding.

TargetConfig.description:
The discriminated union over all 14 target configurations. Wire format is
a flat object tagged by the `type` field (`"image"`, `"stateImage"`, …).

oneOf.description:
Image target configuration supporting multiple images with search
strategies. `imageIds` is required with at least one element — single
image targets use a one-element list.
Region target — a rectangular area of the screen.
Text target — OCR-based targeting by visible text.
State-string target — references one or more strings in a state.

Target a StateRegion by ID. Preserves the region's monitor
association instead of using a global default.

Target a StateLocation by ID. Preserves the location's monitor
association.

Target a StateImage by state ID + image IDs. Used by navigation to
verify state via FIND on state-associated images.
Act at the current mouse position (pure action — no localization).
Use the location from the most recent FIND action.

Target a specific match (by zero-based index) from the last action
result.
Target all matches from the last action result.

Target the match that came from a specific image ID in a multi-image
FIND result (requires the previous FIND used `EACH`).

Target an element by accessibility ref or selector — AI-optimized
element selection via `@e1` refs or role/name matching. Requires a
captured accessibility tree.

TargetConfig.title: TargetConfig

Confidence.description: Confidence level for findings and verification results.

oneOf.description: High confidence — deterministic evidence or clear observation.
Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.
Low confidence — speculative or weakly supported.

CriterionOverride.description:
An override for a verification criterion.

Workers can emit these to indicate that a failing criterion should be
accepted as-is with justification, rather than requiring a fix.

item.description: What specifically is being overridden (e.g., class name, file path).

iteration.description: Iteration when this override was recorded.

justification.description: Justification for why this override is acceptable.

recordedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the override was recorded.

workerId.description: Worker ID that provided the override (if multi-worker).

CriterionType.description: Type of verification for a criterion.

oneOf.description: Can be verified programmatically without AI.

Finding.description: A finding recorded by a worker agent.

confidence.description: Confidence level.

evidence.description: Supporting evidence (file paths, log excerpts, etc.).

findingType.description:
Type of finding.

Valid types: `"bug"`, `"root_cause"`, `"observation"`, `"hypothesis"`,
`"solution"`, `"environment"`. Note: `"environment"` findings (PATH
issues, disk space, tools not installed) require user intervention and
should NOT trigger automatic retries.

IterationVerificationResults.description: Aggregated verification results for a single iteration.

aiPassed.description: Whether all required AI checks passed (true if no AI criteria).

appliedOverrides.description: Criterion overrides applied in this iteration.

deterministicPassed.description: Whether all required deterministic checks passed.

overriddenCriteria.description: Criteria that failed but were accepted due to overrides.

VerificationResult.description: Result of a single verification check.

confidence.description: Confidence level (for AI verification).

rawOutput.description: Raw output / details, e.g., captured command output.

TaskCompletionResult.description:
Task-completion result.

Internally tagged by `status`: the variant fields are inlined alongside the
discriminator rather than nested under a `data` key.

oneOf.description: Task failed (max iterations or unrecoverable error).

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the failure.

oneOf.description: Task stopped by user or system.

at_iteration.description: Iteration at which the task was stopped.

can_resume.description: Whether the task can be resumed from this point.

findings.description: Findings accumulated up to the stop.

oneOf.description: Task paused at max iterations, awaiting user decision.

at_iteration.description: Iteration at which the task paused.

findings.description: Findings accumulated up to the pause.

max_iterations.description: The max-iteration limit that triggered the pause.

TaskCompletionResult.title: TaskCompletionResult

ScheduledTaskStatus.description: Status of a scheduled task execution.

oneOf.description: Finished with an error after the task had started running.

The task could not even start (workflow file missing, prompt resolve
failure, ai_session spawn error, etc.). Distinct from `Failed` because
it drives the launch-failure backoff in
[`ScheduledTask::launch_failure_backoff_seconds`].

Skipped because the task had already completed successfully and
`skip_if_completed` is `true`.

Slot detected as missed during catch-up reconciliation while the
runner was offline. Recorded as a history row when
[`CatchUpPolicy::Skip`] is in effect; never returned for currently
queued tasks.

TaskExecutionRecord.description: Record of a single task execution.

autoFixSessionId.description: Session ID of the auto-fix session, if one was triggered.

catchUpRun.description:
Whether this execution record was created by the catch-up reconciler
(rather than the normal scheduling tick).

errorMessage.description: Error message if the execution failed.

executionId.description: Unique ID for this execution (UUID v4 string).

scheduledFor.description:
ISO 8601 timestamp of the *scheduled* slot this execution covers,
distinct from `started_at` (which is the actual launch time). Allows
detecting "ran X minutes late" and is consumed by the missed-run
reconciler. Optional for backward compatibility with execution rows
written before this field existed.

sessionId.description:
Session ID if this execution triggered an AI session, used for
downstream success tracking.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when execution started.

success.description: Whether the task succeeded, read from the session checkpoint.

triggeredAutoFix.description: Whether auto-fix was triggered after this execution.

TaskExecutionRecord.title: TaskExecutionRecord

TaskRunStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a task run.

oneOf.description: The task finished with an error.
The task was stopped before natural completion.
A one-off AI task launched from a prompt.
A task executed by the scheduler.

TaskRun.description:
A task run as tracked by the local runner during execution.

Mirrors `TaskRun` in the runner's `taskRun.ts`. Optional fields here use
`?` in TypeScript, so they are omitted on the wire when missing.

autoContinue.description: Whether the task will auto-continue into another session on exit.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task completed.

configId.description: ID of the workflow config used to run this task, if any.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the task failed.

goalAchieved.description: Whether the task's goal was achieved (AI assessment).

outputLog.description: Accumulated output log for the task run.

remainingWork.description: Description of any remaining work (AI assessment).

sessionsCount.description: Number of AI sessions that have been run.

summary.description: AI-generated summary of the task run.

summaryGeneratedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the summary was generated.

taskType.description: Kind of task (one-off, automation, or scheduled).

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task record was last updated.

workflowName.description: Name of the workflow used to run this task, if any.

TaskRun.title: TaskRun

TaskRunStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a task run.

oneOf.description: The task finished with an error.
The task was stopped before natural completion.

TaskRunBackend.description:
A task run as returned by the backend API.

Mirrors `TaskRunBackend` in the web app's `task-runs.ts`. Nullable
ownership fields (`project_id`, `created_by_user_id`, `runner_id`,
`max_sessions`, `output_summary`, `error_message`, `duration_seconds`,
`completed_at`) are required on the wire but may be `null`; they are
`Option<T>` with `serde(default)` so deserialize tolerates missing, but
are always serialized (including as `null`) to preserve the wire shape.

autoContinue.description: Whether the task will auto-continue into another session on exit.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task completed.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.

createdByUserId.description: User who created the task run, if known.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the task failed.

fullOutputStored.description: Whether the full output log was persisted.

outputSummary.description: Short summary of the run output, if stored.

projectId.description: Owning project ID, if scoped to a project.

runnerId.description: Runner instance that executed the task, if known.

sessionsCount.description: Number of AI sessions that have been run.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the record was last updated.

TaskRunBackend.title: TaskRunBackend

TaskRunFinding.description:
A finding surfaced during a task run (bug, enhancement, TODO, etc.).

All nullable fields here are required-nullable on the wire (always present,
possibly `null`), so they use `serde(default)` without `skip_serializing_if`.

actionType.description: How the finding should be acted upon.

codeSnippet.description: Snippet of code illustrating the issue.

columnNumber.description: Column number where the issue was found.

detectedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was detected.

detectedInSession.description: Session number in which the finding was detected.

filePath.description: File path where the issue was found.

inputOptions.description: Suggested response options for the user, if input is needed.

lineNumber.description: Line number where the issue was found.

needsInput.description: Whether this finding requires user input.

question.description: Question posed to the user, if input is needed.

resolution.description: How the finding was resolved, if applicable.

resolvedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was resolved.

resolvedInSession.description: Session number in which the finding was resolved.

signatureHash.description: Hash used to deduplicate findings across runs.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the record was last updated.

TaskRunFindingActionType.description: How a finding should be acted upon.

TaskRunFindingCategory.description: Category of a finding surfaced during a task run.

oneOf.description: A security issue (e.g., vulnerability, exposed secret).
A runtime issue observed during execution.
An issue that was already fixed.
Behavior that looked suspicious but is expected.
Critical severity — blocks release or execution.
Needs user input before it can proceed.

TaskRunFindingSummary.description: Aggregated finding counts grouped along each axis.

TaskRunSession.description: A single AI session within a task run.

durationSeconds.description: Duration of the session in seconds.

endedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session ended.

outputSummary.description: Short summary of the session output, if stored.

sessionNumber.description: 1-based session index within the parent task run.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session started.

TaskRunStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a task run.

oneOf.description: The task finished with an error.
The task was stopped before natural completion.

The TypeScript `TaskRunBackendDetail extends TaskRunBackend` is modeled in

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task completed.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.

createdByUserId.description: User who created the task run, if known.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the task failed.

findings.description: Findings surfaced during this task run.

fullOutputStored.description: Whether the full output log was persisted.

outputSummary.description: Short summary of the run output, if stored.

projectId.description: Owning project ID, if scoped to a project.

runnerId.description: Runner instance that executed the task, if known.

sessions.description: AI sessions associated with this task run.

sessionsCount.description: Number of AI sessions that have been run.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the record was last updated.

TaskRunBackendDetail.title: TaskRunBackendDetail

oneOf.description: A one-off AI task launched from a prompt.
A task executed by the scheduler.

executionStepsJson.description: Serialized execution steps, if provided ad-hoc.

runnerId.description: Optional runner that will execute the task.

The task was stopped before natural completion.

TaskRunStatus.title: TaskRunStatus

TaskRunStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a task run.

oneOf.description: The task finished with an error.
The task was stopped before natural completion.

TaskRunUpdate.description:
Request payload for updating an existing task run. All fields are optional;
only those supplied are applied.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task completed.

fullOutputStored.description: Whether the full output log has been stored.

TaskRunUpdate.title: TaskRunUpdate

oneOf.description: A one-off AI task launched from a prompt.
A task executed by the scheduler.

TerminalExitEvent.description:
Event payload emitted when a terminal process exits.

Delivered over the Tauri `terminal-exit` channel and re-broadcast on the
WebSocket relay. After this event fires the corresponding
[`TerminalInfo::is_alive`] will be `false` and [`TerminalInfo::exit_code`]
will carry the same value surfaced here.

exitCode.description:
Exit code reported by the OS, or `None` if the status could not be
captured (e.g., the wait call itself errored).

terminalId.description: ID of the terminal session that exited.

TerminalExitEvent.title: TerminalExitEvent

TerminalInfo.description:
Information about a terminal session, returned to the frontend.

Returned from the `terminal_create` and `terminal_list` Tauri commands
and emitted as the payload of the `terminal-created` event. Derived
fresh from the live `TerminalSession` each time — never persisted.

createdAt.description:
Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the session was created.

Used as the sort key for `TerminalManager::list` so the UI shows
terminals in creation order.

exitCode.description: Process exit code, once the shell has exited.

id.description: Unique session identifier (UUID v4 minted by the runner).

isAlive.description: Whether the shell process is still running.

pageId.description:
Which terminal page this session belongs to (for multi-page support).

Older wire forms without this field hydrate to `"default"` via
[`default_page_id`].

pid.description: OS process ID of the spawned shell, if still known.

title.description: Human-readable title shown in the UI tab (e.g., "Terminal 1").

totalBytesProduced.description:
Monotonic counter of all bytes ever produced by this PTY.

Read by the frontend to detect missed output after a reconnect; the
scrollback buffer's `start_offset` is derived from this counter.

workingDir.description: Absolute working directory the shell was started in.

TerminalInfo.title: TerminalInfo

TerminalOutputEvent.description:
Event payload emitted when a terminal produces output.

Delivered over the Tauri `terminal-output` channel and re-broadcast on
the WebSocket relay for remote/mobile consumers. The `data` payload is
base64-encoded raw bytes because xterm.js needs raw bytes (PTY output
can contain partial UTF-8 sequences that would corrupt a `String`
field).

data.description:
Base64-encoded bytes produced by the PTY.

Raw bytes are required (not UTF-8 text) because PTY output can
contain partial UTF-8 sequences that span read boundaries.

terminalId.description: ID of the terminal session producing this output.

TerminalOutputEvent.title: TerminalOutputEvent

TextSearchOptions.description: Text search options for OCR-based finding.
TextSearchOptions.title: TextSearchOptions

TicketSource.description:
Which ticket provider a config / ticket refers to.

Serialized as the lowercase provider tag (`"github"`, `"linear"`, `"jira"`)
to match the on-the-wire tags used by the watcher config, the MCP API
layer, and the runner's DB storage keys. Variants are explicitly renamed
because `rename_all = "snake_case"` would emit `"git_hub"` for the
`GitHub` variant, which would drift from the existing persisted rows.

oneOf.description:
Atlassian Jira (REST). Provider implementation is not yet wired; the
variant exists for forward compatibility of the config schema.

TicketState.description:
Abstract, provider-neutral ticket lifecycle state.

Maps onto provider-specific states (`open` / `closed` on GitHub, workflow
state types on Linear, etc.) inside the runner's provider implementations.
Serialized as `snake_case`.

oneOf.description: Ticket is open and ready for work.
A task is actively working on this ticket.
Work is done and the ticket is awaiting review/close.

Ticket.description:
A ticket fetched from an external provider.

Identity is carried by `(source, external_id)` — `external_id` is the
provider-specific identifier (GitHub issue number, Linear identifier, Jira
key) rather than a runner-assigned UUID.

body.description: Ticket body / description (Markdown for GitHub/Linear, wiki-markup on Jira).

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the ticket was created.

externalId.description: Provider-assigned external ID (issue number, ticket key, etc.).

labels.description: Labels applied to the ticket by the provider.

source.description: Which provider this ticket came from.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the ticket was last updated.

url.description: Canonical URL to the ticket in the provider's UI.

Ticket.title: Ticket

body.description: Comment body (Markdown for GitHub/Linear, wiki-markup on Jira).

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the comment was created.

TicketSource.description:
Which ticket provider a config / ticket refers to.

Serialized as the lowercase provider tag (`"github"`, `"linear"`, `"jira"`)
to match the on-the-wire tags used by the watcher config, the MCP API
layer, and the runner's DB storage keys. Variants are explicitly renamed
because `rename_all = "snake_case"` would emit `"git_hub"` for the
`GitHub` variant, which would drift from the existing persisted rows.

oneOf.description:
Atlassian Jira (REST). Provider implementation is not yet wired; the
variant exists for forward compatibility of the config schema.

TicketProviderConfig.description:
Configuration for a ticket provider watcher.

Persisted in `workflow_triggers.trigger_config` and
`ticket_provider_configs.config_json` so that watcher processes can be
reconstructed across runner restarts.

**Security**: `api_token` is a secret. This struct serializes it so that
the same shape works for at-rest persistence, but MCP / HTTP API
responses exposing this config to end-user UIs MUST redact the token
before returning. See the module-level doc for the full policy.

actionableLabels.description:
Labels / filters that mark a ticket as "actionable" (i.e. eligible to
spawn a workflow task). All listed labels must be present for a match.

apiToken.description:
API token for the provider. **Secret** — see security note above.
Persisted in the DB so the watcher can reconstruct a provider across
restarts; redact before exposing over UI-facing APIs.

target.description:
Provider-specific target:
- GitHub: `"owner/repo"`
- Linear: team key (e.g. `"ENG"`)
- Jira: project key

updateOnCompletion.description:
Whether to update the remote ticket's state when the spawned task
completes. Default: true.

workflowId.description: ID of the workflow to spawn for matched tickets.

TicketProviderConfig.title: TicketProviderConfig

crate-level docs for the rationale — the types crate is wire-only and
doesn't depend on a chrono version).

endTime.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. `None` while the event is still in flight.

TimingInfo.title: TimingInfo

MatchLocation.description: Location of a pattern match on screen.

h.description: Height of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

w.description: Width of the matched region (optional — point matches omit it).

TopMatch.description: A single match result with confidence and location.

MouseButton.description:
Mouse button for click actions.

Serialized as a bare lowercase string.

Point.description: An (x, y) pixel offset used by the click-position and related parameters.

x.description: Horizontal offset in pixels.

y.description: Vertical offset in pixels.

ScrollDirection.description:
Scroll direction for the `scroll` action.

Serialized as a bare lowercase string.

StandardActionType.description:
All UI Bridge SDK standard actions plus workflow-level actions
(`wait`, `navigate`).

Parameterized actions: `click`, `doubleClick`, `rightClick`, `type`,
`select`, `scroll`, `drag`.
No-param actions: `clear`, `focus`, `blur`, `hover`, `check`, `uncheck`,
`toggle`, `setValue`, `submit`, `reset`.
Workflow-level actions: `wait`, `navigate`.

Serialized as a bare camelCase string matching the TypeScript literal
union (e.g., `"doubleClick"`, `"setValue"`).

oneOf.description: Single left click on the target element.
Double click on the target element.
Right click (context menu) on the target element.
Type text into the target element.
Select a value or label on a `<select>` element.
Move keyboard focus to the target element.
Remove keyboard focus from the target element.
Hover the mouse over the target element.
Scroll the target element or page.
Set the value of a form control directly.
Drag from the target element to another position/element.
Submit the form containing the target element.
Reset the form containing the target element.

TransitionActionValue.description:
A value for `select` / `setValue` actions: either a single string or a list
of strings (for multi-select).

Serialized as an untagged union matching the TS `string | string[]`.

TransitionAction.description:
A single action within a transition.

Each action targets an element and performs an interaction. Most fields are
only meaningful for specific action types — see the per-field docs.

drag_hold_delay.description: Hold delay before the first move in milliseconds (`drag` action).

drag_html5.description: Dispatch HTML5 drag events alongside mouse events (`drag` action).

drag_steps.description: Number of intermediate mousemove steps (`drag` action).

drag_target.description: Drag target element ID or selector (`drag` action).

drag_target_position.description:
Drag target position (e.g., `{ x, y }` stringified, or a named
position) (`drag` action).

position.description:
Click position relative to the element
(`click` / `doubleClick` / `rightClick`).

select_by_label.description: Select by label instead of value (`select` action).

target.description: Target element ID (used by most element actions).

type.description: The kind of action to perform.

value.description: Value to select or set (`select` / `setValue` actions).

TransitionAction.title: TransitionAction

TransitionActionValue.description:
A value for `select` / `setValue` actions: either a single string or a list
of strings (for multi-select).

Serialized as an untagged union matching the TS `string | string[]`.

TransitionEdgeData.description: Data passed to a transition edge in the ReactFlow graph editor.

actionCount.description: Number of actions in this transition.

firstActionTarget.description: Target of the first action, for label rendering.

isHighlighted.description: Whether the edge is highlighted (e.g., part of a path preview).

staysVisible.description: Whether source states stay visible after activation.

TransitionEdgeData.title: TransitionEdgeData

TransitionExecutionResult.description:
Result of executing a single state transition at runtime.

Returned by the runner's Tauri commands or the qontinui Python subprocess.

active_states.description: States that are active after the transition.

error.description: Error message when execution failed.

transition_id.description: Logical transition ID that was executed.

TransitionExecutionResult.title: TransitionExecutionResult

TransitionInfo.description: Information about a single available transition from the current state.

to_state.description:
Destination state ID (`null` if the transition stays within the same
state set — e.g. a self-transition or a guard-only action).

workflows.description: Names of workflows attached to this transition.

TransitionInfo.title: TransitionInfo

Type of action that triggers a state transition.

Mirrors Python `TransitionTriggerType(str, Enum)`.

isInline.description: Whether this action is displayed inline.

Primary event type for execution logging. Carries the event type, the

path.description: Path from root to this node (breadcrumb).

timestamp.description: When this event was emitted (Unix epoch seconds).

type.description: Event type identifier — always `"tree_event"` on the wire.

ComponentActionInfo.description: Information about a single action exposed by a UI Bridge component.

description.description: Longer description of what the action does.

id.description: Unique action identifier within the component.

UIBridgeComponent.description:
A registered component in the UI Bridge registry.

Components group related elements and expose higher-level actions
(e.g. "submit form", "reset filters").

elementIds.description: IDs of elements that belong to this component.

id.description: Unique component ID within the registry.

mounted.description: Whether the component's React component is currently mounted.

registeredAt.description: Unix-epoch millisecond timestamp when the component was registered.

UIBridgeComponent.title: UIBridgeComponent

ElementBbox.description:
Viewport-relative bounding box in CSS pixels.

This is the live on-screen geometry captured at snapshot time via
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`. Present only when the SDK has a
live DOM ref for the element; absent when the element is registered
without a ref or when the snapshot is served from the DOM-fallback
scanner.

Click target for a hit is `(x + width/2, y + height/2)`.

height.description: Height of the bbox in CSS pixels.

width.description: Width of the bbox in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the bbox origin in viewport CSS pixels.

y.description: Y coordinate of the bbox origin in viewport CSS pixels.

ElementIdentifier.description:
Identifier bundle for locating a UI Bridge element.

Elements can be addressed by any combination of UI-Bridge ID, test ID,
AWAS ID, HTML ID, XPath, or CSS selector. The `xpath` and `selector`
fields are always present; the named IDs are optional.

selector.description: CSS selector that uniquely identifies the element.

ElementRect.description:
Bounding rectangle of a DOM element, mirroring the output of
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`.

Contains both the origin+size pair (`x`, `y`, `width`, `height`) and the
edge offsets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) for consumer convenience.

bottom.description: Distance from the top of the viewport plus `height`.

height.description: Height of the element in CSS pixels.

left.description: Distance from the left of the viewport.

right.description: Distance from the left of the viewport plus `width`.

top.description: Distance from the top of the viewport.

width.description: Width of the element in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the element's origin (same as `left`).

y.description: Y coordinate of the element's origin (same as `top`).

ElementState.description:
Observable state of a UI Bridge element as returned from the React
registry.

Every element returned by the bridge includes a snapshot of its current
visibility, interactivity, and form-control value.

checked.description: Current checked state for checkbox/radio elements.

enabled.description: Whether the element is enabled (not disabled).

focused.description: Whether the element currently has keyboard focus.

visible.description: Whether the element is currently visible in the viewport.

UIBridgeElement.description:
A registered element in the UI Bridge registry.

This is the serializable subset of the React `RegisteredElement`; it
includes identity, available actions, current state, and lifecycle info.

accessibleName.description:
W3C "accessible name" per the accessible-name algorithm. Distinct from
aria_label because the algorithm may consult aria-labelledby, associated
label elements, title, or visible content. Source of truth for
IrElementCriteria.accessible_name.

actions.description: Standard actions available on this element.

ariaLabel.description:
Computed aria-label (explicit attribute, falling back to aria-labelledby
reference resolution). Source of truth for IrElementCriteria.aria_label.

bbox.description:
Viewport-relative bounding box in CSS pixels, when the SDK has a
live DOM ref. Absent for elements registered without a ref or when
the snapshot is served from the DOM-fallback scanner.

id.description: Unique element ID within the registry.

identifier.description: Identifier bundle for locating the element.

mounted.description: Whether the element's React component is currently mounted.

registeredAt.description: Unix-epoch millisecond timestamp when the element was registered.

role.description:
ARIA role of the element (explicit `role=` or implicit per W3C ARIA-in-HTML).
Populated by the SDK's element walker. Source of truth for IrElementCriteria.role.

tagName.description: HTML tag name in lowercase. Source of truth for IrElementCriteria.tag_name.

text.description:
Visible text content with whitespace collapsed (DOM innerText-equivalent
on web; accessibilityLabel/text equivalent on native). Source of truth for
IrElementCriteria.text and text_contains. Distinct from state.text_content
which is a snapshot of the form-control value.

visible.description:
Cheap viewport-visibility signal derived by the SDK as
`bbox.width > 0 && bbox.height > 0`. Use `state.visible` for the
richer occlusion check.

UIBridgeElement.title: UIBridgeElement

UIBridgeModalInfo.description: Modal/dialog entry in the active modal stack.

blocking.description: Whether this modal blocks interaction with content behind it.

detectedAt.description: Timestamp when the modal was detected (epoch ms).

dismissible.description:
Whether the modal is dismissible (RN-specific). Optional so the
web shape (which doesn't carry this bit) round-trips cleanly.

hasBackdrop.description: Web-only: whether a backdrop/overlay is present.

zIndex.description: Web-only: computed z-index of the modal.

UIBridgeModalInfo.title: UIBridgeModalInfo

detectedAt.description: Timestamp when the modal was detected (epoch ms).

dismissible.description:

web shape (which doesn't carry this bit) round-trips cleanly.

hasBackdrop.description: Web-only: whether a backdrop/overlay is present.

zIndex.description: Web-only: computed z-index of the modal.

UIBridgeModalStack.description:
Modal stack context attached to a snapshot when the SDK has a
`ModalDetector` enricher configured.
UIBridgeModalStack.title: UIBridgeModalStack
