Identifier bundle for locating a UI Bridge element.

Elements can be addressed by any combination of UI-Bridge ID, test ID,
AWAS ID, HTML ID, XPath, or CSS selector. The `xpath` and `selector`
fields are always present; the named IDs are optional.

selector.description: CSS selector that uniquely identifies the element.

ElementRect.description:
Bounding rectangle of a DOM element, mirroring the output of
`Element.getBoundingClientRect()`.

Contains both the origin+size pair (`x`, `y`, `width`, `height`) and the
edge offsets (`top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`) for consumer convenience.

bottom.description: Distance from the top of the viewport plus `height`.

height.description: Height of the element in CSS pixels.

left.description: Distance from the left of the viewport.

right.description: Distance from the left of the viewport plus `width`.

top.description: Distance from the top of the viewport.

width.description: Width of the element in CSS pixels.

x.description: X coordinate of the element's origin (same as `left`).

y.description: Y coordinate of the element's origin (same as `top`).

ElementState.description:
Observable state of a UI Bridge element as returned from the React
registry.

Every element returned by the bridge includes a snapshot of its current
visibility, interactivity, and form-control value.

checked.description: Current checked state for checkbox/radio elements.

enabled.description: Whether the element is enabled (not disabled).

focused.description: Whether the element currently has keyboard focus.

visible.description: Whether the element is currently visible in the viewport.

UIBridgeComponent.description:
A registered component in the UI Bridge registry.

Components group related elements and expose higher-level actions
(e.g. "submit form", "reset filters").

elementIds.description: IDs of elements that belong to this component.

id.description: Unique component ID within the registry.

mounted.description: Whether the component's React component is currently mounted.

registeredAt.description: Unix-epoch millisecond timestamp when the component was registered.

UIBridgeElement.description:
A registered element in the UI Bridge registry.

This is the serializable subset of the React `RegisteredElement`; it
includes identity, available actions, current state, and lifecycle info.

accessibleName.description:
W3C "accessible name" per the accessible-name algorithm. Distinct from
aria_label because the algorithm may consult aria-labelledby, associated
label elements, title, or visible content. Source of truth for
IrElementCriteria.accessible_name.

actions.description: Standard actions available on this element.

ariaLabel.description:
Computed aria-label (explicit attribute, falling back to aria-labelledby
reference resolution). Source of truth for IrElementCriteria.aria_label.

bbox.description:
Viewport-relative bounding box in CSS pixels, when the SDK has a
live DOM ref. Absent for elements registered without a ref or when
the snapshot is served from the DOM-fallback scanner.

id.description: Unique element ID within the registry.

identifier.description: Identifier bundle for locating the element.

mounted.description: Whether the element's React component is currently mounted.

registeredAt.description: Unix-epoch millisecond timestamp when the element was registered.

role.description:
ARIA role of the element (explicit `role=` or implicit per W3C ARIA-in-HTML).
Populated by the SDK's element walker. Source of truth for IrElementCriteria.role.

tagName.description: HTML tag name in lowercase. Source of truth for IrElementCriteria.tag_name.

text.description:
Visible text content with whitespace collapsed (DOM innerText-equivalent
on web; accessibilityLabel/text equivalent on native). Source of truth for
IrElementCriteria.text and text_contains. Distinct from state.text_content
which is a snapshot of the form-control value.

visible.description:
Cheap viewport-visibility signal derived by the SDK as
`bbox.width > 0 && bbox.height > 0`. Use `state.visible` for the
richer occlusion check.

UIBridgeModalInfo.description: Modal/dialog entry in the active modal stack.

blocking.description: Whether this modal blocks interaction with content behind it.

detectedAt.description: Timestamp when the modal was detected (epoch ms).

dismissible.description:
Whether the modal is dismissible (RN-specific). Optional so the
web shape (which doesn't carry this bit) round-trips cleanly.

hasBackdrop.description: Web-only: whether a backdrop/overlay is present.

zIndex.description: Web-only: computed z-index of the modal.

UIBridgeModalStack.description:
Modal stack context attached to a snapshot when the SDK has a
`ModalDetector` enricher configured.

WorkflowInfo.description: Workflow metadata included in a UI Bridge snapshot.

stepCount.description: Number of steps in the workflow.

UIBridgeSnapshot.description:
Full snapshot of the UI Bridge state.

Captures all registered elements, components, and active workflows
at a single point in time. The optional `modalStack` / `toasts` /
`undoRedo` fields are populated by the SDK's enricher slot when
configured (see `setEnrichers` on web and native registries).

currentRoute.description: Native-only: current navigation route (Expo Router pathname).

modalStack.description: Modal/sheet stack (populated when ModalDetector enricher is set).

timestamp.description: Unix-epoch millisecond timestamp of the snapshot.

toasts.description: Active and recently dismissed toasts (populated by ToastCapture).

UIBridgeSnapshot.title: UIBridgeSnapshot

UiBridgeRequestEnvelope.description:
Wire envelope for `ui-bridge-request` events emitted by
`mcp::ui_bridge::request::ui_bridge_request_sync` (and the long-timeout
variant in `mcp::ui_bridge::screenshots::ui_bridge_capture_element_images_handler`).

The runner emits an envelope whose flat shape is
`{ requestId, type, ...payloadFields }` — the previous implementation built
it via `serde_json::json!({ "requestId": ..., "type": ... })` plus a manual
merge of an `additional_payload: Value::Object`. This struct preserves that
wire shape via `#[serde(flatten)]` on `data` so byte output is identical
when callers pass `Value::Object` payloads (the only shape they use today).

The discriminator field is **`type`** on the wire; the Rust field is named
`request_type` for ergonomics and renamed via `#[serde(rename = "type")]`.
Keep this struct in sync with the TS `UIBridgeRequestPayload` envelope head
in `qontinui-runner/src/hooks/ui-bridge-events/types.ts`.

`request_type` is a free-form `String` — the set of valid discriminators is
the 171-variant `UIBridgeRequestType` TS union, but per-payload typing is
deferred indefinitely. Dispatch exhaustiveness is enforced on the React
side via `AssertEqual<AllHandledTypes, UIBridgeRequestType>` plus per-hook
`never` checks.

requestId.description:
UUID generated by the runner; round-trips back inside the matching
[`UiBridgeResponseEnvelope`] so the Rust dispatcher can pair responses
with their pending oneshot senders.

type.description:
Wire discriminator — the runner-side request kind (e.g. `"get_elements"`,
`"capture_element_images"`). Field is named `request_type` in Rust for
ergonomics; serializes as `type`.

UiBridgeRequestEnvelope.title: UiBridgeRequestEnvelope

UiBridgeResponseEnvelope.description:
Wire envelope for `ui-bridge-response` events emitted by the React
frontend in response to a [`UiBridgeRequestEnvelope`].

The runner-side dispatcher in
`mcp::ui_bridge::request::handle_ui_bridge_response` matches `request_id`
to a pending oneshot sender and forwards the inner `data` to the awaiting
caller; `success`, `error`, `hint`, and `timestamp` are surfaced via
`wrap_ipc_result`'s F2 two-tier envelope flattening.

`data`, `error`, and `hint` are all optional. The hint sibling carries
closest-match / recovery hints (set by frontend handlers like
`useControlEvents` for typo recovery on element-not-found and
action-not-allowed) and stays a sibling of `error` — the success/error
envelope shape is unchanged.

data.description:
Inner data payload on success. Frontend handlers set this to whatever
shape the operation returns (e.g. an `elements` array for
`get_elements`, a discovery report for `discover`, etc.).

error.description:
Error message on failure. Mirrors the inner-failure path of
`wrap_ipc_result`'s F2 two-tier envelope flattening.

hint.description:
Optional closest-match / recovery hint payload. Sibling field to
`error` (does NOT replace the success/error envelope shape). Used by
`useControlEvents` for element-not-found / action-not-allowed typo
recovery and by Rust `page.rs` for eval-rejected workaround guidance.

timestamp.description:
Frontend-side timestamp (ms since Unix epoch) of when the response was
produced.

type.description:
Echoes the request's [`UiBridgeRequestEnvelope::request_type`].
Serializes as `type` to match the wire.

UiBridgeResponseEnvelope.title: UiBridgeResponseEnvelope

CanonicalStep.description:
Discriminated union over the four canonical step variants.

Wire format is a flat object with a `"type"` discriminator — serde's
internal tagging merges the inner struct's fields (including the flattened
[`BaseStepFields`]) up into the top-level object. Example:

```text
{"type":"command","id":"s1","name":"build","phase":"setup","mode":"shell","command":"cargo build"}
```

Consumers that want a strict 4-variant typed view should use
[`CanonicalStep`]. Consumers that need to tolerate runner-specific step
types (e.g. `gate`, `screenshot`, `playwright`, `state`, `action`,
`log_watch`, and others dispatched by the runner but absent from the
wire-contract surface) should use [`UnifiedStep`], which preserves
unknown payloads verbatim as `serde_json::Value`.

Variant sizes are similar (~200–672 bytes each); the asymmetry reflects
real differences in step-field cardinality and doesn't warrant boxing.

Kinds of deterministic check surfaced by `CommandStep` when
`mode = "check"` or `mode = "check_group"`.

Shell commands, checks, check groups, and tests.

A single variant covers all command-like steps; the specific sub-kind is
carried by [`CommandMode`] and the matching `*_id` / `*_type` fields.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

failOnWarning.description: Fail the step on warnings in addition to errors.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

phase.description: Phase in which the step appears.

UnifiedStep.description:
A workflow step, preferring typed canonical variants and falling back to an
opaque [`serde_json::Value`] for runner-specific types not yet part of the
wire contract.

Serialization is transparent: a [`CanonicalStep`] serializes with its flat
`"type"`-tagged shape; [`UnifiedStep::Other`] serializes the wrapped value
as-is. Deserialization tries the canonical shape first; any payload that
does not match (unknown `"type"`, missing fields, or missing discriminator)
is preserved as [`UnifiedStep::Other`].

This catch-all is what makes the type *robust* on the wire: a runner can
emit a `{"type":"gate", ...}` step and a consumer using [`UnifiedStep`]
will round-trip it losslessly even though `gate` is not in the canonical
set.

## Layout note (`#[allow(large_enum_variant)]`)

`Canonical` carries a [`CanonicalStep`] (~672 bytes) while `Other` carries
a [`serde_json::Value`] (~32 bytes). The size asymmetry is intentional and
the enum is not held in bulk by any hot path today — `UnifiedWorkflow.*_steps`
remains `Vec<serde_json::Value>`. Boxing `Canonical` would save stack space
in hypothetical dense `Vec<UnifiedStep>` consumers at the cost of an extra
heap allocation per step everywhere else and noisier pattern-matching.

ModelOverrideConfig.description:
Per-phase model override configuration.

Each phase can independently specify a provider and/or model, along with
optional temperature, max_tokens, fallback config, and conditional routing
rules.

fallbackModel.description: Fallback model if the primary fails with a retryable error.

fallbackProvider.description: Fallback provider if the primary fails with a retryable error.

maxTokens.description: Max output tokens override for this phase.

routingRules.description:
Conditional routing rules evaluated at runtime. First matching rule
wins; unmatched falls back to this config's static fields.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

RoutingRule.description:
A conditional routing rule that selects model/provider based on runtime
context.

Rules are evaluated in order; the first matching rule wins. Condition
syntax: `"<variable> <op> <value>"` where:
- Variables: `verification_failures`, `iteration`, `stage_index`
- Operators: `>=`, `>`, `<=`, `<`, `==`, `!=`

maxTokens.description: Max tokens override when this rule matches.

model.description: Model to use when this rule matches.

provider.description: Provider to use when this rule matches.

temperature.description: Temperature override when this rule matches.

StageCondition.description:
Condition for conditional stage execution.

When attached to a [`WorkflowStage`], the stage is skipped if the condition
evaluates to "should skip". All condition fields are optional and combine
with AND semantics — all specified conditions must be met for the stage to
run.

ifPrevious.description:
Run this stage only if the previous stage had this outcome.

- `"passed"`: run only if previous stage verification passed
- `"failed"`: run only if previous stage verification failed
- `"any"`: always run regardless of previous outcome (default behavior)

minFailures.description:
Skip this stage if the total number of failed stages so far is below
this threshold. Useful for "recovery" stages that only run when
multiple prior stages have failed.

minIteration.description:
Run this stage only after this many loop iterations have occurred
(across all stages). Useful for "escalation" stages that only kick in
after initial attempts have failed.

StageInput.description: An input required by a stage, referencing a prior stage's output.

fromStage.description:
Which stage provides this input (stage id). If omitted, searches all
prior stages.

key.description:
The key to bind this input to (matches a [`StageOutput::key`] from a
prior stage).

required.description:
Whether this input is required (default: `true`). Missing required
inputs are Critical findings.

StageOutput.description: An output declared by a stage, available to subsequent stages.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `action-plan`
module into this crate; the TS side re-imports the typed `ActionPlan`
after regeneration.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

phase.description: Phase in which the step appears.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

UiBridgeStepPhase.description:
Phases in which a [`UiBridgeStep`] may appear.

UI-bridge interactions only run in deterministic phases — never inside
the agentic loop (where the AI drives steps directly via prompts).

anyOf.description: One of the four canonical step variants (command / prompt / ui_bridge / workflow).
Any other step-shaped JSON value, preserved verbatim.

UnifiedStep.description:
A workflow step, preferring typed canonical variants and falling back to an
opaque [`serde_json::Value`] for runner-specific types not yet part of the
wire contract.

Serialization is transparent: a [`CanonicalStep`] serializes with its flat
`"type"`-tagged shape; [`UnifiedStep::Other`] serializes the wrapped value
as-is. Deserialization tries the canonical shape first; any payload that
does not match (unknown `"type"`, missing fields, or missing discriminator)
is preserved as [`UnifiedStep::Other`].

This catch-all is what makes the type *robust* on the wire: a runner can
emit a `{"type":"gate", ...}` step and a consumer using [`UnifiedStep`]
will round-trip it losslessly even though `gate` is not in the canonical
set.

## Layout note (`#[allow(large_enum_variant)]`)

`Canonical` carries a [`CanonicalStep`] (~672 bytes) while `Other` carries
a [`serde_json::Value`] (~32 bytes). The size asymmetry is intentional and
the enum is not held in bulk by any hot path today — `UnifiedWorkflow.*_steps`
remains `Vec<serde_json::Value>`. Boxing `Canonical` would save stack space
in hypothetical dense `Vec<UnifiedStep>` consumers at the cost of an extra
heap allocation per step everywhere else and noisier pattern-matching.

VerificationCategoryKind.description:
Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].

WorkflowArchitecture.description:
The workflow execution architecture to use.

This is a first-class workflow architecture option, allowing direct
comparison between traditional deterministic verification and agentic
verification approaches. Mirrors the runner-side enum in
`crate::agentic_verification`.

oneOf.description:
Traditional: Setup → \[Deterministic Verification ↔ Agentic Fix\]* →
Completion. Pre-defined verification steps run deterministically; the
agentic phase fixes failures.

Agentic Verification: \[Verification Agent → Worker Agent\]* loop.
No pre-defined verification steps — a verification agent reasons about
success.
Multi-Agent Pipeline: specialized agents in a DAG-structured pipeline.

WorkflowStage.description:
A workflow stage — a self-contained unit of execution with its own
setup / verification / agentic / completion steps and verification-agentic
loop.

Multi-stage workflows execute stages sequentially. Each stage gets its own
verification-agentic loop, and later stages see full output from all prior
stages. Step arrays are opaque `serde_json::Value` payloads pending the
Wave 4 typed-step migration.

agenticSteps.description: Agentic phase steps for this stage.

approvalGate.description: Whether to pause for human approval after each agentic phase.

completionPromptsFirst.description:
When true, run completion prompt steps BEFORE automation steps.
Default (`false`) runs automation first, then prompts.

completionSteps.description: Completion phase steps for this stage.

condition.description:
Optional condition for stage execution. When set, the stage is
evaluated against this condition before running. If the condition is
not met, the stage is skipped.

description.description: Description of what this stage does.

inputs.description: Inputs required from prior stages.

maxIterations.description:
Maximum iterations for this stage's verification-agentic loop.

`None` (omitted in JSON) means no iteration cap — the loop terminates
on success, explicit stop, or fix-attempt exhaustion.

modelOverrides.description: Per-phase model overrides for this stage.

outputs.description: Declared outputs that this stage produces for downstream stages.

provider.description: AI provider override for this stage.

retryPolicy.description: Retry policy for this stage (overrides per-step defaults).

setupSteps.description: Setup phase steps for this stage (polymorphic; see module docs).

timeoutSeconds.description: Optional inactivity timeout in seconds for this stage's AI sessions.

verificationSteps.description: Verification phase steps for this stage.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

phase.description: Phase in which the step appears.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

workflowId.description: ID of the saved workflow to run.

workflowName.description: Display name of the saved workflow (denormalized for UI).

WorkflowStepPhase.description: Phases in which a [`WorkflowStep`] may appear.

UnifiedWorkflow.description:
A unified workflow with steps organized by phase.

The "frame" carries all non-step metadata — iteration caps, provider/model
selection, log-source routing, health checks, stage list, generator
outputs (dependency graph, cost annotations, quality report, acceptance
criteria), and timestamps. Step arrays remain opaque until the Wave 4
typed-step migration lands.

acceptanceCriteria.description:
Acceptance criteria from the specification agent (opaque JSON blob).

Used by the canvas panel manager to show a live requirements tracker.

aiReviewed.description:
Whether the AI semantic review actually ran successfully during
generation.

When `false`, the workflow passed through the pipeline without AI
verification (e.g., all verification iterations failed at
infrastructure level).

approvalGate.description: Whether to pause for human approval after each agentic phase.

autoCommitSubagents.description:
Auto-commit files touched by sub-agents on successful terminal state.

Phase C of the Commit Progress feature. Commits the file-set recorded
in `session_touched_files` to the workflow's worktree branch when the
sub-agent reaches a successful terminal state, so the parent workflow
sees an atomic delta instead of a half-edited working tree.

- `Some(true)` — commit after success regardless of worktree state.
- `Some(false)` — never commit, even with worktree.
- `None` (default) — commit if and only if the workflow uses a worktree.

Failure to commit never fails the workflow — best-effort durability.

autoIncludeContexts.description:
Whether to auto-include contexts based on task mentions (default:
`true`).

completionPromptsFirst.description:
When `true`, run completion prompt steps BEFORE automation steps.

Used by meta-workflows so the AI hardener runs before
`save_workflow_artifact`. Default (`false`) runs automation first,
then prompts.

completionSteps.description:
Completion phase steps (polymorphic JSON array) — runs once after the
verification loop exits.

constraintOverrides.description:
Per-constraint overrides: map of `constraint_id` to enabled (`true`) /
disabled (`false`).

Applied to the constraint engine at execution time, after loading
builtins and config.

costAnnotations.description: Cost annotations computed during generation (opaque JSON blob).

dependencyGraph.description: Dependency graph computed during generation (opaque JSON blob).

description.description: Description of what this workflow does.

enableSweep.description:
Whether to run a completion sweep after verification passes.

The sweep reviews all completed work for gaps before proceeding to
completion.

enforceTokenBudget.description:
When `true`, the pipeline will stop execution if accumulated token
usage exceeds the token budget. Disabled by default — only logs
warnings.

flowControlJson.description:
Flow control configuration as a JSON string (e.g., concurrency limits,
queue behavior).

generatedByTaskRunId.description: Task run ID that generated this workflow (for meta-workflow tracking).

healthCheckEnabled.description:
Whether to automatically include health check steps before
verification.

When enabled and `health_check_urls` is non-empty, health check steps
are prepended to verification steps to verify configured servers are
running.

healthCheckUrls.description:
URLs to health check before verification (user-configurable).

Each entry specifies a URL to check, expected status, and timeout.
If empty, no health checks are performed even if `health_check_enabled`
is true.

htnEnabled.description:
Whether HTN (Hierarchical Task Network) planning is enabled for this
workflow.

When `true`, the loop attempts structured plan-based fixes before
falling back to AI agentic sessions.

htnStateMachinePath.description:
Path to a serialized state machine JSON file for HTN planning.

When `None` and HTN is enabled, defaults to the bundled
`data/runner_state_machine.json`.

htnUiBridgeUrl.description:
UI Bridge URL for HTN planning (e.g., `"http://localhost:1420"`).

When set, the HTN planner connects to UI Bridge for querying element
state. If `None`, HTN runs in plan-only mode without GUI execution.

isFavorite.description: Whether this workflow is marked as a favorite for quick access.

logSourceSelection.description:
Log source selection for this workflow.

- `"default"`: use the global default profile (from Settings → Log Sources)
- `"ai"`: let AI automatically select relevant sources based on context
- `"all"`: use all enabled log sources
- `{ "profile_id": "..." }`: use a specific profile

logWatchEnabled.description:
Whether to automatically include a `log_watch` step before verification.

When enabled (default), a `log_watch` step is prepended to
verification steps to detect runtime errors in backend/frontend logs.

maxCiAutoResumes.description:
Maximum number of CI-triggered auto-resumes before requiring human
intervention. Used by the PR watcher integration. `0` = disabled.
Default: `10`.

maxFixAttempts.description:
Maximum consecutive non-improving fix attempts before escalating.

When the verification check count does not improve across this many
iterations, the loop exits with `fix_attempts_exhausted`. `0` =
disabled. Default: `3`.

maxIterations.description:
Maximum iterations for the agentic phase.

`None` means no iteration cap — the loop terminates on success,
explicit stop, or fix-attempt exhaustion.

modified_at.description:
ISO 8601 timestamp of last modification (serialized as `"modified_at"`
to match the frontend).

multiAgentMode.description:
Enable multi-agent fixer mode for the agentic phase.

When `true`, verification failures are triaged and fixed by
specialized agents (quick-fix for lint/compilation, feature-fix for
missing functionality). Default: `true`.

phaseTimeoutsJson.description: Per-phase timeout configuration as a JSON string.

preflightCheckEnabled.description:
Whether to automatically include a pre-flight environment check at the
start of setup.

When enabled (default), a shell command step runs to verify disk
space, Node.js/npm, Python/Poetry, Rust/Cargo, and Git availability.
Uses the global setting from Settings if not explicitly set per
workflow.

promptTemplate.description:
Custom developer prompt template for this workflow.

When set, this template is used instead of the global default when
running the workflow. Supports variables: `{{SESSION_ID}}`,
`{{ITERATION}}`, `{{MAX_ITERATIONS}}`, `{{GOAL}}`,
`{{EXECUTION_STEPS}}`, `{{WORKSPACE_ESCAPED}}`.

qualityReport.description: Quality report from the revision phase (opaque JSON blob).

reflectionMode.description:
Whether to enable reflection mode during agentic iterations.

When `true`, the AI investigates root causes before fixing failures.
Default: `true` for user-created workflows.

rollbackPolicy.description:
Policy for automatic git rollback when the workflow fails.

Values: `"none"` (default), `"last_good"`, `"clean"`.

securityProfile.description:
Per-workflow security profile override.

When set, overrides the default security profile from settings for
this workflow. Values: `"permissive"`, `"standard"`, `"strict"`,
or `"custom"`. If `None`, uses the default from Settings > Security.

setupSteps.description: Setup phase steps (polymorphic JSON array; see module docs).

skipAiSummary.description:
Skip AI summary generation at the end (default: `false`, meaning the
AI summary is generated).

stages.description:
Optional stages for multi-stage workflows.

When non-empty, the workflow executes stages sequentially instead of
using top-level steps. Each stage has its own
setup / verification / agentic / completion steps and loop.

stopOnFailure.description:
Whether to stop execution if a stage fails verification.

Default: `false` (autonomous mode — continue to the next stage even
if the previous failed).

strictCwd.description:
Restrict working directory resolution to the workspace boundary.

When `true`, steps cannot resolve paths outside the workspace root.
Default: `false` (permissive, current behavior).

targetedErrorIds.description:
Error IDs targeted by this workflow (for auto-resolution on success).

When the workflow completes successfully, these errors will be marked
as resolved. Used by error-fix workflows generated from the Error
Monitor.

timeoutSeconds.description:
Optional inactivity timeout in seconds for AI sessions.

- `None` (default): no timeout, runs until completion or manual stop.
- `Some(N)`: kill AI session after `N` seconds of no output.

Takes precedence over the global AI settings timeout.

toolTags.description:
Tags for per-execution tool whitelisting.

When non-empty, only skills matching at least one tag are included in
the AI prompt context, reducing prompt bloat.

useWorktree.description:
Run the workflow in an isolated git worktree.

When `true`, a new branch and worktree are created before execution.
Changes stay on the worktree branch and can be merged back after
review. Default: `false`.

workflowArchitecture.description:
Workflow execution architecture override.

When set, forces the workflow to use a specific execution architecture
instead of the default Traditional loop. When `None`, the system
infers the best architecture based on workflow complexity.

UnifiedWorkflow.title: UnifiedWorkflow

HttpConfig.description:
HTTP-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `headers` typically carries an `Authorization` token.
See module-level docs.

headers.description:
HTTP headers to include on every request. **Secret surface** —
typically includes `Authorization: Bearer …`.

url.description:
Server URL (e.g. `"http://localhost:8080/mcp"`). The runner appends
`/tools/list` and `/tools/call` to this base.

McpTransport.description:
Transport type for an MCP server connection.

Serialized as lowercase to match the existing DB column values
(`"stdio"` / `"http"`). Defaults to `Stdio` to preserve the pre-extraction
default from the runner.

oneOf.description:
Stdio subprocess transport — launches the configured command and
speaks newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout.
HTTP transport — POSTs JSON-RPC 2.0 to the configured URL.

StdioConfig.description:
Stdio-transport configuration.

**Secret surface**: `command`, `args`, and `env` must be treated as
execution-critical. See module-level docs.

cwd.description: Working directory (absolute path). `None` inherits the runner's cwd.

env.description:
Extra environment variables for the subprocess. **Secret surface** —
frequently holds API tokens.

UpdateMcpServerInput.description:
Request body for updating an MCP server configuration. Every field is
optional — fields left as `None` are preserved from the existing row.

**Secret surface**: as with [`McpServerConfig`], the nested transport
configs carry secrets.
UpdateMcpServerInput.title: UpdateMcpServerInput

UpdateOlConfigRequest.description:
Request payload for partially updating an existing saved preset.

All fields are optional — only those set are applied.

configJson.description: Replace the stored config JSON blob.

UpdateOlConfigRequest.title: UpdateOlConfigRequest

CatchUpPolicy.description:
Catch-up policy applied when the runner restarts after missing one or
more scheduled slots.

oneOf.description: Run the missed slot once for each occurrence that was skipped.

Skip all missed slots; emit `MissedRunnerDown` history rows for
audit but do not execute.
Collapse multiple missed slots into a single catch-up run.

rearmDelayMinutes.description:
Minutes to wait after an execution completes before re-evaluating
conditions for another run.

IdleCondition.description:
Condition that requires the runner to be idle (not executing workflows or
AI tasks) before the task may run.

McpConnectionRef.description:
Lightweight reference to an MCP connection for [`ScheduledTaskType::RemoteAgent`].

`name` is the MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.
`url` is an optional override; when omitted the runner resolves the URL
from its existing MCP config at dispatch time.

name.description: MCP server name as registered in the runner's MCP config.

url.description:
Optional URL override; falls back to the runner's MCP config when
`None`.

RepositoryInactiveCondition.description:
Condition that requires repositories to have no file modifications for a
period. ALL configured repositories must be inactive for the overall
condition to be met.

repositories.description: List of repositories to watch. ALL must be inactive simultaneously.

RepositoryWatch.description: A single repository to monitor for inactivity.

inactiveMinutes.description: Minutes of inactivity required before the watch is considered met.

path.description: Path to the repository directory.

ScheduleConditions.description: Conditions that must ALL be met before task execution.

requireIdle.description: Require the runner to be idle.

requireRepoInactive.description: Require repository file inactivity across one or more paths.

timeoutMinutes.description:
Maximum time to wait for conditions (minutes). `None` = wait
indefinitely.

ScheduleExpression.description:
How a task should be scheduled.

Serialized with the external tag `type` and payload under `value` so that
`Once("...")`, `Cron("...")`, and `Interval(60)` round-trip as
`{ "type": "Once", "value": "..." }` etc. The `Condition` variant wraps a
[`ConditionScheduleConfig`] rather than a scalar, but uses the same
`{ type, value }` envelope.

oneOf.description: Run once at a specific datetime (ISO 8601).

Cron expression (e.g., `"0 9 * * *"` for 9 AM daily). The runner
accepts both 5-field and 6/7-field cron forms and normalizes internally.
Interval in seconds between runs (for testing/debugging).

Condition-only schedule: no time trigger, runs whenever the attached
[`ScheduleConditions`] on the task are met.

ScheduledTaskType.description:
Type of task to schedule.

Internally tagged by `task_type`: the variant fields are inlined alongside
the discriminator rather than nested under a `value` key.

oneOf.description:
Run a workflow — either a legacy workflow-by-name from a loaded config
or a unified workflow by ID.

config_path.description: Optional path to a workflow config file.

workflow_id.description:
If set, run a unified workflow by ID instead of a legacy workflow
by name.

workflow_name.description: Display name (also used to look up legacy workflows).

oneOf.description: Run a prompt from the Prompt Library.

max_sessions.description:
Optional override for `max_sessions`. `None` uses the prompt's own
setting.

prompt_id.description: ID of the prompt to run.

oneOf.description: Trigger auto-fix: check findings and fix any auto-fixable items.

check_findings.description: Whether to check the findings queue before running.

force_run.description: Force a run even if no findings are present.

oneOf.description:
Execute a watcher (screenpipe-inspired reactive AI agent). Queries the
activity timeline, reasons with AI, and triggers an action.

watcher_id.description: ID of the watcher definition in PostgreSQL.

oneOf.description:
Continuous background capture (screenpipe-style). Periodically
captures screen state and stores it in the activity timeline.

capture_on_focus_change.description: Whether to also trigger a capture on window focus change.

oneOf.description:
Run an ad-hoc Claude agent prompt without pre-registering it in the
Prompt Library. Reuses the runner's existing `POST /prompts/run`
ad-hoc surface — no separate Claude CLI plumbing required.

allowed_tools.description:
Allowed tool names (e.g. `["Bash", "Read", "Write", "Edit"]`).
Empty = use the runner's default tool allow-list.

max_turns.description:
Hard cap on Claude turns per run (safety bound). `None` =
runner's default cap (typically 50).

mcp_connections.description:
Optional MCP connection references resolved at dispatch time
against the runner's existing MCP config. Empty = inherit
whatever the runner currently has configured.

prompt.description: The prompt content to send to the Claude session.

timeout_seconds.description:
Wall-clock timeout in seconds. `None` = runner default
(typically 600s = 10 min).

working_directory.description:
Working directory for the spawned session. `None` means the
runner's project root. Stored as a string (not `PathBuf`) to keep
this crate JSON-Schema-friendly.

UpdateScheduledTaskRequest.description:
Request body for updating an existing scheduled task. All fields are
optional; only those supplied are applied.

conditions.description: Replace the conditions block (pass `null` to clear).

successCriteria.description: Update the success criteria (pass `null` to clear).

UpdateScheduledTaskRequest.title: UpdateScheduledTaskRequest

description.description: Why this constraint exists (shown to the AI on violation).

enabled.description: Whether this constraint is enabled. Default: true.

ConstraintCheck.description:
What to check and how.

Internally tagged by the `type` field. Variants correspond to the four
check kinds implemented by the runner's constraint engine.

oneOf.description:
Grep modified files for a regex pattern.
Violation if the pattern IS found (use for secrets, debug statements, etc.)

file_glob.description:
Optional glob to limit which modified files are checked.
Default: all modified files.

oneOf.description:
Grep modified files for a regex pattern.
Violation if the pattern is NOT found (use for required headers, licenses, etc.)

file_glob.description: Optional glob to limit which modified files are checked.

pattern.description: Regex pattern that must be present.

oneOf.description:
Check that all modified files are within allowed paths.
Violation if any modified file is outside the allowed directories.

allowed_paths.description:
Allowed directory prefixes (relative to project root).
e.g., `["src/", "tests/", "config/"]`.

oneOf.description:
Run a shell command. Violation if exit code is non-zero.
Useful for quick compilation checks, linting, etc.

cwd.description: Working directory (relative to project root). Default: project root.

ConstraintSeverity.description:
Severity of a constraint violation.

- `Block`: Reject the fix, inject violation context, re-run agentic phase
  without consuming an iteration. After `max_retries`, consume the iteration.
- `Warn`: Apply the fix, but inject violation context for the next iteration.
- `Log`: Record only, don't affect execution.

oneOf.description:
Reject the fix and re-run the agentic phase with injected violation
context. After `max_retries`, the iteration is consumed.
Apply the fix, but inject violation context for the next iteration.
Record the violation only; do not affect execution.

constraints.description: Successfully parsed constraints (may be partial if some were skipped).

errors.description: Parse errors or non-fatal warnings (e.g., constraints skipped due to bad regex).

valid.description: Whether the config is fully valid (parseable with no errors or warnings).

BoundingBox.description:
Bounding box coordinates for a GUI element.

Mirrors `rag.models.BoundingBox`.

ElementType.description:
Type of GUI element detected and stored for RAG retrieval.

Mirrors `rag.models.ElementType`.

GUIElementChunk.description:
Complete representation of a GUI element chunk for RAG retrieval.

Contains all information needed for storing, searching, and retrieving GUI
elements from a vector database. Mirrors `rag.models.GUIElementChunk`.

is_defining_element.description: Is this element required for state identification?

is_fixed_position.description: Does the element stay in the same position?

is_optional_element.description: Is this element optional in the state?

is_shared.description: Is element shared across multiple states?

VectorSearchResult.description:
Result from a vector database search query.

Contains the matched element and relevance scores.
Mirrors `rag.models.SearchResult`.
VectorSearchResult.title: VectorSearchResult

VerificationAgentContext.description:
Context passed to the verification agent (intentionally limited — does not
include work history, to avoid biasing AI evaluation).

evaluationPrompt.description: The evaluation prompt from the criterion.

VerificationCategoryKind.description:
Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].
VerificationCategoryKind.title: VerificationCategoryKind

oneOf.description: Log pattern must match (or not match).

CheckIssueDetail.description: A specific issue detail from an individual verification check.

code.description: Error code or lint rule, if applicable.

file.description: File path where the issue was detected.

fixable.description: Whether the check can auto-fix this issue.

severity.description: Severity label (free-form string from the upstream check).

GateEvaluationResult.description: Result of evaluating a named gate across a set of steps.

missingStepIds.description: IDs of required steps that were missing.

passedStepIds.description: IDs of required steps that passed.

requiredStepIds.description: IDs of steps the gate required.

errorMessage.description: Error message, if the check itself failed to run.

filesChecked.description: Number of files the check inspected.

issuesFixed.description: Number of issues auto-fixed by this check.

Execution config captured for a verification step.

The TypeScript type includes an index signature `[key: string]: unknown`,

VgaAction.description:
A single action inside a [`VgaAutomateStep`] action sequence.

Wire shape is internally tagged with `"kind"`:
- `{ "kind": "click", "elementId": "<uuid>", "timeoutMs": 10000 }`
- `{ "kind": "type", "text": "hello", "elementId": "<uuid>", "timeoutMs": 10000 }`
  (`elementId` omitted/null = type into the currently focused element)
- `{ "kind": "wait_for", "elementId": "<uuid>", "timeoutMs": 30000 }`

oneOf.description:
Click the element identified by `element_id` (UUID referencing an
element inside the VGA state machine).

elementId.description: UUID of the target element in the VGA state machine's element set.

timeoutMs.description:
Per-action timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `10000` on the
consumer side.

oneOf.description:
Type `text` into `element_id`, or into the currently focused element
when `element_id` is absent/`null`.

elementId.description:
UUID of the target element. Absent / `null` means "type into
whichever element currently has focus".

timeoutMs.description:
Per-action timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `10000` on the
consumer side.

oneOf.description: Wait for `element_id` to become visible / actionable.

timeoutMs.description:
Per-action timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `30000` on the
consumer side.

VgaAction.title: VgaAction

VgaActionKind.description:
Kinds of action supported by a [`VgaAutomateStep`] `action_sequence` entry.

Mirrors the TS `VgaActionKind` literal union. Each variant carries its own
set of fields (see [`VgaAction`]).
VgaActionKind.title: VgaActionKind

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].

VgaAutomateStep.description:
Run a Visual GUI Automation (VGA) action sequence against a target process.

Wire tag: `"vga_automate"`.

Delegates to the Python `qontinui.vga.worker` via the `python-bridge` IPC.
The worker loads the referenced state machine (`state_machine_id` →
`runner.vga_state_machines`), focuses `target_process`, and executes each
entry in `action_sequence` by grounding the element prompt against a fresh
screenshot and dispatching the HAL click/type/wait primitive.

Canonical field name for the VGA state machine reference is
`vgaStateMachineId` (camelCase) in emitted/accepted JSON. Two
legacy aliases exist for back-compat: `vga_state_machine_id`
(snake_case) and the bare `stateMachineId`. New code must emit
`vgaStateMachineId`. Aliases may be removed after 2026-Q3.

actionSequence.description: Ordered sequence of VGA actions to execute.

async.description:
Reserved for future async mode. Currently must be `false` (or omitted)
— the handler rejects `true` until async mode is implemented.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

stateMachineId.description:
UUID referencing `runner.vga_state_machines.id` — the persisted state
machine that defines the elements this step may click / type into /
wait for.

targetProcess.description:
Target process / window — e.g. `"notepad++.exe"`. Used by the HAL to
focus the correct top-level window before each action.

timeoutMs.description:
Overall step timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to `300000` (5 minutes)
on the consumer side; bounds `[1000, 3600000]`.

VgaAutomateStep.title: VgaAutomateStep

Monitor.description:
Standardized monitor information — a physical display with its position
in the virtual desktop and metadata for UI display.

height.description: Monitor height in pixels.

isPrimary.description: Whether this is the primary/main monitor.

position.description: Spatial position based on X coordinate (for UI display).

width.description: Monitor width in pixels.

x.description: X position in absolute screen coordinates (can be negative).

y.description: Y position in absolute screen coordinates (can be negative).

MonitorPosition.description:
Spatial position of a monitor in a left-to-right layout, derived from the
X coordinate. Used for human-friendly display; use `index` for
programmatic operations.

VirtualDesktop.description:
The combined coordinate space of all monitors.

- Origin: `(min_x, min_y)` across all monitors
- Size: bounding box containing all monitors
- Monitors may have gaps between them or different resolutions/DPI

monitors.description: List of all monitors in the virtual desktop.

VirtualDesktop.title: VirtualDesktop

VisualComparisonResult.description: Result of visual comparison of a screenshot against a baseline.

diffImageUrl.description: URL to a diff image, if generated.

threshold.description: Threshold used for the pass/fail decision.

VisualComparisonResult.title: VisualComparisonResult

WaitOptions.description:
Wait-condition options attached to an element action request.

Before executing the action the bridge can optionally wait until the
target element reaches a specified visibility/enabled/focused state.

enabled.description: Wait until the element is enabled (or disabled if `false`).

focused.description: Wait until the element has focus (or loses focus if `false`).

interval.description: Polling interval in milliseconds for condition checks.

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait in milliseconds before timing out.

visible.description: Wait until the element is visible (or hidden if `false`).

WaitOptions.title: WaitOptions

Confidence.description: Confidence level for findings and verification results.

oneOf.description: High confidence — deterministic evidence or clear observation.
Medium confidence — reasonable but not certain.
Low confidence — speculative or weakly supported.

Finding.description: A finding recorded by a worker agent.

confidence.description: Confidence level.

evidence.description: Supporting evidence (file paths, log excerpts, etc.).

findingType.description:
Type of finding.

Valid types: `"bug"`, `"root_cause"`, `"observation"`, `"hypothesis"`,
`"solution"`, `"environment"`. Note: `"environment"` findings (PATH
issues, disk space, tools not installed) require user intervention and
should NOT trigger automatic retries.

WorkerCoordinationMessage.description:
Coordination message between workers.

Externally tagged as `{ "type": ..., "data": { ... } }` to match the
pre-extraction wire.

worker_id.description: ID of the worker that modified the files.

oneOf.description: Worker found an issue that may affect other workers.

worker_id.description: ID of the worker that made the finding.

oneOf.description: Worker is blocked waiting for another worker.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the block.

waiting_for.description: ID of the worker being waited on.

domain.description: Optional domain scope for the verification.

worker_id.description: ID of the worker that is ready.

oneOf.description: Coordination signal to synchronize multiple workers.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the sync point.

worker_ids.description: IDs of the workers that should synchronize.

WorkerCoordinationMessage.title: WorkerCoordinationMessage

WorkflowArchitecture.description:
The workflow execution architecture to use.

This is a first-class workflow architecture option, allowing direct
comparison between traditional deterministic verification and agentic
verification approaches. Mirrors the runner-side enum in
`crate::agentic_verification`.

oneOf.description:
Traditional: Setup → \[Deterministic Verification ↔ Agentic Fix\]* →
Completion. Pre-defined verification steps run deterministically; the
agentic phase fixes failures.

Agentic Verification: \[Verification Agent → Worker Agent\]* loop.
No pre-defined verification steps — a verification agent reasons about
success.
Multi-Agent Pipeline: specialized agents in a DAG-structured pipeline.

WorkflowArchitecture.title: WorkflowArchitecture

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

CanonicalStep.description:
Discriminated union over the four canonical step variants.

Wire format is a flat object with a `"type"` discriminator — serde's
internal tagging merges the inner struct's fields (including the flattened
[`BaseStepFields`]) up into the top-level object. Example:

```text
{"type":"command","id":"s1","name":"build","phase":"setup","mode":"shell","command":"cargo build"}
```

Consumers that want a strict 4-variant typed view should use
[`CanonicalStep`]. Consumers that need to tolerate runner-specific step
types (e.g. `gate`, `screenshot`, `playwright`, `state`, `action`,
`log_watch`, and others dispatched by the runner but absent from the
wire-contract surface) should use [`UnifiedStep`], which preserves
unknown payloads verbatim as `serde_json::Value`.

Variant sizes are similar (~200–672 bytes each); the asymmetry reflects
real differences in step-field cardinality and doesn't warrant boxing.

CheckType.description:
Kinds of deterministic check surfaced by `CommandStep` when
`mode = "check"` or `mode = "check_group"`.

CommandStep.description:
Shell commands, checks, check groups, and tests.

A single variant covers all command-like steps; the specific sub-kind is
carried by [`CommandMode`] and the matching `*_id` / `*_type` fields.

autoFix.description: Whether to auto-fix during the check.

configPath.description: Path to the check's config file.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

failOnError.description: Whether non-zero exit status fails the step.

failOnWarning.description: Fail the step on warnings in addition to errors.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

ModelOverrideConfig.description:
Per-phase model override configuration.

Each phase can independently specify a provider and/or model, along with
optional temperature, max_tokens, fallback config, and conditional routing
rules.

fallbackModel.description: Fallback model if the primary fails with a retryable error.

fallbackProvider.description: Fallback provider if the primary fails with a retryable error.

maxTokens.description: Max output tokens override for this phase.

routingRules.description:
Conditional routing rules evaluated at runtime. First matching rule
wins; unmatched falls back to this config's static fields.

PlaywrightExecutionMode.description: Whether a Playwright test executes independently or as part of a chain.

criterionIds.description: Acceptance criterion IDs verified by this step.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

isSummaryStep.description: Marks this prompt as the summary step at the end of completion.

phase.description: Phase in which the step appears.

promptId.description: Saved prompt ID (when the body is a reference).

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`
dependency chain into this module; the TS side re-imports the typed
`SkillOrigin` after regeneration.

PromptStepPhase.description:
Phases in which a [`PromptStep`] may appear.

Prompt steps are the only variant that may appear in the agentic phase.

RetryPolicy.description: Retry policy for a step or stage.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

RetrySpec.description:
Per-step retry configuration carried by [`BaseStepFields::retry`].

Distinct from the workflow frame's `RetryPolicy` — that one also carries a
`backoff` flag, this per-step form is the older, simpler shape that step
DTOs inherited from the TS `BaseStep` interface.

count.description: Number of retry attempts (`0` = no retries).

RoutingRule.description:
A conditional routing rule that selects model/provider based on runtime
context.

Rules are evaluated in order; the first matching rule wins. Condition
syntax: `"<variable> <op> <value>"` where:
- Variables: `verification_failures`, `iteration`, `stage_index`
- Operators: `>=`, `>`, `<=`, `<`, `==`, `!=`

maxTokens.description: Max tokens override when this rule matches.

model.description: Model to use when this rule matches.

provider.description: Provider to use when this rule matches.

temperature.description: Temperature override when this rule matches.

StageCondition.description:
Condition for conditional stage execution.

When attached to a [`WorkflowStage`], the stage is skipped if the condition
evaluates to "should skip". All condition fields are optional and combine
with AND semantics — all specified conditions must be met for the stage to
run.

ifPrevious.description:
Run this stage only if the previous stage had this outcome.

- `"passed"`: run only if previous stage verification passed
- `"failed"`: run only if previous stage verification failed
- `"any"`: always run regardless of previous outcome (default behavior)

minFailures.description:
Skip this stage if the total number of failed stages so far is below
this threshold. Useful for "recovery" stages that only run when
multiple prior stages have failed.

minIteration.description:
Run this stage only after this many loop iterations have occurred
(across all stages). Useful for "escalation" stages that only kick in
after initial attempts have failed.

StageInput.description: An input required by a stage, referencing a prior stage's output.

fromStage.description:
Which stage provides this input (stage id). If omitted, searches all
prior stages.

key.description:
The key to bind this input to (matches a [`StageOutput::key`] from a
prior stage).

required.description:
Whether this input is required (default: `true`). Missing required
inputs are Critical findings.

StageOutput.description: An output declared by a stage, available to subsequent stages.

UiBridgeAssertType.description: Kinds of assertion supported by `assert` actions.

UiBridgeStep.description: UI Bridge SDK interaction — navigate, execute, assert, snapshot, compare.

actionPlan.description:
Structured action plan (for `action_plan`).

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `action-plan`
module into this crate; the TS side re-imports the typed `ActionPlan`
after regeneration.

dependsOn.description: IDs of other steps that must complete first.

failOnConsoleErrors.description: If `Some(true)`, a console-error signal from the UI fails this step.

inputs.description: Named input bindings evaluated at step entry.

phase.description: Phase in which the step appears.

required.description: Whether this step is required (default: `true` on consumer side).

skillOrigin.description:
Provenance of this step when generated from a skill template.

Typed as `serde_json::Value` here to avoid pulling the `skill`

UiBridgeStepPhase.description:
Phases in which a [`UiBridgeStep`] may appear.

UI-bridge interactions only run in deterministic phases — never inside
the agentic loop (where the AI drives steps directly via prompts).

anyOf.description: One of the four canonical step variants (command / prompt / ui_bridge / workflow).
Any other step-shaped JSON value, preserved verbatim.

UnifiedStep.description:
A workflow step, preferring typed canonical variants and falling back to an
opaque [`serde_json::Value`] for runner-specific types not yet part of the
wire contract.

Serialization is transparent: a [`CanonicalStep`] serializes with its flat
`"type"`-tagged shape; [`UnifiedStep::Other`] serializes the wrapped value
as-is. Deserialization tries the canonical shape first; any payload that
does not match (unknown `"type"`, missing fields, or missing discriminator)
is preserved as [`UnifiedStep::Other`].

This catch-all is what makes the type *robust* on the wire: a runner can
emit a `{"type":"gate", ...}` step and a consumer using [`UnifiedStep`]
will round-trip it losslessly even though `gate` is not in the canonical
set.

## Layout note (`#[allow(large_enum_variant)]`)

`Canonical` carries a [`CanonicalStep`] (~672 bytes) while `Other` carries
a [`serde_json::Value`] (~32 bytes). The size asymmetry is intentional and
the enum is not held in bulk by any hot path today — `UnifiedWorkflow.*_steps`
remains `Vec<serde_json::Value>`. Boxing `Canonical` would save stack space
in hypothetical dense `Vec<UnifiedStep>` consumers at the cost of an extra
heap allocation per step everywhere else and noisier pattern-matching.

Verification-depth category for a step.

Mirrors the TS `VerificationCategory` literal union. Kept local to this
module because it is only referenced from [`BaseStepFields`].

workflowName.description: Display name of the saved workflow (denormalized for UI).

WorkflowStepPhase.description: Phases in which a [`WorkflowStep`] may appear.

WorkflowStage.description:
A workflow stage — a self-contained unit of execution with its own
setup / verification / agentic / completion steps and verification-agentic
loop.

Multi-stage workflows execute stages sequentially. Each stage gets its own
verification-agentic loop, and later stages see full output from all prior
stages. Step arrays are opaque `serde_json::Value` payloads pending the
Wave 4 typed-step migration.

agenticSteps.description: Agentic phase steps for this stage.

approvalGate.description: Whether to pause for human approval after each agentic phase.

completionPromptsFirst.description:
When true, run completion prompt steps BEFORE automation steps.
Default (`false`) runs automation first, then prompts.

completionSteps.description: Completion phase steps for this stage.

condition.description:
Optional condition for stage execution. When set, the stage is
evaluated against this condition before running. If the condition is
not met, the stage is skipped.

description.description: Description of what this stage does.

inputs.description: Inputs required from prior stages.

maxIterations.description:
Maximum iterations for this stage's verification-agentic loop.

`None` (omitted in JSON) means no iteration cap — the loop terminates
on success, explicit stop, or fix-attempt exhaustion.

modelOverrides.description: Per-phase model overrides for this stage.

outputs.description: Declared outputs that this stage produces for downstream stages.

provider.description: AI provider override for this stage.

retryPolicy.description: Retry policy for this stage (overrides per-step defaults).

setupSteps.description: Setup phase steps for this stage (polymorphic; see module docs).

timeoutSeconds.description: Optional inactivity timeout in seconds for this stage's AI sessions.

verificationSteps.description: Verification phase steps for this stage.
